title: An Edge-Guided Structural Transformer Network for SAR Time Series Land Cover Mapping
authors: Kang Ni, Jiaxing Ren, Chunyang Yuan, Zhizhong Zheng, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Real-time and accurate interpretation of time-series SAR imagery is vital for land-cover monitoring. Nevertheless, the presence of speckle noise makes it extremely challenging to distill the essential information from redundant SAR time series. Current methods not only struggle to suppress temporal-feature redundancy but also tend to overlook the structural cues along land-cover boundaries that are crucial for boosting classification performance, leading to slower inference and sub-optimal accuracy. To address these challenges, we propose Edge-Guided Structural Transformer Network (EGSTNet), a lightweight and efficient land cover mapping network for time-series SAR imagery. The edge-guided convolution (EGConv) and axial separable large kernel attention (ASLKA), based on a lightweight design, utilize a re-parameterizable multi-branch convolutional module and a large-kernel attention mechanism to capture multi-order gradient features and model global spatiotemporal characteristics along both spatial and temporal dimensions in SAR time series. Notably, an implicit semantic graph style is incorporated into implicit semantic graph Transformer (ISGT) to facilitate efficient joint modeling of spatiotemporal and structural information of land covers. Experimental results on three benchmark time-series SAR land cover mapping datasets show that EGSTNet achieves mIoU scores of 35.87%, 48.16%, and 50.27% on the Slovenia, Brandenburg S1, and Brandenburg S2 datasets, respectively, with 17.10M trainable parameters and an inference speed of 105.53 FPS.

title: Open-Source Client Modules Supporting Time Transfer with Satellite- and Decrypted Network-Based Precise Point Positioning [Roadmap for Measurement and Applications]
authors: Runzhi Zhang, Rui Tu, Jihai Zhang, Xiaochun Lu
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) now provides global users with high-precision positioning, navigation, and timing services. Precise point positioning (PPP) technique has been developed and utilized for international atomic time link computations. However, PPP relies on high-precision satellite products to correct satellite-related errors in measurement observations output by a GNSS receiver. To support applications, precise satellite products were provided either via network transmission or through direct satellite broadcast in real-time. In recent years, network-based products, which are encrypted, internationally standardized, and transmitted in binary format, and satellite-based products, which are fragmented, customized, encoded, and transmitted in binary format, have made it more difficult for users to reliably and continuously recover them. At present, real-time open-source PPP software does not provide these two supports, which limits the application of real-time PPP, particularly for stable and continuous real-time PPP time transfer research and implementation. In this study, two supporting software packages have been developed and released as open source, designed to be embedded into mainstream open-source C/C++ PPP software. RTStreamHub is a support package for receiving and processing encrypted network-based products, designed to ensure reliable, real-time reception of securely transmitted satellite binary products. HASPPP is a dedicated software for Galileo satellite-based PPP, providing integrated support for recovering complex satellite-based products and implementing PPP, while also offering interfaces for seamless integration into mainstream C/C++ software for PPP time transfer applications. The real-time PPP time transfer precision of both network- and satellite-based products was analyzed using the PPP model embedded within the software HASPPP. Taking fiber-optic link results as a reference, the PPP time transfer precision is 125.9 ps based on network-based satellite products and 133.8 ps based on Galileo satellite-based products. The performance of these software packages has been validated, providing support modules for mainstream C/C++ PPP software.

title: Polarization-Insensitive Silicon-on-Insulator Arrayed Waveguide Grating Router with Bidirectional I/O
authors: Siyang Zhou, Jun Zou, Zikang Xu, Shuojian Zhang, Zhuping Fan, Jian-Jun He
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Polarization sensitivity of silicon-based wavelength division multiplexing (WDM) devices complicates the deployment in randomly polarized fiber communication systems. Here we present a polarization-insensitive arrayed waveguide grating router (AWGR) on conventional silicon-on-insulator (SOI) platforms using a 2D-grating based polarization diversity scheme and bidirectional transmission in a single symmetric AWGR. The device shows 0.32 dB polarization-dependent loss (PDL) and 3.29 dB average excess loss, with 1.2 nm channel spacing. It has a compact size of only 2.3×1.0 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. It also features bidirectional input/output ports, which supports simplified single fiber array coupling and greatly facilitates the wavelength routing in dense wavelength division multiplexing (DWDM) systems employing bidirectional transceivers with a reduced number of fibers.

title: Multistatic Radar Joint Range-Velocity Anti-Deceptive Jamming Methods Based on Information Geometry
authors: Siyi Yao, Yi Liao, Yanwen Su, Yusheng Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a joint range-velocity anti deceptive jamming method based on information geometry for multistatic radar scenarios and adapts to both measurement information and spoofing parameter approaches. The location information of physical targets can be obtained from the radar's angle and range measurements, while velocity can be derived from the radar's velocity and angle measurements, both of which contain correlated components. This paper maps normalized samples of location and velocity information obtained from multiple measurements onto a power-spectrum manifold to discriminate between physical targets and active false targets. Compared with existing methods, the proposed distance-velocity joint anti-deceptive jamming algorithm not only theoretically leverages information from both distance and velocity dimensions more effectively but also demonstrates its feasibility and effectiveness by simulation results.

title: SINdex: Semantic INconsistency Index for Hallucination Detection in LLMs
authors: Samir Abdaljalil, Parichit Sharma, Erchin Serpedin, Rachad Atat, Hasan Kurban
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed across diverse domains, yet they are prone to generating factually incorrect outputs-commonly known as “hallucinations.” Among existing mitigation strategies, uncertainty-based methods are particularly attractive due to their ease of implementation, independence from external data, and compatibility with standard LLMs. In this work, we introduce a novel and scalable uncertainty-based semantic clustering framework for automated hallucination detection. Our approach leverages sentence embeddings and hierarchical clustering alongside a newly proposed inconsistency measure, SINdex, to yield more homogeneous clusters and more accurate detection of hallucination phenomena across various LLMs. Evaluations on prominent open- and closed-book QA datasets demonstrate that our method achieves AUROC improvements of up to 9.3% over state-of-the-art techniques. Extensive ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each component in our framework.

title: Implicit Preference Learning for Adaptive Human-Robot Collaboration Using Task Graphs
authors: Liliana Antão, João Reis, Gil Gonçalves
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: As human-robot collaboration (HRC) becomes increasingly essential in industrial settings, ensuring flexible and adaptive interaction is critical for enhancing productivity and worker safety. This paper presents a framework designed to enable true HRC by using task graphs, real-time human motion prediction, and online preference learning to adapt robot actions based on operator preferences and behaviors. The framework was implemented in a car axle assembly task. The system dynamically adjusted its behavior using task graphs that represented the task as granular operations such as grabbing, placing, and dropping. Human movements were predicted and actions classified using an LSTM network to anticipate and learn operator preferences within the task graph. Additionally, voice control allowed operators to command the robot during critical actions such as dropping heavy components. Experimental results with six operators demonstrated that the system achieved high adaptability, with workflow interruptions reducing by 77.6% over 15 trials and task completion time improving by 27.5%. The ability to adapt in real-time to individual operator preferences represents a significant improvement over traditional fixed-sequence workflows.

title: Optimized Digital Filtering of EEG Signals for ERP Preservation and Noise Suppression
authors: Md. Mostafa Goni, Amir KhosruTalha, Yaseen Rahat, Razia Sultana Rimu, Rakayet Rafi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Accurate filtering of EEG signals is essential for reliable brain activity interpretation, particularly in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) and neurodiagnostic applications where noise can distort event-related potentials (ERPs). This study compares the performance of four widely used digital filters applied to real EEG data from a rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP) task (PhysioNet, 2048 Hz sampling rate): FIR bandpass filters with Hamming, Hanning, and Blackman windows, IIR Butterworth, IIR Chebyshev Type II, and FIR Parks-McClellan notch filters. Filter performance was assessed using SNR, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\triangle$</tex> SNR, MSE, RMSE, PSNR, and frequency-domain metrics including stopband attenuation, passband ripple, and group delay. Results show that the IIR Butterworth filter achieved the highest realistic SNR improvement (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\triangle$</tex> SNR = <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+12.89 ~\text{dB}$</tex>), while FIR bandpass filters offered moderate denoising (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\triangle \text{SNR}=+5.94 ~\text{dB}$</tex>) and preserved waveform fidelity due to linear phase characteristics. Among FIR windows, the Blackman design achieved the lowest MSE (0.7226) and highest PSNR (1.411 dB), indicating superior signal fidelity. The Chebyshev II filter, however, introduced artifacts, limiting its practical use. Overall, Butterworth filters are recommended for real-time EEG denoising, whereas Blackmanwindowed FIR filters are best suited for offline ERP analysis where phase preservation is critical.

title: A 3-bit Digitally-Reconfigurable Driving-Capability nW-Powered Three-Stage Fully-Synthesizable Inverter-Based OTA for Multi-Load/Applications
authors: Riccardo Della Sala, Marco Privitera, Giuseppe Scotti, Alfio Dario Grasso, Massimo Alioto
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents a fully synthesizable three-stage inverter-based operational transconductance amplifier (OTA) featuring a 3-bit digitally reconfigurable output stage for robust operation across a wide range of capacitive loads in ultra-low-voltage (ULV) and ultra-low-power (ULP) environments. Implemented entirely with standard-cell inverters and inverting-tristate (ITS) buffers, the proposed architecture operates from a 0.3-V supply while consuming less than 2.7 nW. The digital tuning mechanism enables near-constant gain-bandwidth product (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$GBW$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) and average slew rate (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${SR}_{av}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) across a 10-110 pF load range, making the OTA suitable for both on-chip and off-chip applications in battery-less and energy-harvested systems. Fabricated in 180-nm CMOS, the OTA achieves > 65-dB DC gain, 3.5-kHz <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$GBW$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, 0.16-V/ms <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${SR}_{av}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and 94% rail-to-rail output swing, while demonstrating strong resilience to PVT variations. Measurements confirm > 2.1X improvement in small-signal efficiency establishing a new state-of-the-art for < 0.5-V digital-based OTAs.

title: Coherent Radar Networks: Terminology, Synchronization, and Applications
authors: David Werbunat, Lukas Paulus, Julian Aguilar, Vinzenz Janoudi, Julian Kanz, Max Basler, Martin Vossiek, Christian Waldschmidt
publish: IEEE Journal of Microwaves
abstract: In coherent radar networks, multiple radar sensors not only operate cooperatively but also facilitate coherent evaluation of signals from other spacially distant radar units. This overcomes the limitations of single radars, enabling extended apertures and new multistatic sensing geometries. Applications are manifold, ranging from network-based direction of arrival (DoA) estimation to bi-/multistatic synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and joint communication and sensing (JCAS). In recent years, this has led to significant research interest and the design of a wide range of radar networks and synchronization concepts. This paper introduces a unified terminology and a general signal model applicable to radar networks and bistatic radars. Building on this model, this work provides a detailed review of the main sources of synchronization errors and their impact on radar signal processing. The paper further surveys and categorizes synchronization strategies proposed in the literature to address these errors. It thereby enables both a structured comparison of current radar networks and the design of novel network and synchronization concepts for emerging applications. To illustrate the potential of these approaches, three applications are presented in detail: a novel DoA estimation strategy for distributed radar networks, UAV-borne bistatic SAR, and JCAS.

title: Fabrication Defect Detection and Characterization of Semiconductor Devices from SEM Images Using Morphological Image Processing
authors: Sakhawat Hossen Rakib, A. S. M. Bakibillah, Tazin Hossain, Muhammad Towfiqur Rahman, Md Shafiul Alam, Md. Shofiqul Islam, Tasnia Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This paper presents defect detection and characterization methods of semiconductor devices using morphological image processing. The proposed algorithm can detect various processing and fabrication defects, such as cracks, delamination, EOS, and ESD damage, from SEM images of the devices. It can also characterize the defects based on properties of the defected region. Like other image processing computational methods of defect detection; this algorithm does not need any reference image to compare with the inspected image. The algorithm involves different steps like filtering, global thresholding, segmentation, edge detection, feature extraction and defect characterization. The algorithm is implemented using MATLAB R2018b Software. The proposed method is cost-effective and less time-consuming for defect detection of semiconductor devices.

title: Enhancing Bangla Spam SMS Detection with BERT: A Deep Learning Perspective
authors: Jobaida Abedin, Yasha Fatema, Farzana Tasnim, Fariba Tasnia Khan, Tanjim Mahmud, Rakhimjon Rajapboyevich Rakhimov, Valisher Sapayev Odilbek Uglu, Abubokor Hanip, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Spam messages pose a persistent problem in digital communication, particularly for low-resource languages like Bangla. The complex grammatical structure of Bangla, frequent code-mixing with English, and informal writing styles make spam detection far more challenging than in high-resource languages such as English. This study explores a comparative deep learning approach for classifying Bangla spam SMS messages. The models evaluated include Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT), BanglaBERT, XLM-BERT, and a hybrid BiLSTM + CNN model. A balanced dataset of 2,004 Bangla SMS messages- 1,030 spam and 971 ham-has been prepared for training and evaluation. Among the tested models, BanglaBERT has achieved the highest accuracy of 98.5 %. This outcome has demonstrated the strength of language-specific transformer models in capturing the contextual and structural nuances of Bangla text. Explainable AI (XAI) methods, including LIME and SHAP, have been applied to interpret the model's internal decision-making process and ensure transparency. The analysis has revealed that the words “ফ্রি”, “অফার”, and “জয়” are the most influential indicators for identifying spam messages. The proposed framework has achieved an effective balance between performance and interpretability, setting a new benchmark for Bangla spam SMS detection. This work contributes to the growing field of Bangla Natural Language Processing (NLP) and illustrates that combining sequential learning with contextual modeling can effectively address the challenges of spam detection in low-resource languages.

title: Fundamental-Frequency Switching Strategy for a Five-Level Cascaded H-Bridge Inverter Using Selective Harmonic Elimination
authors: Sadikur Rahman, Fahim Shaharia, Md Zunaid Hossen, Samia Sultana Tuly, Ziam Chowdhury Noun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This paper presents the design, simulation, and experimental validation of a single-phase five-level Cascaded Hbridge (CHB) inverter employing Selective Harmonic Elimination (SHE) with Newton-Raphson-based switching-angle computation. The topology comprises two H-bridge cells fed by isolated DC sources, synthesizing a five-level stepped output <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left\{-2 V_{d c},-V_{d c}, 0, V_{d c}, 2 V_{d c}\right\}$</tex>. A Fourier model is derived and SHE equations with two switching angles per quarter-cycle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\alpha_{1}, \alpha_{2})$</tex> are formulated to set the fundamental while canceling dominant low-order harmonics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5^{{th }}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7^{{th }})$</tex>. Angles are solved iteratively via Newton-Raphson and executed on a controller that drives the CHB power stage at fundamental frequency. A MATLAB/Simulink model is used to verify waveform synthesis, RMS voltage, and harmonic spectra. Hardware measurements using a Fluke power analyzer show close agreement with theory; for a representative case with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha_{1} \approx 17.9^{\circ}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha_{2} \approx 57.4^{\circ}$</tex>, the analyzer reported <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{rms}} \approx 16.87 \mathbf{V}$</tex> at 49.2 Hz and Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 20.4 \%$</tex>. The results demonstrate that five-level SHE with fundamental-frequency switching achieves effective harmonic control without high-frequency PWM, making the approach well-suited to cost-sensitive UPS, renewable energy interfaces, and low-frequency motor drives.

title: Are Pose Estimators Ready for the Open World? STAGE: A GenAI Toolkit for Auditing 3D Human Pose Estimators
authors: Nikita Kister, István Sárándi, Jiayi Wang, Anna Khoreva, Gerard Pons-Moll
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: For safety-critical applications, it is crucial to audit 3D human pose estimators before deployment. Will the system break down if the weather or the clothing changes? Is it robust regarding gender and age? To answer these questions and more, we need controlled studies with images that differ in a single attribute, but real benchmarks cannot provide such pairs. We thus present STAGE, a GenAI data toolkit for auditing 3D human pose estimators. For STAGE, we develop the first GenAI image creator with accurate 3D pose control and propose a novel evaluation strategy to isolate and quantify the effects of single factors such as gender, ethnicity, age, clothing, location, and weather. Enabled by STAGE, we generate a series of benchmarks to audit, for the first time, the sensitivity of popular pose estimators towards such factors. Our results show that natural variations can severely degrade pose estimator performance, raising doubts about their readiness for open-world deployment. We aim to highlight these robustness issues and establish STAGE as a benchmark to quantify them.

title: A Deep Learning and Explainable Ai Framework to Rank Solar Irradiance Zones in Southern Bangladesh
authors: Wazia Haque Onti, Safiul Haque Chowdhury, Mohammed Ibrahim Hussain, Muhammad Minoar Hossain, Mohammad Mamun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Bangladesh currently faces a shortage of natural gas, inefficient power plants, and significant system losses, which prevent the country from meeting its electricity demand. In this case, solar energy utilization in Bangladesh has great potential, especially in southern Bangladesh. This study presents a data-driven framework for assessing and modeling multivariate environmental and solar energy data in the context of renewable energy research. The workflow starts with collecting and preprocessing the datasets to guarantee data quality and uniformity. Exploratory data visualization: Heatmaps and KDE plot analyses are a refinement of the classics, primarily centered around understanding feature distributions and relationships. In the following phases, the model is trained, and predictions are made from the extracted feature representations using Autoencoder-based modeling for unsupervised feature extraction and performance analysis with standard metrics evaluating reconstruction quality. Then, the reduced feature space is used for K-means clustering and validation using clustering validity indices such as Silhouette Score, Calinski-Harabasz Index, and Davies-Bouldin Index. Lastly, classification modeling and SHAP are executed to explain our model's outcome interpretation through the CatBoost classifier. Finally, solar zones are ranked utilizing the clustering score, and a visualization map determines geographically advantageous areas to locate solar energy.

title: Noninvasive Assessment of Lung Fluid Accumulation Using Tetrapolar Impedance Measurement
authors: Mahjabin Mobarak, Habiba Begum, Md. Moniruzzaman, Ruhul Amin, Abdullah Al Mahfazur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Electrical impedance technique is a noninvasive and low-cost technique which plays a very significant role in bioimpedance measurement. Lung being dynamic organ, where the impedance changes during respiration, and the changes of impedance is an indicator of lungs condition. This study investigates the performance of a six-electrode Tetrapolar Impedance Measurement (TPIM) configuration in isolating and monitoring the left and right lungs individually, with a focus on the impact of fluid accumulation in the lower part of the right lung. Simulations were conducted using a phantom with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$663 \text{cm}^{3}$</tex> of fluid introduced into the lower segment of the right lung. 6electrode TPIM configurations were implemented by placing electrode on the right side (RS) and left side (LS) of the thorax to assess sensitivity to each lung. Results indicated that in the absence of fluid, percentage changes in total impedance during respiration were nearly identical for RS and LS placements (∼ 34%). However, with fluid in the right lung, the RS placement showed a significant drop in percentage change (from 34.65% to 9.76%), while the LS placement remained largely unaffected (35.24%). This validates the TPIM's ability to distinguish between lung conditions. Additionally, simulations at various vertical electrode placements showed that the percentage change increased gradually with elevation, reaching a peak about 15 cm, indicating sensitivity to fluid location.

title: CAOA - Completion-Assisted Object-CAD Alignment
authors: Hiranya Garbha Kumar, Minhas Kamal, Balakrishnan Prabhakaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: Accurately aligning CAD models to their corresponding objects in indoor RGB-D scans is a central challenge in 3D semantic reconstruction. The task requires estimating a 9-Degree-of-Freedom (DoF) pose-position, rotation, and scale along three axes-but is hindered by noisy and incomplete scans, as well as segmentation errors that cause geometric distortions. We present Completion-Assisted Ob-ject-CAD Alignment (CAOA), a method that integrates a semantically and contextually aware point cloud completion module with a symmetry-aware relative pose estimation algorithm, enabling precise alignment of CAD models to scanned objects. Existing completion methods are typically trained and evaluated on synthetic datasets, which often fail to generalize to real-world scans. To bridge this gap, we introduce a synthetic data generation strategy tailored to indoor scenes, significantly reducing the synthetic-to-real domain gap-validated through quantitative comparisons with widely used completion datasets. In addition, we release S 2 C-Completion, an expert-annotated dataset of over 8,500 object-CAD pairs from Scan2CAD, created for real-world indoor single-object completion and intended as a new benchmark for this task. For object-CAD alignment, we incorporate symmetry information via a symmetry-aware loss, improving robustness to symmetric ambiguities. On the Scan2CAD benchmark, CAOA achieves a 17 % accuracy improvement over state-of-the-art methods. All code, datasets, and annotation tools will be publicly available on GitHub.

title: RXV: Interpretable RF-Stacked CNNs for Brain Tumor MRI Classification
authors: Abu Tahim Habiby, Anika Bushra, Shaikh Ahmed Fayaz, Md. Esrat Hossan, Md. Mahim Khan, Md. Asaduzzaman Asaduzzaman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Brain tumors cause significant deaths across the world with, early and correct diagnosis being crucial in enhancing patient survival. Although deep learning has shown impressive results in medical imaging, single convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can experience poor generalization because of inter-class similarity, small size of lesions and variations in image quality of MRI. The proposed study, RXV, aims to deal with these issues by combining three highest-performing CNNs ResNet50V2, VGG16 and Xception with a 100-tree Random Forest meta-learner. An equal sample of 6,004 brain MRI images of four categories (glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and normal) was used, and CLAHE preprocessed, and augmented extensively to increase robustness. RXV obtained an accuracy of 99.56% on the test, which was more than the individual CNNs (maximum error was ResNet50V2 with a 99.33 percent), and minimized the error to 4 out of 901 tests. Grad-CAM was used to ensure interpretability, demonstrating the areas of tumor relevance. These results make RXV a consistent, understandable, and deployable CAD system.

title: Corrections to “Semi-Classical Monte Carlo Simulation of Quantum Confinement Effects in Si Nanosheet and Fin FETs in Industry Standard Orientation”
authors: Uiho Lee, Jaeyeon Kim, Byoung Hak Hong, Wookhyun Kwon, Branch T. Archer, Leonard F. Register, Sanjay K. Banerjee, Jiwon Chang
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: Presents corrections to the paper, (“Semi-Classical Monte Carlo Simulation of Quantum Confinement Effects in Si Nanosheet and Fin FETs in Industry Standard Orientation”).

title: Temperature-Based Hybrid Modeling for Heating Power Prediction and Fault Detection in Power Transformers
authors: Fanghui Yin, Siyu Nie, Shaowei Rao, Guotao Feng, Tao Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Accurate estimation of internal heating power is essential for transformer thermal assessment and fault diagnosis. However, the multi-physics coupling and nonlinear heat transfer in oil-immersed transformers make it difficult to infer heating power directly from external temperatures using purely physical models. This paper proposes a hybrid temperature-based framework that integrates physical understanding with data-driven learning. The physical thermal–electrical model is used to reveal the nonlinear temperature–power relationships, guiding feature selection via mutual information (MI) analysis. Selected temperature features are then applied to a random forest (RF) regressor for heating power prediction. Experiments on a thermally equivalent scaled prototype of a 500 kV power transformer, involving 28 operating cases under both normal and fault conditions, demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high prediction accuracy, with an average test <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> of 0.9956 and a test mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 2.27%. Moreover, deviations in predicted heating power under constant load effectively indicate localized thermal anomalies. The results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid approach offers a practical and interpretable solution for transformer condition monitoring using only external temperature measurements.

title: Secrecy Analysis of Pinching-Antenna Systems
authors: Osamah S. Badarneh, Hugerles S. Silva, Meysam Ghanbari, Yazan H. Al-Badarneh, Tamer M. Khattab, Mazen O. Hasna, Khalid A. Qaraqe
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the performance of physical-layer security of a pinching-antenna system on a lossless dielectric waveguide. In particular, the system uses a single pinching-antenna to convey confidential information from a base station to a legitimate destination equipped with a single antenna, while an eavesdropper, also equipped with a single antenna, attempts to decode the transmitted information. As such, the performance of the pinching-antenna system is evaluated in terms of average secrecy capacity, strictly positive secrecy capacity, and secrecy outage probability. To this end, accurate mathematical expressions for the aforementioned performance metrics are provided. To validate the analysis, the analytical results are numerically evaluated and further validated through MonteCarlo simulations. The results demonstrate that secrecy capacity between the base station and the legitimate destination improves when the height of the pinching-antenna placed closer to the destination. Additionally, the performance can be improved when the eavesdropper's location over a rectangular area increases.

title: Voltage-Dependent Dark Current Modeling in Thin-Film Solar Cells: Effects of Surface Recombination and Device Parameters
authors: Mesbahus Saleheen, A. Arefin, Chamak Ganguli, Shahana Akter
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: In thin-film solar cells, dark current serves as a key indicator of diode quality and recombination dynamics. Its dependence on applied voltage provides valuable information about both interfacial recombination process and bulk transport limitations. In this work, we present a comprehensive analytical model that captures the voltage-dependent behavior of dark current in thin-film devices. The model explicitly incorporates the influence of surface recombination velocity, carrier mobilities, and layer thicknesses on the dark current profile across a range of applied voltages. The model is validated using numerical simulations perfomed in SCAPS-1D for a simplified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{n}^{+} \mathrm{p}$</tex> solar cell structure. Our results reveal that surface recombination at interfaces significantly alters the ideality factor and leakage current, especially in low-voltage regimes. Parameter variation studies highlight the importance of surface passivation and optimized layer design to minimize recombination losses and improve charge collection. The proposed framework offers a reproducible tool for diagnosing dark current behavior and guiding material and interface engineering in next-generation thin-film photovoltaics.

title: Intelligent Heat Stress Management: Economic Impacts and Policy Strategies for Informal Women Workers
authors: Md. Sakib Zubayer, Siam Muhammad Shakil, Lamia Ferdous, Md. Taraquzzaman Tonmay
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This study examines the economic impact of heat exposure on informal women workers in Rajshahi City, Bangladesh, focusing on income losses and out-of-pocket (OOP) expenditures. Using a cross-sectional survey, it evaluates how income from cooling affects the unpaid care work that generates heat-related economic burdens. The results suggest that cooling-related income loss reductions benefited street vendors, synopses, and auxiliary construction workers the most, who were the greatest beneficiaries of improved cooling resources. On the other hand, unpaid care work increases income loss among home-based pieceworkers. The average heat stress-related income loss is estimated at 19%, which is nearly BDT 1,950 per worker per month. The total economic loss, when extended to the informal workforce of Rajshahi, is estimated to be between BDT 94 million and BDT 146 million per heat event. To complement these empirical findings and address reviewer recommendations, the paper introduces a concise intelligent heat-stress management framework integrating low-cost IoT microclimate sensing, lightweight predictive modeling, and mobile alerting to support early risk detection and worker protection. The study calls for policy interventions to enhance cooling access and adaptation for vulnerable informal workers facing climate change-induced heat stress.

title: PASTA: Controllable Part-Aware Shape Generation with Autoregressive Transformers
authors: Songlin Li, Despoina Paschalidou, Leonidas Guibas
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: The increased demand for tools that automate the 3D content creation process led to tremendous progress in deep generative models that can generate diverse 3D objects of high fidelity. In this paper, we present PASTA, an autoregressive transformer architecture for generating high quality 3D shapes. PASTA comprises two main components: An autoregressive transformer that generates objects as a sequence of cuboidal primitives and a blending network, implemented with a transformer decoder that composes the sequences of cuboids and synthesizes high quality meshes for each object. Our model is trained in two stages: First we train our autoregressive generative model using only annotated cuboidal parts as supervision and next, we train our blending network using explicit 3D supervision, in the form of watertight meshes. Evaluations on various ShapeNet objects showcase the ability of our model to perform shape generation from diverse inputs e.g. from scratch, from a partial object, from text and images, as well size-guided generation, by explicitly conditioning on a bounding box that defines the object's boundaries. Moreover, as our model considers the underlying part-based structure of a 3D object, we are able to select a specific part and produce shapes with meaningful variations of this part. As evidenced by our experiments, our model generates 3D shapes that are both more realistic and diverse than existing part-based and non part-based methods, while at the same time is simpler to implement and train.

title: Key-Driven Identity Protection and Secure Traceability for Face-Swapping
authors: Miaomiao Wang, Sheng Li, Xinpeng Zhang, Guorui Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Face swapping replaces the identity of a target face with that of a source face while retaining non-identity attributes, and is widely used in privacy protection scenarios. However, removing identity information makes it difficult to support sub sequent recognition tasks. Although active tracing methods can determine content ownership, techniques such as watermarking rely on external data binding, which cannot establish identity associations in the feature space nor support authorization controlled identity verification. To address this issue, we propose a key-driven Identity Protection and Secure Traceability (IPST) framework for face-swapped images, which enables identity bind ing and controlled verification without exposing visual identity. The proposed method operates as a plug-in applied to face swapping results, introducing an implicit identity association in the feature space so that the target identity participates in the generation process while remaining visually imperceptible. Specifically, IPST consists of three stages. First, an identity transfer stage aligns the generated image with the target identity in the recognition space via feature-guided perturbations. Second, an identity hiding stage performs key-controlled latent space editing to map the identity into a concealed state, ensuring that the published image cannot be directly recognized. Finally, an identity recovery stage reconstructs the target identity under authorized conditions through inverse latent manipulation, enabling identity verification using face recognition models. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves stable identity binding while preserving visual quality, and shows strong robustness against unauthorized recovery. Moreover, the framework is model-agnostic and can be seamlessly integrated with existing face swapping systems, making it suitable for practical deployment.

title: Comprehensive Analysis and Noise Suppression of Real-World Mud Pulse Telemetry Using an Adaptive Signal Decomposition Method for Downhole Sensing
authors: Nimra Irshad, Mingdong Xu, Nan Xie, Henry Leung
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Reliable signal extraction in mud pulse telemetry (MPT) is critical for accurate pressure-based downhole sensing and safe drilling operations. To enhance sensing reliability and suppress severe noise in harsh drilling environments, this article presents an enhanced approach to mud pulse signal processing using an Adaptive Trend Variational Mode Decomposition (ATVMD) algorithm. Traditional signal decomposition methods, such as Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), face notable limitations in practical MPT scenarios due to their sensitivity to the predefined number of modes and their inability to handle strong global signal trends induced by poorly synchronized mud pumps. Moreover, spectral overlaps between non-harmonic pump noise and the useful signal, especially in the presence of strong low-frequency noise affecting the signal’s trend, leads to severe mode mixing, which in turn lowers decoding accuracy and sensor system reliability. However, the proposed ATVMD framework dynamically determines the number of modes (k) and explicitly extracts dominant signal trends. An Otsu-based thresholding strategy applied to Power Spectral Density of signals is employed to identify and suppress the strongest noise components. Additionally, an adaptive filtering technique is used to extract the strong signal’s trends that enhance VMD’s ability to accurately decompose the remaining signal into its true modes. The approach is validated through both synthetic datasets and real-field measurements collected from drilling operations in diverse geological environments. Experimental results demonstrate a significant improvement in mud pulse signal recovery under harsh noise conditions, achieving a pulse detection accuracy of 97.65% with a 95% confidence interval of [97.01%, 98.28%] across 31 detected pulses.

title: Learning High-Fidelity Garment Deformation via Skinning-Free Image Transfer
authors: Rong Wang, Wei Mao, Changsheng Lu, Hongdong Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: We present a novel method for generating 3D garment deformations from underlying body poses, which is key to a wide range of applications, including virtual try-on and extended reality. To simplify the cloth dynamics, existing methods mostly rely on linear blend skinning to obtain low-frequency posed garment shape and only regress highfrequency wrinkles. However, due to the lack of explicit skinning supervision, such skinning-based approach often produces misaligned shapes when posing the garment, consequently corrupts the high-frequency signals and fails to recover high-fidelity wrinkles. To tackle this issue, we propose a skinning-free approach by independently estimating posed (i) vertex position for low-frequency posed garment shape, and (ii) vertex normal for high-frequency local wrinkle details. In this way, each frequency modality can be effectively decoupled and directly supervised by the geometry of the deformed garment. To further improve the visual quality of deformation, we propose to encode both vertex attributes as rendered texture images, so that 3D garment deformation can be equivalently achieved via 2D image transfer. This enables us to leverage powerful pretrained image models to recover fine-grained visual details in wrinkles, while maintaining superior scalability for garments of diverse topologies without relying on manual UV partition. Finally, we propose a multimodal fusion to incorporate constraints from both frequency modalities and robustly recover deformed 3D garments from transferred images. Extensive experiments show that our method significantly improves animation quality on various garment types and recovers finer wrinkles than state-of-the-art methods.

title: Masthead
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Computer Graphics and Applications

title: Knowledge-Driven Memetic Algorithm with Forgetting Mechanism for the Energy-Aware Flexible Job-Shop Scheduling Problem with Variable Machining Speeds
authors: Hongquan Qu, Peng Shi, Maolin Cai, Yibo Li, Xiaomeng Tong
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Against the backdrop of global energy conservation, emission reduction, and sustainable development, energy-aware scheduling in manufacturing workshops has become a growing focus. However, existing research on the energy-aware flexible job-shop scheduling problem (EFJSP) with variable machining speeds primarily considers energy consumption at the job level, neglecting the energy heterogeneity of individual operations under different constraints, a factor of key environmental significance. To address this problem, this paper innovatively incorporates processing quality constraints to define upper limits on variable speeds. The study develops a mathematical model for the speed-adjustable flexible job shop scheduling problem considering operation-level energy consumption. Considering limitations in current multi-neighborhood structure selection, we propose a knowledge-driven memetic algorithm with a forgetting mechanism (KDMA-FM). The model's effectiveness is validated through comparisons with the exact solver OR-Tools on a simplified system. Furthermore, KDMA-FM's performance is evaluated against existing algorithms across instances of varying scales. Results demonstrate that KDMA-FM outperforms benchmark algorithms in over 80% of instances and achieves notable energy savings.

title: Towards Real-Time Tea Leaf Disease Management: A Deep Learning Solution for Resource-Constrained Environments
authors: Zakia Sultana Simin, Md. Raisul Islam, Saiba Aabira, Arnob Ghosh, Arshean Subah, Kazi Newaj Faisal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: As one of the most consumed beverages worldwide, tea holds substantial economic importance in Bangladesh, contributing significantly to both production and employment. Nevertheless, tea plants are susceptible to numerous diseases that can adversely affect crop yield and quality. This research introduces a deep learning methodology for the automated detection of tea leaf diseases, utilizing the teaLeafBD dataset. This dataset comprises <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5, 2 7 8}$</tex> high-resolution images of tea leaves sourced from various tea estates in Bangladesh, encompassing six distinct tea leaf diseases and one category for healthy leaves. An efficient and lightweight convolutional neural pretrained network (EfficientNetV2B0), was utilized for the proposed model. The proposed model was successful in classifying diseases, achieving a comprehensive accuracy of 95.45 %. The proposed model demonstrated superior performance compared to other lightweight baseline models and previously developed models, exhibiting high classification accuracy alongside computational efficiency. The efficacy of the model was also assessed through metrics such as precision, recall, F1-score, and Cohen's Kappa, underscoring its robustness, particularly in addressing class imbalance. Additionally, the study highlights the model's potential for real-time use in devices like mobile phones and drones, giving tea farmers in Bangladesh a practical way to manage diseases and boost productivity.

title: ClustRecNet: A Novel End-to-End Deep Learning Framework for Clustering Algorithm Recommendation
authors: Mohammadreza Bakhtyari, Bogdan Mazoure, Renato Cordeiro De Amorim, Guillaume Rabusseau, Vladimir Makarenkov
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Identifying an effective clustering algorithm for a given dataset remains a fundamental unsupervised learning issue. We introduce ClustRecNet, a novel end-to-end deep learning framework that recommends suitable clustering algorithm(s) by directly learning high-order representations of raw tabular data. To facilitate robust meta-learning, we first construct a comprehensive repository of 34, 000 synthetic datasets encompassing a large variety of clustering scenarios, run 10 popular clustering algorithms, and use Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) to establish ground-truth labels. ClustRecNet’s architecture integrates a convolutional, two residual, and an attention block to capture both local and global structural patterns, effectively bypassing the knowledge bottleneck associated with manual feature engineering. Extensive evaluation on both synthetic and real-world benchmarks demonstrates that ClustRecNet consistently outperforms traditional internal cluster validity indices such as Silhouette, Calinski-Harabasz, Davies–Bouldin, and Dunn as well as state-of-the-art Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) approaches such as ML2DAC, AutoCluster, and AutoML4Clust. For example, our framework achieves an average 0.497 ARI gain over the Calinski–Harabasz cluster validity index on synthetic data and an average 44.16% ARI improvement over the leading AutoML approach (ML2DAC) on real-world benchmarks.

title: Simulation-Based Modeling and Standing Stabilization of a Bird-Inspired Bipedal Robot with Multi-Joint Dynamics with Contact
authors: Alejandra Paola Ticlla Gonzalez, Ricardo Sebastian Arce Calero, Eddie Angel Sobrado Malpartida, Jose Luis Becerra Felipe, Ayrton Krickst Nieves Acosta, Nain Maximo Ramos Alvarez
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This work presents the modeling and simulation of a bipedal robot that is structurally humanoid while adopting locomotion principles inspired by avian gait. Rather than replicating bird anatomy, the proposed robot employs a simplified lowerlimb configuration composed of femur, tibia, and ankle joints, emphasizing heel-less support and a forward-leaning posture characteristic of bird locomotion. The system is implemented in a physics-based simulation environment using the MuJoCo engine to support the development and validation of baseline standing balance control strategies over short time horizons. A joint-level proportional-derivative (PD) control framework, augmented with hierarchical stabilization of the floating base, is employed to achieve and maintain a stable initial upright posture under gravity and foot-ground contact interactions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed model can maintain a consistent standing configuration with bounded joint motion and physically plausible contact behavior over short durations, validating the mechanical model and control structure. The presented framework emphasizes mechanical realism, structured control design, and reproducible simulation setup, providing a baseline platform for future extensions toward dynamic locomotion control.

title: Integrating HTS Technology in Wind Energy Systems: A Case for Sustainable Power in Bangladesh
authors: Nuzhat Ahmed Rudhy, Sabrina Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Bangladesh's energy consumption is increasing while its natural gas reserves are declining making it necessary for the country to explore renewable energy sources. Wind power holds great promise but traditional turbines are limited by high costs and can be inefficient. This study is a feasibility survey that investigates the integration of High-Temperature Superconducting (HTS) generators into wind turbines for the coastal regions of Bangladesh. Using wind resource data and MATLAB simulations, HTS machines were compared with Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generators (PMSGs). Results show that HTS turbines yield <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 - 1 2 \%}$</tex> more energy at mean wind speeds of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-6 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, while reducing nacelle weight and improving efficiency. Economic analysis indicates higher onshore costs but <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> lower Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) in offshore fixed and floating applications. This study validates the positive impacts on the power sector of Bangladesh of incorporating HTS technology into the next generation wind turbines by performing simulative studies.

title: Blood Cancer Classification from Microscopic Images via ViTResNetEnsemble with XAI
authors: Tasnim Sultana Sintheia, Mahafozur Rahman, Badhon Sutrudhar, Saif Mostasir Rohan, Mian Mohammad Rassel, Md. Sayem Kabir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Blood cancers, particularly leukemia, remain a major cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, placing significant pressure on healthcare systems due to their rapid progression and the need for early, accurate detection. Ensuring timely and reliable identification of leukemia subtypes is therefore critical, yet manual microscopic examination is both laborintensive and susceptible to observer variability. To address this challenge, this study introduces ViTResNetEnsemble, a hybrid deep learning model designed to capture both global structural patterns and fine-grained cellular details in leukocyte images. The model combines Vision Transformer embeddings with ResNet50-v2 feature representations, forming a complementary architecture that enhances discrimination among visually similar cancer subtypes. A balanced dataset of 8,000 images was created through targeted augmentation to reduce class imbalance and improve generalization. Trained with optimized hyperparameters and evaluated through extensive metrics, the proposed model achieves 99.17% accuracy with strong reliability metrics (Cohen's Kappa = 0.989, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MCC}=0.989$</tex>, Macro <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F} 1=0.992$</tex>), outperforming several CNN and Transformer baselines. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of each model component, while calibration curves and XAI techniques (LIME, Grad-CAM, Grad-CAM++) provide insights into prediction reliability and clinical interpretability. These results highlight the potential of hybrid Transformer-CNN models to deliver robust and transparent assistance in hematological diagnosis.

title: Bengali Fake Shield: Developing an Efficient Transformer-based Fake News Detection System for Bengali Language
authors: Ranjana Karmaker, Fariya Islam, Tasnia Ishrat Khan, Promi Madhu, Sayma Alam Suha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Fake news is a huge threat to social trust and cohesion whichhave little resources,particularly in languages such as Bengali. But fake news is hard to find in these languages: There are not many annotated datasets and the grammar structurescan be complicated. In this work we introduce Bengali FakeShield, a strong detecting system offake news in Bengali based on transformer models. We used theBanFakeNews dataset with 48,000 real news and 1,000 fake news. It also addresses class imbalanced problemby applying SMOTE and upsampling. A exhaustive analysis of diverse models (including traditional machine learning and deep-learning approaches) was carriedout. Bangla-BERT performed the best among them, interms of accuracy by 99.97% and recall by 100 %. The final model was then integrated intoa Flutter app optimised for real-time inference using TensorFlow Lite. This work provides a scalable method for addressing misinformation in Bengali and demonstrates the transformers effectiveness for low-resourcelanguage settings.

title: Influence of geometry and polarization parameters on piezoelectric response of polypropylene piezoelectrets with an arch structure
authors: Zehai Ruan, Zhiming Shi, Xiaoqing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Flexible piezoelectret sensors have significant advantages in the application of electronic skin to capture dynamic mechanical signals, showing promise in robotic perception. In this study, polypropylene (PP) piezoelectret films with an arched air-filled channel structure were designed and systematically investigated by theoretical analysis, simulation calculation and experimental verification. The results demonstrate that the piezoelectric properties of such piezoelectrets can be predicted via a capacitance energy method, and the films exhibit significant anisotropy in their piezoelectric response and excellent shape adaptability. Specifically, for piezoelectret film samples constructed from two 38 μm-thick PP electret films, with an arch height (apex-to-base distance) of 200 μm and a cell width of 2 mm, the piezoelectric d33 coefficient attains its maximum value when the surface potentials of the two films are set to ±1176 V, respectively. The anisotropic piezoelectric response in the films was successfully leveraged for multi-dimensional force sensing. When the applied force (~82 mN) slides along different in-plane directions, the sample yields markedly distinct piezoelectric responses. The motion perpendicular to the arched air-channel axis generates a periodic charge signal (~40 pC), while the motion parallel to the arched air-channel axis almost don’t generate discernible signal, manifesting a pronounced anisotropy. Therefore, taking into account the distance between the channels, the device can identify direction and speed of force sliding on it.

title: Joint Channel and AoA Estimation in OTFS System with Fractional Doppler Channels
authors: Lin Litao, Boyi Tang, Zhen Chen, Jie Tang, Kai-Kit Wong, Chan-Byoung Chae, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation is a promising technology for high-Doppler-effect mobile communication. However, fractional Doppler shifts cause inter Doppler interference, degrading the channel estimation (CE) performance, particularly at low signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs). Previous approaches have framed Doppler shift estimation as a direction-of-arrival problem but have limited accuracy because of the finite number of available Doppler points. To overcome this challenge, we propose a delay-staggered pilot scheme that leverages the abundant resources in the delay domain, effectively increasing the number of points for CE and enhancing its accuracy. Additionally, we introduce a novel angle-of-arrival estimation method tailored to the unique channel characteristics of OTFS modulation, reducing the antenna array requirements. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior NMSE performance compared with existing approaches, including embedded pilot-based CE and grid evolution-based CE algorithms.

title: A Variegated Look at Direct-to-Cell Satellites in the Wild

title: Do Not Forget the Role of Address Mapping Policy in Achieving DRAM-Less SSD Performance
authors: Yang Zhou, Fang Wang, Zhan Shi, Dan Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: For a long time, fully exploiting the multi-level parallelism of SSDs has been an important technique for optimizing SSD performance. By distributing data to different flash parallel units through address mapping policies, SSDs can achieve multi-task parallel processing, thereby significantly improving data read and write performance. However, our experiments show that when SSD blocking causes task queues to stack up, excessive pursuit of flash parallelism can cause performance interference between multiple users, thereby reducing SSD performance. Additionally, in DRAM-less SSDs, differences in data access patterns directly impact the address mapping module, thereby exacerbating the degree of performance degradation. Based on the above analysis, this paper conducts a systematic study of the impact mechanism of different data flows on address mapping policies and proposes a hybrid address mapping allocation policy with dynamic switching (HMpp) for DRAM-less SSDs. This policy identifies and classifies different data access patterns to dynamically select the appropriate address mapping policy, thereby reducing multi-user interference and improving SSD performance. Experimental results based on real cloud block storage workloads show that this policy can achieve 87.2% performance improvement and 77.3% fairness optimization, while reducing the write amplification factor by 32.9% to 40.2%.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Helmet for Real Time Rider Safety Monitoring and Automated Emergency Response System Technology
authors: P. Rajeswari, D. Kishore, M. Manoj, T. Logeshkumar, R. Dhanushkodi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In this case, two-wheeler accidents are a serious issue, as it takes a long time to respond to them, and no one can pay attention to a rider in real time. The proposed project suggests the use of an IoT-Enhanced Smart Helmet that will monitor the rider's real-time safety and automatically respond to emergencies to enhance road safety and minimize deaths from accidents. It has developed the system around the ESP32 microcontroller as the core processing unit upon which the data is received, data is analyzed, and communications are realized. The helmet will be smart and include a number of sensors, including an accelerometer and vibration sensor, to notify of unexpected impacts, unusual movements, and possible accidents. The alcohol sensor that measures the rider's alcohol concentration monitors for unscrupulous riding. It is also sustainable and energy-efficient because it uses a solar panel, a charging controller, and a battery, which means it can be applied in the long term. It has a GPS module that enables it to get the rider's geographical position in real time, as well as in an emergency. The sensor data, along with the location, is sent over GPRS to the Adafruit Cloud, so one can always monitor the sensor even without being physically present. The system will automatically email the programmed emergency contacts with important information, such as location, in case of an accident or when alcohol consumption exceeds the required mandate. The presented smart helmet system can contribute to rider safety by enabling the detection of accidents in time, emergency notifications, and real-time monitoring. It uses less energy, is a renewable source, and supports IoT architecture; that is why it is a dependable solution for intelligent transportation and next-generation road safety applications.

title: Total Ionizing Dose Effects on Clock Systems and Peripheral Modules of the MSP430FR6989
authors: I. Hudson, J. Carpenter, T. Peyton, J. Kim, J. Ermi, R. Baltazar Felipe, T. D. Loveless
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Total ionizing dose (TID) testing was performed on the Texas Instruments (TI) MSP430FR6989 microcontroller to evaluate the effects of bias dependence and peripheral module degradation. The clock system, analog-to-digital converter (ADC), and Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter (UART) peripherals were tested across two low-power modes (LPMs) and more than 30 configurations. The results reveal bias- and peripheral-dependent variability in TID response, both functional and catastrophic failures, and recovery of device functionality through room-temperature annealing.

title: Rolling Bearing Domain Generalization Fault Diagnosis Method Based on CWT-MTNet
authors: Peng Li, Chenyang Lu, Lixiu Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the failure of traditional deep transfer learning when target domain labels are completely unavailable for key equipment under extreme working conditions, this paper proposes a domain generalization fault diagnosis model (CWTMTNet) based on Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) and multi-resolution feature fusion vision Transformer. CWT converts 1D vibration signals into 2D time-frequency diagrams to enhance non-stationary feature expression. An enhanced multi-resolution feature fusion (EnhancedMRF) structure extracts multi-scale local features, while a lightweight Swin Transformer performs global association modeling to extract domain-invariant features. Experiments on the CWRU bearing dataset show 99.75% accuracy under single condition, 91.5% average accuracy in crossload transfer without target labels, and 91.2% accuracy under 6dB noise, demonstrating excellent generalization and anti-noise capability.

title: Modeling and Experiment of Horizontal Vibration Isolation for Series-Connected Inverted Pendulum and Single-Chamber Pneumatic Spring
authors: Qiang Li, Dengfeng Xu, Baolong Yu, Gang Wang, Wenhui Zhang, Liekai Su, Guoting Huang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To improve the horizontal vibration isolation performance of pneumatic springs, this paper proposes a novel structure that serially connects a single-chamber pneumatic spring with an inverted pendulum. Taking the pneumatic spring piston as the research object, the compressed air pressure is calculated, the horizontal restoring force and stiffness of the spring are deduced, and the horizontal vibration isolation dynamic equation as well as the analytical expression of horizontal natural frequency is established. Parametric analysis identifies the inverted pendulum length as the key factor governing horizontal vibration isolation performance. For heavyload and low-frequency vibration isolation engineering demands, a four-unit experimental vibration isolation system with a 10-ton bearing capacity and a designed horizontal natural frequency of nearly 1 Hz is developed. Experimental tests show the actual horizontal natural frequency is 0.75 Hz, and the horizontal transmissibility reaches −10 dB at an external horizontal disturbance frequency of 4.5 Hz, validating the theoretical design. This system realizes heavy-load bearing and low-frequency vibration isolation simultaneously, and has broad application prospects in the horizontal vibration isolation of bases for precision manufacturing, optics, life sciences, medicine and other fields.

title: Novel “S-Cut” Flows for Highly Uniform Multi-Tier Integrated Oxide Semiconductor DRAM
authors: Ziheng Bai, Hao Shi, Zhou He, Zhao Liu, Jianqi Chen, Zhiyu Tian, Huan Liu, Jiahan Ke, Jiajia Wu, Gangping Yan, Wenjun Chen, Zehuan Hei, Tongshang Su, Baodong Han, Xueli Ma, Xiangsheng Wang, Yuke Li, Jinjuan Xiang, Weihan Fan, Hongbo Sun, Guilei Wang, Hao Meng, Mingyuan Liu, Kanyu Cao, Chao Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: We propose “S-Cut” (S-shaped Cut) integration flows to achieve five-tier oxide-based DRAM transistors and a 64 × 256 array without parasitic channels on a 12-inch manufacturing line. Two “S-Cut” approaches are investigated: the “outer-cut” flow, which removes the parasitic channel from the bit line direction, and the “inner-cut” flow, which removes it from inside the transistor hole. After flow and process optimization, including bit line/oxide channel contact optimization and common bit line/staircase parasitic resistance reduction, the “inner-cut” flow demonstrates good uniformity both across the five tiers and across the entire 12-inch wafer. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{on}}$</tex> of a single transistor reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77 \mu \mathrm{A} / \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{off}}$</tex> is less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 10^{-18} \mathrm{A}$</tex>/cell, and I-V hysteresis is nearly 0 mV. Remarkably, the uniformity (Std./Mean across the 12-inch wafer) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{th}}$</tex> among the five tiers ranges from 0.10 to 0.22, while that of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{on}}$</tex> ranges from 0.14 to 0.51. The survival ratio for both <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{th}}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{on}}$</tex> exceeds 90%. This study is the first to demonstrate that “S-Cut” is a promising integration pathway for oxide-based multi-tier DRAM architectures on a 12-inch manufacturing line.

title: Ensemble based Driver Behaviour Analysis and Accident Mitigation using CNN and LSTM
authors: Mohan S T, Sreekanth Rallapalli
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The proposed system is a new method for improving road safety by launching intelligent accident mitigation on smart highways that are deep learning models, specifically Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, as those are among the most effective tools. Thus, the system here has taken it one step further by combining visual data with the temporal deep learning behavioral patterns of the drivers to be able to detect and interpret the possible hazardous driving behavior accurately and in the course of time. The CNN module is employed to gather significant spatial features from the video or the images of the driver's face or the scenes of the environment part that is consistent with the signal of facial expression, the eye movement, or even the traffic signs around. These features, which are continuously updated, are further processed with LSTM that is best suiting in the recognition of time-dependent patterns of the driver. LSTM then analyzes the driver's actions and recognizes the instances of fatigue, distraction, or aggressive behavior if any. The ensemble of CNN and LSTM is a hybrid framework that makes it possible for the system to digest comprehensively the behavioral part of its input in an anticipatory mode to enable it to forecast the accidents that are going to happen before running into them. The method is quite new, but it is still a major input to the field of intelligent transportation because it is actively engaged in the early detection of accidents and responses to emergencies hence cutting down the number of accident occurrences and boosting the overall safety and efficiency of highway systems.

title: An EfficientNetV2B3-Based Deep Learning Model for Accurate Diagnosis and Classification of Liver Tumors
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Diagnosis of liver tumor is critical in timely intervention and better patient outcomes. Here, a deep learning model named EfficientNetV2B3 was created to help conduct automated diagnosis and classification of liver tumors based on medical imaging data. The model was trained and fine-tuned on a labelled liver image dataset and its performance was examined based on important metrics, which included accuracy, loss, ROC curve, and confusion matrix analysis. The ROC-AUC was 0.53, which implied low discriminative capacity, and the training and validation accuracy was close to 50, which hinted at possible underfitting. The loss analysis indicated that there were slight overfitting tendencies and the confusion matrix indicated a bias in prediction towards a single class, which could be attributed to the imbalance in the data. Although the results were modest, the research demonstrates the potential of the EfficientNetV2B3 architecture on the medical image classification task and the significance of the data augmentation and hyperparameter optimization to improve the accuracy of the diagnosis in the further studies.

title: Design, Modeling and High-Performance MTPA Control of an Oil-Submersible PMSM
authors: Yunlong Peng, Tingting An, Liangfang Zhang, Yanliang Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the design, modeling, and high-performance maximum torque per ampere (MTPA) control of a unit Oil-submersible permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) for electric submersible pump systems. First, the stator and rotor topologies, pole-slot combination, stator slot geometry, and permanent magnet materials of the oil-submersible motor are studied, based on which the design scheme of the unit motor is developed and the corresponding motor parameters are presented. Then, considering the operating characteristics of the oilsubmersible motor, an improved nonlinear mathematical model is established in which the inductance and flux-linkage parameters are influenced by core saturation and cross-coupling effects. Finally, a simulation study on the high-performance MTPA control method is carried out by implementing the improved motor model in simulation software, thereby verifying the effectiveness of both the motor modeling approach and the highperformance MTPA control method.

title: An Intelligent Home Interior Design Suggestion System Using Deep Learning
authors: Sunitha M R, Shreeya D, Druthi Jain S C, Shimsha K P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This electronic document is a "live" template and already defines the components of your paper [title, text, heads, etc.] in its style sheet. *CRITICAL: Do Not Use Symbols, Special Characters, Footnotes, or Math in Paper Title or Abstract. (Abstract) The increasing demand for personalized home interiors has exposed the limitations of traditional design recommendation approaches. This paper presents An Intelligent home interior design suggestion system that employs deep learning to generate context-aware and personalized design recommendations. The proposed system analyses room images and user preferences using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for style recognition and feature extraction, along with a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for realistic design visualization. The system performs room segmentation, furniture identification, and spatial analysis to recommend suitable colour palettes, furniture arrangements, and layout optimizations. A modular web architecture enables seamless interaction and real-time visualization. The system enhances design accuracy, accessibility, and decision-making while reducing dependence on professional interior designers.

title: Detection of the Upper Surface Pose of Yarn Cones for Robot Gripping
authors: Jian He, Yujie Chen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenge of accurately measuring the center position of the spindle during robotic picking operations, this paper proposes a 3D vision-based algorithm for detecting the upper surface of yarn cones. This algorithm can accurately estimate the center position and orientation of the spindle, thereby guiding the robotic arm to insert it and complete the picking task.First, based on the image data captured by the 3D camera, the circular contour of the top surface of the yarn cone is extracted and optimized. Next, the least squares method is used to fit this circular contour, yielding a mathematical expression for the top surface's circular contour. Building on this, a pose estimation model is constructed to calculate the normal vector of the circle's center and the spatial center coordinates of the top surface using local point cloud data. The experimental results demonstrate that the detection model proposed in this paper exhibits good robustness, and its detection accuracy meets operational requirements: the average detection error for the yarn cone's axis vector is less than 1.3°, and the average detection error for the distance from the center of the upper end face to the axis is less than 1.24 mm, ensuring that the robot can reliably perform yarn cone grasping operations. Importantly, this model can detect yarn cones in any orientation with greater efficiency than point cloud processing; it not only offers a new perspective on yarn cone center detection but also provides valuable insights for related object detection.

title: Section-Weighted Hybrid Approach for Legal Case Retrieval
authors: Rajith Arulanandam, Nisansa De Silva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Finding truly analogous precedents requires capturing legal reasoning beyond surface word overlap. We present a two-stage, section-aware framework for legal case retrieval that first segments raw judgments into facts, issues, decision, and reasoning using a deterministic large language model (LLM) offline. In Stage 1, we combine parallel lexical (BM25) and semantic (dense ANN) whole-document searches via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to form a high-recall candidate pool. In Stage 2, we perform fine-grained, like-for-like comparisons (e.g., query reasoning vs. candidate reasoning). To address the scale mismatch between unbounded lexical scores and cosine similarities, we apply query-wise Z-score normalization before aggregating signals with learned section weights. For the top results, the system returns the relevant section text with a concise, grounded rationale and party-stance labels. We evaluate on a jurisdiction-scale benchmark, demonstrating consistent gains over strong lexical and neural baselines while maintaining high candidate coverage.

title: Best First Search based Control of Residential Appliance Load Demand
authors: Anoohya Runkana, Kandala Prapthi, Nagaraja Chitimi M., Rashmi M. R., Hoong Pin Lee, Rachana M. Hullamani
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The increasing use of rooftop photovoltaic (PV) cells in residential buildings requires effective demand management to utilise the available power effectively. This paper presents a household appliance load demand control method based on the Best First Search (BFS) algorithm, implemented in MATLAB. The proposed approach schedules household appliances by considering the available <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> power, demand by the appliances, and user-defined priority levels.
