title: Removal of Lamotrigine From Wastewater Using Activated Carbon Derived From Ziziphus Jujube Leaves
authors: Mohammed Alyami, Mohammed Benaafi, Tauqir Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Pharmaceutical pollutants such as lamotrigine pose significant environmental risks due to their persistence in wastewater and resistance to conventional treatment methods. In this study, we investigated the sustainable removal of lamotrigine using activated carbon (AC) derived from locally sourced biomass (Ziziphus Jujube). Green leaves of Ziziphus Jujube were collected from Dhahran, Saudi Arabia, washed with deionized water, ovendried at 100° C for 24 h, ground, and sieved through a 100-mesh screen. The biomass was chemically activated with NaHCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> at a 1: 4(w/w) ratio, then pyrolyzed at 1073 K for 3 h under nitrogen. The resulting product was washed with 0.1 mol/L HCl and DI water and dried at 343 K for 24 h to yield the final adsorbent, labelled as (ZJ) AC, with a pyrolysis yield of approximately 18%. Batch adsorption experiments demonstrated the high efficiency of developed AC for lamotrigine removal, highlighting its potential as a sustainable, low-cost adsorbent for advanced water treatment applications.

title: Design and Optimization of an Instruction-Level Reconfigurable FPGA-Based Low-Power DNN Accelerator
authors: Tao Zhuang, Ke Ma, Shinichi Nishizawa, Shinji Kimura
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Efficient Deep Neural Network (DNN) acceleration on edge platforms faces the dual challenges of the memory wall and the strict power constraints. This work introduces a low-power FPGA accelerator that synergizes a channel-parallel data layout with an instruction-level reconfigurable architecture. The proposed design enforces a strict alignment between the external memory format and the internal buffering mechanism for supplying data to the operational unit. By coupling offline channel-parallel reordering with on-chip FIFO-based streaming, we can eliminate complex runtime addressing logic and use efficient burst DMA access of the AXI bus. In addition, the fine-grained instruction-level reconfigurable mechanism enables flexible configuration of diverse convolution operations with minimal hardware cost. The proposed accelerator is implemented on Zynq-7100 FPGA, and achieves improved throughput and energy efficiency for a lightweight model YOLO-v3-tiny, compared with existing FPGA-based designs.

title: End-To-End Hyperbolic Graph Neural Networks for Brain Age Prediction with MEG Data
authors: John Girgis, Mengjia Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disease that affects a large population. This disease drives a devastating rapid decline of brain functionality in the human brain. Identifying AD early is essential to effective treatment. Magneticoencephalography (MEG) is an effective tool for identifying changes in normal brain aging trajectories. In this study, we created an end-to-end Hyperbolic Graph Convolutional Neural Network (HGCN) that is able to learn the subtle hierarchical representations from training data to achieve high accuracy on unseen data. We found that the HGCN model outperformed the regular GCN model and other classic classification models in all classification metrics, and in both binary and multiclass classification tasks. We also found that our MEG data were best represented with a relatively large negative hyperbolic curvature. In our embedding visualizations, we motivated an intuition explaining why hyperbolic space is able to better fit hierarchical data. This model can be used for both future research and clinical applications to help predict and prevent neurogenerative disorders before they progress. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/astarryknight/hgcn_e2e

title: AFRICON 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON

title: Design Considerations for High Performance Vertical Channel DRAM Cell Array Transistor
authors: Moonyoung Jeong, Kilho Yu, Yootak Jun, Jae-Hyun Choi, Seungwoo Park, Junsoo Kim, Jeonghoon Oh, Segeun Park, Jaihyuk Song
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: This paper address challenges in designing next generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 F}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> DRAM cell array transistor. A comprehensive study was conducted on the junction extension coupling effects on the device characteristics of cell array transistors having ultrathin body. Dual work function word line and raised back gate as a junction extension shield were introduced as the most feasible solutions to cope with the junction extension coupling effects. Performance gain by these architectures allows the junction extension to be designed in a way more resistant to the coupling effects.

title: Deep Cross-modal Fusion by Attention for Proxy Hashing
authors: Guo Shu, Baohua Qiang, Wenhui Zhang, Ruidong Chen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Cross-modal hashing retrieval serves as a fundamental task for connecting disparate modal data (e.g., image and text), presenting significant application value and challenges for large-scale multimedia retrieval. Existing deep cross-modal hashing methods typically focus on learning independent deep representations for each modality, aligning them only during the final hash code generation stage. This approach overlooks the importance of feature interactions at intermediate layers, leading to the loss of fine-grained semantic information. To address this problem, we propose a Deep Cross-modal Fusion by Attention for Proxy Hashing (DCFPH), aiming to achieve dynamic and refined alignment between image and text modalities at the intermediate feature layer. This module adaptively captures salient semantic associations between modalities, enhancing the discriminative power of feature representations. Extensive experiments on public datasets MIRFLICKR-25K, NUS-WIDE, and MS COCO demonstrate that our method achieves higher retrieval accuracy compared to existing mainstream cross-modal hashing models.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a Coplanar Antenna at Millimeter-Wave Frequencies 39-66 GHz
authors: Subbarayudu B, Patnam Noor Mahammad, B. Narasimha Reddy, Kathem Manohar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In this paper, a design and analysis of a compact wideband planar antenna with a patterned ground for high frequency operations is proposed. In this design, a standard radiating patch with a feeding point at its center and a periodic hexagonal-shaped ground plane is utilized. The designed antenna operates effectively in a wide frequency bandwidth encompassing even higher frequencies in the millimeter-wave region. From the simulation, it can be seen that there is good impedance matching, with the reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}$</tex>) values below -10 dB for the major part of the operating frequencies. Additionally, there are multiple spots of resonance, ensuring good excitation of the radiation part of the design because of the coupled effects of the radiator and the design in the ground plane. Also, there is improvement in the gain plot with positive peak values recorded, especially in the mid-band range of the frequencies. Even in the higher frequencies, the design still performs acceptably. Thus, there is degradation in the gain in the end regions of the spectrum. The ground hexagonal configuration significantly contributes to the mitigation of surface currents and the enhancement of radiation efficiency. This yields greater gain and bandwidth capabilities than what could be achieved with the conventional solid ground plane. As a result of its compact profile and versatility in operating within a wide frequency band and with satisfactory gain properties, the proposed antenna is promising and suitable for usage in future wireless applications.

title: Measuring the Complexity of Sub-Policies in Online Terms of Service Networks
authors: Rishabh Garg, Ziheng Huang, Hari Sundaram
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Online Terms of Service (ToS) agreements are often spread across a vast network of interconnected documents, making it difficult for users to give informed consent. While prior research has focused on the complex language of individual policy documents, this study investigates the structural complexity that arises from network-based presentation of these documents. Using a custom data-extraction pipeline, we constructed and analyzed the ToS networks for 16 major online services. To synthesize the structural factors, we introduce a composite complexity score that models both document volume and navigational efficiency. Our findings reveal that the sheer volume of documents (nodes) is the most significant barrier to user comprehension. This structural problem is compounded by a second barrier, the use of dense, college-level language across almost all services.

title: Can I Park Here? Understanding Complicated Parking Signs on the Street
authors: Junlai Shi, Yuankai He, Yuxin Wang, Weisong Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Parking is an important component of urban traffic management, and accurate recognition of parking sign rules is essential for intelligent parking. Although computer vision has advanced in traffic sign recognition, parking signs differ as they often include free-form text, symbols, and stacked formats, making understanding more complex. Current research on parking signs primarily focuses on signs detection, while studies on rule understanding remain insufficient. To address this problem, we propose a parking rule checking (PaRC) service designed to understand complicated parking signs and support parking decision making. PaRC is an on-device hybrid framework with (1) a YOLOv11n-based Signs Detection (SD) module to extract parking sign regions, (2) a fine-tuned Donut–driven Rule Parsing (RP) module to parse structured rules, and (3) an interpretable program-based Decision Making (DM) module to produce the final parking decision. We construct datasets by consolidating multiple U.S. street-view parking sign collections. PaRC achieves an F1-score of 96.15% with an inference time of 0.90 s, surpassing on-device models in speed while maintaining accuracy comparable to that of LVLMs. Most remaining errors arise from severely blurred signs that even humans struggle to recognize, and PaRC also performs well on low-end devices.

title: Self-Supervised Learning of Behavioral User Representations for Authorship Similarity Analysis
authors: Saba Aslanzadeh, Anu G. Bourgeois, Mario M. Kubek
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Identifying behaviorally similar users on large messaging platforms is challenging due to the absence of groundtruth labels. We study authorship similarity on Telegram by learning user representations from message content, temporal activity, and interaction context. Users are modeled as nodes in a graph constructed from reply and co-occurrence relationships and represented by multi-modal behavioral features. We train an inductive GraphSAGE encoder using self-supervised contrastive learning with stochastic graph and feature perturbations. Evaluation using a synthetic same-author protocol on held-out users yields a ROC-AUC of 0.92 and Average Precision of 0.94, and unsupervised clustering reveals coherent behavioral groups.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings Based on 1D-CNN
authors: Man Qiu, Kulevome Delanyo, Qian Wang, Wanzhen Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Bearings are one of the common key components in rotating machinery, and their faults can lead to equipment shutdown and production losses. Traditional methods, which combine feature extraction and classifiers, have problems such as the subjectivity of feature selection and the complexity of feature extraction. To address this, a rolling bearing fault diagnosis strategy based on a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture is proposed. Using the open-source dataset from Southeast University, normalization technology is adopted for feature extraction, and the CNN is used to train the processed data. By adjusting the model parameters, automatic fault identification and diagnosis are realized. Tests show that this method has an accuracy rate of 99.75%, exhibiting better performance and robustness.

title: Cryogenic Reliability Physics of 28nm HKMG nMOSFETs: Insights from A ΔDIBL Model and Gated-Diode Measurement
authors: Pin-Jie Pu, Jia Zhe Ao, An-Yao Hsu, E Ray Hsieh, Chien-Nan Kuo, Pei-Wen Li
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive characterization of 28nm HKMG nMOSFETs operating at cryogenic temperatures ranging from 300K down to 4.5K. The research investigates the impact of Hot Carrier stress on key device parameters, including Vth, S.S., G<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>, and DIBL. The experimental results reveal that while cryogenic operation enhances carrier mobility, it also exacerbates reliability concerns such as interface trap generation N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</inf>. A physical model based on geometric approximations is proposed to correlate the degradation of DIBL with S.S., showing excellent agreement with experimental data. Furthermore, Gated Diode measurements confirm the significant generation of interface states, providing physical insight into the degradation mechanisms observed over stress time.

title: Effective Atrial Fibrillation Recognition by Wearable ECGs using Gradient Boosting Mechanism
authors: Ch Venkata S S P Kumar, Chillale Bhargav, Kalaichelvi K, Anil Kumar Singh, Kaviarasu K, Sangeetha S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: This study focuses on real-time identification of atrial fibrillation (AF) using ECG signals with a Gradient Boosting Algorithm. The aim is to improve AF detection on the accuracy and reliability scale during real-time continuous monitoring of the heart. The ECG devices capture signals of high dimensions and noisy levels. Thus, signals need to be processed with noise filtering and signal normalization to capture some extent of temporal and morphological traits. The features that the Gradient Boosting model has trained on will be able to classify normal vs. abnormal heart rhythms with added precision. Progress is made all the time on the model training as the Gradient Boosting Ensemble learns to reduce classification. The proposed model focuses on the continuous wear ECGs selfmonitoring to stream, making the system easier to manage in self-monitoring of AF. The model focuses on the continuous wear self-monitoring system, making the AF detection easier to manage. With the ability to focus on rapid system selfmonitoring, the model allows the development of highperformance AF detection. With continuous ECG selfmonitoring, the model allows stream detection. The proposed study focuses on detection stream-efficient systems.

title: Cardiovascular Risk Prediction using Retinal Eye Images
authors: VimalaDevi R, Prathibha A, Sireesha Bai K, Swathi A, Vaishnavi R, Varsha D
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Heart attacks remain a significant global health issue. This makes early detection and prevention very important. The retina provides a non-invasive method to understand a person’s heart health because the blood vessels in the eye reflect changes that also happen in the heart. In this work, we introduce a deep-learning method in the case of calculating the heart attack risk, all the symptoms can be detected. In our research, we used the convolutional neural network to automatically learn key features from the retinal images, such as vessel patterns and structural changes, linked to cardiovascular risk. We use these learned features to classify individuals as low-risk or high-risk. This method eliminates the need for painful or invasive medical tests and makes screening faster, easier, and more affordable. Our experimental results show that the model achieves strong accuracy and consistency on standard retinal datasets. The findings indicate that retinal imaging, combined with AI, can play an important role for early heart attack risk prediction and preventive healthcare.

title: Multi-Band Noise-Adaptive DEMON with SNR-Weighted Fusion for Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Jagadish S Jakati, S. S. Ittannavar, Raju Hebbale, Mahadevaswamy S, B P Pradeep Kumar, Bhavaling Alias Pratima Khot
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Multi-Band Noise-Adaptive DEMON with SNR-Weighted Fusion for Bearing Fault Diagnosis addresses the inherent sensitivity of classical DEMON to manual band selection and band-dependent noise. The objective of this work is to design a machine-agnostic demodulation framework that preserves fault-induced impulsiveness while remaining robust across heterogeneous spectral conditions. To this end, the vibration signal is decomposed into multiple spectrally diverse sub-bands, each processed through envelope demodulation. A noise-aware fusion mechanism is then introduced, where band-wise contributions are adaptively weighted using a detection signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) criterion, emphasizing diagnostically dominant channels and suppressing noise-dominated responses. Experiments conducted on the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing dataset employ group-wise statistics, window-wise trend analysis, qualitative waveform inspection, and ablation studies. The results demonstrate that the proposed method preserves the physical characteristics of bearing faults, yielding significantly higher impulsiveness in defective samples (kurtosis ≈ 5–7) compared to healthy conditions (≈ 2.6–2.8), while producing smoother and more stable diagnostic trajectories across time windows. Compared with single-band DEMON and equal-weight multi-band fusion, the adaptive strategy consistently improves robustness without degrading performance on clean benchmark data. These findings confirm that noise-adaptive fusion eliminates the brittleness of manual band selection and establishes a principled, interpretable foundation for scalable fault monitoring in both benchmark and industrial environments.

title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Toxic Comment Classification: A Systematic Review
authors: Jasmin Princy Lobo, Harshitha G P, H Shama Jain, Greeshma
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The rapid growth in social media has enriched the capacity for global communication. It has also, however, given way to the growth of toxic comments in the form of hate speech, cyberbullying, harassment, and abusive language. There are severe psychological and social effects due to toxic comments, hence explaining why there is a need for accurate detection automatically. The automatic classification of toxic comments has been able to attain notable results in recent advances. This review paper, therefore, has deeply reflected on works already published and research studies pertaining to traditional machine learning approaches and recent developments concerning the classification of toxic comments using deep learning. All research studies examined were evaluated based on inherently shared data, text handling, attribute extraction, and classification approaches, together with their various performance measures. This review paper, however, covers the pros and cons of different approaches too and presents some major challenges in this field. The role of benchmark datasets and their annotations will be discussed regarding its effect on models' reliability and generalization ability. Other open challenges regarding multilingual toxic comment detection, interpretability, and real-time deployment are also underlined.

title: Example Guided Prompt Tuning for Sentiment Analysis of African Languages on Llama
authors: Amir Ince, Howard Prioleau, Saurav K. Aryal
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: With the rise of social media, vast amounts of text are being generated, presenting unique linguistic challenges for sentiment analysis, particularly for underrepresented African languages. This study explores how existing models perform without fine-tuning to understand the challenges of analyzing multilingual datasets containing monolingual examples. We propose a prompt-tuning approach based on generated versus human-labeled multilingual datasets. Our results show that the Few-shot technique and the Prompt Optimization Framework with Dataset Examples offer the most consistent performance, highlighting the importance of real-world examples and language-specific data in improving multilingual sentiment analysis. However, the studied models and technique do not exhibit the ability to accurately triage sentiments for African languages.

title: Detection of Illicit Content on Online Marketplaces using Large Language Models
authors: Quoc Khoa Tran, Thanh Thi Nguyen, Campbell Wilson
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Online marketplaces, while revolutionizing global commerce, have inadvertently facilitated the proliferation of illicit activities, including drug trafficking, counterfeit sales, and cybercrimes. Traditional content moderation methods such as manual reviews and rule-based automated systems struggle with scalability, dynamic obfuscation techniques, and multilingual content. Conventional machine learning models, though effective in simpler contexts, often falter when confronting the semantic complexities and linguistic nuances characteristic of illicit marketplace communications. This research investigates the efficacy of Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically Meta’s Llama 3.2 and Google’s Gemma 3, in detecting and classifying illicit online marketplace content using the multilingual DUTA10K dataset. Employing fine-tuning techniques such as Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) and quantization, these models were systematically benchmarked against a foundational transformerbased model (BERT) and traditional machine learning baselines (Support Vector Machines and Naive Bayes). Experimental results reveal a task-dependent advantage for LLMs. In binary classification (illicit vs. non-illicit), Llama 3.2 demonstrated performance comparable to traditional methods. However, for complex, imbalanced multi-class classification involving 40 specific illicit categories, Llama 3.2 significantly surpassed all baseline models. These findings offer substantial practical implications for enhancing online safety, equipping law enforcement agencies, e-commerce platforms, and cybersecurity specialists with more effective, scalable, and adaptive tools for illicit content detection and moderation.

title: Towards System-2 AI: Workloads and Characterizations of Energy-Based Models
authors: Hanchen Yang, Jiayi Qian, Zishen Wan, Jingtian Dang, Ziwei Li, Yilun Du, Tushar Krishna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: The rapid progress of artificial intelligence (AI) has been largely driven by deep neural networks, yet their insufficient compositional reasoning abilities, limited robustness, and lack of explainability expose fundamental limitations for next-generation cognitive systems. Energy-Based Models (EBMs), adhering to System-2-style thinking, have recently shown remarkable performance across generative, compositional, and reasoning tasks, offering a pathway toward more robust and cognitively grounded AI. However, the system behavior of EBMs remains poorly understood. Their long sampling loops, strict execution data dependencies, model compositionality and heterogeneity make them highly inefficient on off-the-shelf hardware, creating significant barriers for scalable and real-time deployment. In this paper, we present the first comprehensive workload characterization and system study of EBMs. We first introduce a taxonomy that unifies all the diverse EBM algorithms (around twenty), then formalize their end-to-end runtime to expose key optimization opportunities, and experimentally profile representative models across CPU, GPU, and TPU platforms to understand their runtime breakdowns, memory behavior, computational operators, roofline model, algorithmic performance, and MCMC (Markov Chain Monte Carlo) sampling impacts. Our analysis reveals fundamental system bottlenecks unique to EBMs, including highly repetitive forward and backward neural operations, long and inefficient sampling trajectories, and severe hardware under-utilization caused by heterogeneous model components and operations. Furthermore, based on the experiments and analysis, we suggest optimization solutions across the algorithm, system and architecture levels, to improve EBM computing performance, efficiency, and scalability, aiming to pinpoint the challenges and future directions of EBM research.

title: Statistical Assessment of Soil Heat Flux Variability Using Time-Series Data
authors: Arpitha K, B Uma Maheswari, Saravanan S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The electronic document is a “live” template and already Soil Heat Flux (SHF) is an essential part of the landatmosphere energy balance and provides useful information on the nature of the subsurface thermal processes related to the evolution of droughts. This paper includes a comprehensive statistical analysis and time-series prediction model of soil heat flux based on long-term measurements of the National Ecological Observatory Network. Data on monthly SHF were pre-processed with the help of quality control, resampling, and stationarity analysis based on Augmented Dickey-Fuller tests and KPSS tests. Various classical and hybrid forecasting models were performed with ARIMA, SARIMA, Holt-Winters exponential smoothing, Prophet, Seasonal-Trend breakdown with ARIMA (STL-ARIMA), and Seasonal-Trend breakdown with Exponential Smoothing (STL-ETS). The evaluation of models' performance was based on a comprehensive list of error and productivity measures. STL-ARIMA showed the best performance with MAE (2.676), RMSE (3.323), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{0. 4 6 1 9}$</tex>), Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency(0.4619), Theil's-U-statistic(0.4218).

title: Detection of AI Generated Audio Using Deep Learning and Signal Processing Techniques
authors: Rohan A Gajare, Sandeep Kumar Hegde, Rajalaxmi Hegde
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: As generative artificial intelligence (AI) generates human sounding audio through speech synthesis/voice cloning, the advances made have increased the chances of deep fake impersonations, misinformation, and fraud. To aid in the detection of human speech versus AI generated speech, the application proposed will use deep learning combined with the processes of signal processing, spectral processing, and selfsupervision to detect differences. The Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and the Transformer architecture will apply Wav2Vec2.0 and HuBERT to facilitate fact finding in human audio. The system is trained on the dataset which includes both real audio samples as well as AI generated audio samples (combined total approximately 500 hours), such as those provided by Tacotron, Wavenet, VALL-E, Diffusion models, and more. The features examined, use MFCC, spectrogram, and Pitch to identify features representative of generative artifacts. The model will perform well based on Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, ROC-AUC metrics. As of now, it is being developed as a lightweight Real-Time Web and Mobile prototype developed on TensorFlow Lite & ONNX, providing a scalable approach to detecting AI generated audio; thereby, improving Trust & Authenticity and promoting Cyber Security in digital communications

title: QUEST: A Multi-Metric Framework for Analyzing Consensus in LLM Outputs
authors: Robin Nicolay, Felix Gratzkowski, Sebastian Bader, Thomas Kirste
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: The growing availability of decoder-based Large Language Models (LLMs), both locally hosted and APIaccessible, has intensified the need for systematic comparison of their outputs. However, existing evaluation tools often fail to capture both surface-level and semantic variations across models or to assess answer consistency. We introduce Quest, a web-based evaluation framework that enables a side-by-side comparison of decoder-based LLMs using multi-metric similarity and clustering-based consensus scoring. It analyzes responses using syntactic (Levenshtein distance, Jaccard similarity) and semantic (cosine similarity over embeddings) metrics. Quest further quantifies inter-model consensus through clustering and coherence measures, revealing agreement structures among models. Designed for flexibility and transparency, Quest supports API models and local inference via Ollama, and is implemented in Node.js and React. Our evaluations across medical questions demonstrate that high inter-model agreement can serve as a proxy for output plausibility, while disagreement highlights epistemic uncertainty or model-specific reasoning artifacts. We present a use case and discuss Quest’s value for quality assurance, and safety monitoring in sensitive application domains such as clinical NLP, and propose a collection of measures to quantify consensus between models.

title: High-RA STT-MRAM for In-Memory Computing: From Device Engineering to Prototype-IMC Validation
authors: Ming-Chun Hong, Hsin-Han Lee, Guan-Long Chen, Sin-You Huang, Chiao-Yun Lo, Yi-Hui Su, Ho-Lin Tsai, Shih-Ching Chiu, Cheng-Yi Shih, Yao-Jen Chang, Chih-Yao Wang, Shan-Yi Yang, Yu-Chen Hsin, Jeng-Hua Wei, Shyh-Shyuan Sheu, Wei-Chung Lo, Shih-Chieh Chang, Tuo-Hung Hou
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In contrast to the low-RA STT-MRAM for memory applications, high-RA (> 500 Ω•μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) STT-MRAM with high cell resistance (> 250 kΩ) is developed to support future energy-efficient in-memory computing (IMC). Scaling coercive magnetic field and cell dimensions together with the increase of MgO thickness are critical for enlarging RA and write margin. Unlike SOT-MRAM, IMC based on the high-RA STT-MRAM achieves high energy efficiency, excellent reliability, and robust immunity to variability and circuit non-ideality without compromising the advantage in density and technology maturity.

title:
Dual-Function Surface Topology: Optimizing Ohmic Contacts and Channel Mobility via Cycle-Controlled PEALD Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    on 20nm Si Nanosheet
authors: Bi-Xian Wu, Yi-Hsiang Chen, Heng-Jui Chang, Chi-Tai Hsieh, Tzu-Hsuan Chang
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Carrier modulation through conventional doping often suffers from carrier mobility degradation due to increased scattering. To address this issue, an approach utilizing plasma-enhanced atomic layer deposition (PEALD) to grow sub-nanometer polarized dielectric layer of Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> on Si-on-Insulator (SOI) substrate is presented. Specifically, a 3-cycle Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> interlayer is applied at the contact interface, combined with a 50-cycle Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> deposition on top of the Si nanosheet channel. This heterostructure effectively induces a Two-Dimensional Electron Gas (2DEG), resulting in a 15-fold improvement in sheet resistance and a 2.5-fold reduction in Ohmic contact resistance with improved carrier mobility up to 400 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/Vs. The low-temperature nature of the PEALD process ensures the compatibility with advanced silicon technologies, such as FinFETs and GAAFETs, and facilitates integration with the Back-End-of-Line (BEOL) processes.

title: Compiler and System Optimizations for Gem5 Simulator
authors: Haneul Park, Siddharth Agarwal, Pradyun Narkadamilli, Kiung Jung, Yongjun Park, Ipoom Jeong, Nam Sung Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Architectural simulators are indispensable for modern computer architecture research, but they remain notoriously slow due to their event-driven, cycle-level execution model. In this work, we present a set of software- and system-level optimizations to accelerate large-scale design-space exploration with gem5. First, we reduce per-instance simulation time via compiler-level optimization. We demonstrate that although gem5 suffers severe frontend stalls on modern CPUs stemming from its large instruction footprints, naïve Profile-Guided Optimization (PGO) is impractical in this setting because it requires frequent reprofiling and recompilation. To address this, we challenge the conventional reliance on self-profiling and instead construct a universal, performance-driven profile that generalizes across simulation inputs. Second, we improve aggregate simulation throughput by strengthening performance isolation using Sub-NUMA clustering (SNC). Finally, we show that a simple co-scheduling heuristic has great potential for reducing resource stranding and boosting multi-instance efficiency. Together, these techniques improve single simulation speed by 17 % and aggregate throughput by $27 \%$, making large-scale design-space exploration more practical and efficient.

title: Differentiable Sensitivity-Based Skew Scheduling Framework for Timing Optimization
authors: Hsin-Tzu Chang, Wen-Hao Liu, Yi-Chen Lu, Anand Rajaram, Iris Hui-Ru Jiang, Haoxing Ren
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: As chip design complexity continues to grow, timing closure becomes increasingly challenging. Resolving timing violations is essential for reliable system performance but often demands considerable design effort. Useful-skew optimization is commonly adopted to address these challenges by adjusting clock latencies. However, conventional methods, typically applied after clock tree synthesis (CTS), restrict the solution space, leading to suboptimal timing outcomes. To overcome these limitations, we incorporate differentiable sensitivity analysis into the optimization flow, enabling precise and effective timing adjustments. Our proposed framework uses gradient descent driven by timing sensitivity to refine clock latencies, achieving a 22.2% reduction in worst negative slack (WNS) and a 43.1% reduction in total negative slack (TNS) compared to a commercial baseline. These results confirm that our method delivers significant timing improvements in large-scale integrated circuit designs with minimal runtime overhead.

title: Hybrid Deep Ensemble Learning with Metaheuristic Optimization for Heart Disease Prediction
authors: B Shamreen Ahamed, Bal Virdee, Ashish Khanna, R G Vidhya, S Sivashankar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The major causes of deaths due to heart disease rank among the top for global health concerns, with predicting heart disease for diabetic patients a major challenge. This research study proposes a deep ensemble learning with metaheuristic optimization framework for heart disease prediction. The workflow of this model includes performing extensive preprocessing of the provided dataset, followed by an optimization algorithm-based feature selection. Furthermore, the proposed model utilizes a weighted fusion method for combining various health data sources, convolution-pooling, attention, and spatial-temporal learning. Ensemble stacking learning is also used with an SVM learner, with Pufferfish Optimization Algorithm used for fine-tuning. As indicated, the proposed model was successful in achieving 0.980 accuracy, 0.970 precision, 0.990 recall, and 0.950 F1score for Dataset 1, with 0.990 accuracy, 0.980 precision, 0.970 recall, and 0.980 F1-score for the Cleveland dataset.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Oil Well Working Conditions Based on Dual-Modal Feature Weighted Fusion
authors: Junchuan Xu, Ying Wang, Junhe Zhang, Tong Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In traditional oil well condition monitoring methods, the accuracy of diagnostic models is reduced due to the low discrimination of some working condition features extracted based on dynamometer cards or electrical power parameters. To address this issue, a dual-modal feature fusion-based oil well condition diagnosis method is proposed. Firstly, the dynamometer card and the corresponding electrical power parameters are selected as dual-modal inputs, and the ResNet18 model is used to extract features from them respectively. Secondly, a confidence weighting module is designed to perform confidence weighting on the extracted dynamometer card and electrical power features, and a multi-head attention mechanism is adopted to extract the fused features of the dynamometer card and electrical power, aiming to capture the inter-modal relationship information and enhance the feature expression ability. Then, the inter-modal relationship features output by the multi-head attention, the original superimposed features, and the weighted dynamometer card and electrical power features are concatenated in the channel dimension, and a channel attention mechanism is used for channel weighting. Finally, the fused and weighted features are recognized by a CNN.

title: Uplift Pricing for Inertia and Reserve via ML-Based Frequency-Constrained Unit Commitment
authors: Azeez O. Olasoji, David T. O Oyedokun, Mohammad Rajabdorri, Juan Esteban Sierra-Aguillar, Mkhutazi Mditshwa, Chukwuemeka E. Okafor, Best Khoza, Komla A. Folly
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: High penetration of inverter-based sources makes power systems susceptible to frequency excursions, leaving power systems short of synchronous inertia and uncompensated spinning reserve headroom. Traditional unit commitment (UC) approaches are incapable of catering to the needs of modern power systems. This paper proposes a transparent, regulator-friendly remedy that requires no real-time market redesign. A linear logistic regression surrogate, trained on 117 000 dynamic simulations, is embedded in the MILP to enforce post-fault frequency constraint. Spinning reserve headroom is remunerated through a fixed uplift tariff proportional to each generator’s marginal energy cost. Three deterministic day-ahead scenarios are compared on a real power system—La Palma (Spain): S0—reserve free; S1—tariff applied ex-post; S2—tariff co-optimised with energy. Co-optimisation (S2) increases weekly expenditure only 4.2 % relative to the cost-only baseline and is 0.2 % cheaper than the ex-post variant (S1). Fuel (operation) cost and renewable curtailment remain unchanged, depicting that the tariff does not distort the merit order. Although worst-case system inertia drops by 9 MW•s, the nadir limit binds 75 h/wk<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> versus 59 h/wk<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> in S0/S1, impeding under-frequency risk without raising RoCoF exposure. Reserve payments become less concentrated; the Gini coefficient, a measure of inequality, falls from 0.26 to 0.24, and the share captured by the three highest-earning units drops slightly to 56%. These results demonstrate that a single co-optimised uplift tariff—underpinned by an ML-based nadir constraint—thus delivers frequency security and fair cost recovery at negligible economic and operational impact, offering an immediately deployable solution for low-inertia grids.

title: The Radiation Performance and Production Flows of Renesas's Radiation Tolerant and Radiation Hardened Space Plastic Parts
authors: W. H. Newman, M. Campanella, J. Brewster, E. J. Thomson
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: We describe the production flows of Renesas's ISL71xxxM/SLHM families of radiation-tolerant and radiation-hardened plastic-package integrated circuits and report some example single event and TID results.

title: Design and Control of 5DOF Tiltrotor Quadcopter Aerial Operation Platform
authors: Zhongyu Yao, Jin Yu, Wusheng Chou
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Standard multi-rotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) with parallel rotor planes can only generate vertical lift. To move in three-dimensional space, these UAVs only change their pitch-roll angles, which makes them unsuitable for applications in contact or non-contact operation fields that require decoupling of position and attitude control. Although the later developed multirotor UAV with tilted rotor planes and tilt control rotor planes solved this problem, the tilted rotor planes led to a decrease in flight efficiency. This paper proposes a prototype of a 5DOF tiltrotor quadcopter that introduces a tilt control degree of freedom around the pitch axis of the fuselage, enabling decoupling of horizontal forward motion and pitch motion control. Which is used as an air communication node. It maintains the signal's orientation towards the ground by adjusting the pitch and yaw angles during hovering. A backstepping controller and a nonlinear model predictive controller (NMPC) are developed to achieve trajectory tracking, attitude regulation, and control allocation. Simulation results show that the designed tilt-rotor quadcopter can effectively track spatial curves and desired pitch-yaw angles.

title: EmotionSense: A Real-Time Emotion Monitoring and Analysis System Based on Physiological Signals
authors: Theodore Morra, Jordan Murphy, Rui Li
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Real-time emotional monitoring and visualization is an increasingly important topic in the field of human-machine interaction. Most current emotion monitoring studies focus on visual- or speech-based methods. However, due to multiple reasons, these methods can be unstable and unreliable in realworld applications. Compared to visual and speech data, physiological signals offer a more direct and involuntary reflection of emotional states, as they are less influenced by conscious control. However, capturing and interpreting physiological data for emotion analysis in real-time is challenging. To address this issue, this paper presents EmotionSense, a real-time emotion analysis system based on physiological data, which includes realtime signal processing for emotion recognition and a user-friendly emotion visualization interface. For physiological data, the developed system utilizes a wearable sensor to capture key physiological signals including heart rate, electrodermal activity, temperature, motion, and photoplethysmography in real-time. Specifically, this paper introduces (1) the development of a realtime emotion analysis algorithm that maps physiological signals to dimensional emotional arousal-valence-intensity values, discrete emotional states, and human stress levels; (2) the creation of a realtime emotion visualization interface displaying emotion analyzing results for enhancing user engagement and understanding; and (3) evaluation experiments to demonstrate the effectiveness of the developed systems. The results highlight the system's ability in analyzing human emotions in real-time, and guide future improvements for biofeedback systems focused on enhancing human-machine interaction.

title: Distilling Knowledge via Attention Transfer for Efficient Facial Expression Recognition in Classrooms
authors: Shuyan Wang, Mengfan Liu, Zhaorong Ji, Yao Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: With the growing adoption of smart classrooms, real-time analysis of student engagement has gained significance. However, conventional deep expression recognition models are often too computationally intensive for embedded classroom devices. To address this, we propose a lightweight expression classification method using attention-based knowledge distillation. A teacher-student framework is adopted, where ResNet-50 provides supervision, and a modified ResNet-18 student network employs depthwise separable convolution and an SE module to reduce complexity and enhance feature emphasis. An activation attention distillation mechanism further transfers discriminative knowledge by aligning feature map attention. Experiments show the model achieves 75.2 % accuracy on a dataset from FER-2013 and DAiSEE, with only $\mathbf{6. 4 M}$ parameters and $\mathbf{6. 3 m s}$ inference time, outperforming ResNet-18 and MobileNetV2 in balancing accuracy and efficiency for real-time classroom use.

title: Multi-Axis Mechatronic Object Handling System
authors: Kavyashree H N, Bhoomika H M, Ajay M S, Disha K M, Seema S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a Wi-Fi-enabled mobile robot equipped with a robotic arm and controlled through the Blynk IoT mobile application. The ESP32 microcontroller serves as the primary control unit, enabling wireless communication between the user and the robot via the internet. The system consists of a four-wheel mobile platform driven by DC motors and a four-servo robotic arm for object manipulation. User commands entered through a smartphone interface are transmitted to the Blynk cloud and received by the ESP32, which processes the data and generates appropriate control signals for motion and arm positioning. This wireless configuration removes the constraints associated with wired control systems and allows flexible remote operation. The design emphasizes low cost and straightforward implementation, making it suitable for laboratory use, educational projects, and basic industrial training. Experimental results demonstrate reliable communication, accurate motor control, and effective integration of embedded hardware with cloud-based IoT platforms for real-time robotic applications.

title: Alternating 2-Face Path Unfoldings of the Tesseract
authors: Soham Samanta, Hugo A. Akitaya, Erik D. Demaine, Martin L. Demaine
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: We study a special class of 2-face unfoldings of the tesseract (4 D hypercube) where we cut edges of its 2 -skeleton and lay down its 24 square faces on the plane, forming a nonoverlapping polyomino. We explore the family of 2-face unfoldings with the maximum diameter: unfoldings whose dual graph is a path, commonly called path unfoldings. More specifically, we explore the subset of path unfoldings that alternate between visiting faces of the top and bottom cubes of the tesseract, with a single side face in between. We experimentally enumerated all 35,520 2-face path unfoldings in this class. We also implemented an algorithm to test whether a given 24-omino is a path unfolding of the 2 -skeleton of the hypercube. We used this software to design a geometric font by selecting unfoldings that resemble Latin letters and digits, illustrating the intersection of computational geometry and typographic design.

title: Named Entity Recognition in COVID-19 tweets with Entity Knowledge Augmentation
authors: Xuankang Zhang, Jiangming Liu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic causes severe social and economic disruption around the world, raising various subjects that are discussed over social media. Identifying pandemicrelated named entities as expressed on social media is fundamental and important to understand the discussions about the pandemic. However, there is limited work on named entity recognition on this topic due to the following challenges: 1) COVID-19 texts in social media are informal and their annotations are rare and insufficient to train a robust recognition model, and 2) named entity recognition in COVID-19 requires extensive domain-specific knowledge. To address these issues, we propose a novel entity knowledge augmentation approach for COVID-19, which can also be applied in general biomedical named entity recognition in both informal text format and formal text format. Experiments carried out on the COVID-19 tweets dataset and PubMed dataset show that our proposed entity knowledge augmentation improves NER performance in both fully-supervised and few-shot settings.

title: Enhancing Wildlife Conservation Using Deep Learning and Nocturnal Surveillance
authors: Suruthi S, Sathish Kumar T M, Sivasankar Rajamani P, Nishanthini S, Sasidharan K, Balaji S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The real-time wildlife monitoring and intrusion detection system that makes use of the latest advanced deep learning models for identifying the illegal human intrusion in the protected places, the animals in the wildlife boundaries, to reinforce conservation measures and minimize human-wildlife conflicts. The two model groups analyzed in this study are two. Group 1 is composed of a baseline ANN model of wildlife classification, and Group 2 is the proposed YOLOv10-GAN model of real time nocturnal intrusion identification. Statistical comparison of performance of both groups is done to justify improvement in detection. The statistical validation is performed with a statistical power of 80%, threshold significance value of 0.05 and a 95% confidence interval. A total of 5000 annotated samples were used for training and evaluation. The system is assessed based on the accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, RMSE value, and the speed of the system. The proposed YOLO and GAN models gave better results in terms of the highest accuracy of 96.5% was reached, as well as an average accuracy of 95.2%, which is better than Standard ANN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(90.78 \%)$</tex>. The two algorithms had significantly different values (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t=-63.365, p= 0.001$</tex>) respectively, while the value of RMSE was decreased to 0.72, requiring less than 2 seconds to make a prediction. The results confirm that the proposed YOLO model has greater accuracy and robustness than the standard ANN with respect to the precision, stability and real-time performance. The system works effectively for wild-life monitoring, intrusion detection, and animal surveillance for achieving sustainable wildlife conservation and the mitigation of human-wildlife conflict.

title: A Study and Analysis of Permanent Magnet Biased Hybrid Excited Inductor Motors for Energy Storage Flywheels
authors: Changhu Wang, Xiaofeng Hua, Lu Sun, Yanliang Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid-excited motor for flywheel energy storage systems and further refines its topological structure to enhance power density. Building upon the AxialStacked Hybrid-Excited Inductor Motor(ASHEIM), a CircularStacked Hybrid-Excited Inductor Motor(CSHEIM) topology is proposed. The mechanism by which the permanent magnet bias structure enhances the output torque of the CSHEIM is investigated and analysed. Furthermore, the CSHEIM is compared with the ASHEIM. The results indicate that the proposed CSHEIM for flywheel energy storage possesses excellent flux regulation capabilities. Under constant load output power, it can significantly reduce no-load losses, improve the efficiency of the flywheel energy storage system, and lower the system's selfdissipation rate during cyclic operation.

title: System-Level Single Event Effects Testing of the PIRT 1280MVCam InGaAs Infrared Camera
authors: Landen D. Ryder, Jean-Marie Lauenstein, Michael J. Campola, Matthew Kowalewski
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: System-level single event effects testing was conducted on a commercial-off-the-shelf InGaAs camera system (PIRT 1280MVCam) to examine susceptibility and functional impacts of single event functional interrupts (SEFI) originating within the constitutive electronics. SEFI signatures are reported and the complexities of testing tightly packaged commercial imaging systems are discussed.

title: Performance of TENG Device using Cornstarch and PTFE as Charge Generating Layer
authors: Syaza Afiqah Fadzil, Lyly Nyl binti Ismail, Habibah binti Zulkefle, Nur Sa’adah binti Muhamad Sauki, Mohd Abdul Talib bin Mat Yusoh, Norsabrina binti Sihab
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This study investigates the triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG) device performance utilizing cornstarch film and polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) as charge-generating layers. The objective is to enhance the electrical output of TENG devices by varying the cornstarch concentration from 0.5g to 2.5 g in 6ml of deionized (DI) water, while maintaining a constant thickness of PTFE layer. The fabricated TENG device operates in a contact-separation configuration consisting of cornstarch and PTFE as charge generating layers. Performance evaluation of TENG device was conducted through electrical characterization and material characterization analyses. Material characterization included film thickness measurement, UV-VIS spectroscopy, Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) analysis and contact angle measurement. Result demonstrated that increasing amount of cornstarch increases the film thickness from 10 μm to 80 μm. This significantly improved the output power from 6.81 μW to 86.5 μW. UV-VIS analysis revealed a peak shift at 298 nm with reduced absorbance, while FTIR spectra indicated decreased transmittance in the 1000 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> region. Contact angle measurements confirmed hydrophilic behavior with denser films exhibiting higher water absorption. The optimal cornstarch concentration was identified at 2.5g, yielding the highest output power ~ 86.5 μW. These findings validate cornstarch as a promising, sustainable material for TENG-based energy harvesting applications.

title: An ISAR Time Window Selection Processor for Focused Maritime Vessel Imaging
authors: Azhar Ebrahim, Mohammed Yunus Abdul Gaffar
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This paper presents the development and evaluation of an Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR) Time Window Selection Processor (ITWSP) for identifying focused ISAR images from long-duration radar recordings of sea vessels. In ISAR imaging, especially for non-cooperative maritime targets, image quality is sensitive to uncontrolled three-dimensional rotational motion. To address this challenge, the ITWSP integrates translational motion compensation and imaging interval selection into a unified processing pipeline and validates its effectiveness on measured data. Two imaging interval selection algorithms are investigated: the maximum contrast-based time window selection (MC-ATWS) method and a method that jointly optimises image contrast and Doppler spread. Experimental results using high-resolution radar data of a single-mast yacht demonstrated that the contrast-and-Doppler-based method produced side-view ISAR projections suitable for target classification. In contrast, the MC-ATWS was less effective at identifying discriminative features in ISAR images. The proposed ITWSP provides a practical means to quantify and extract usable imaging intervals from large maritime ISAR datasets, enabling more efficient dataset evaluation and supporting future research into target recognition.

title: An IoT-Based System for Early Detection of Soil Health
authors: Kabelo Moroke, Tiou Ramabu, Khuliso Sigama
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: A lack of timely and reliable soil information often results in soil deterioration and a reduction in bioactive qualities required for healthy plant or crop growth. Furthermore, a lack of early alerts to detect soil health may worsen the problem at hand. In this paper, we present an IoT-based system for the early detection of soil health. The IoT-based system aims to enable proactive agricultural management, allowing farmers to make informed decisions about irrigation, fertilization, agrochemicals, and general crop management. The approach combines a 7-in-1 soil sensor with an Arduino Leonardo microcontroller to capture real-time environmental data. The developed IoT system shows a stable refresh rate and excellent sensor accuracy, indicating the viability of continuous soil monitoring. This is a work in progress, and at this stage, our IoT system can effectively detect (collect) and display soil parameters like pH, moisture, temperature, and nutrient levels in real time.

title: Automated and Personalized Catheter Design for Right Ventricular Endomyocardial Biopsies
authors: Zain Shariff
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Right Ventricle Endomyocardial Biopsy (RV-EMB) is a procedure performed by physicians for diagnoses of multiple cardiac conditions including myocardial inflammation and malignancies. Catheters used for the procedure are generic and getting a sufficient biopsy requires expertise and multiple manipulations of the catheter which could result in complications. RV-EMB is associated with a 6% failure rate. Computed Tomography (CT) Scans are a non-invasive imaging technique that could be used to create a 3D model. Chest CT Scans contain useful information of cardiac structures. CT Scans from the LIDCIDRI provides information that can be used by independent researchers for external projects. YOLO11 segmentation models are the state of the art artificial intelligence models that allow for the segmentation of objects in images and more robust processing of the segmentations. This study creates an automated approach using a trained YOLO11 segmentation model and chest CT Scans to be able to curate a catheter sheath for RV-EMB that is personalized for patient's anatomy. CT Scans were extracted from the LIDC-IDRI and a cardiac path from the Superior Vena Cava at the arch of the Aorta to the area of RV-EMB was annotated. After annotation of 9978 slices, a YOLO11 Extra-Large Segmentation Model was trained for 623 epochs. A final validation of the model was conducted separately. The model scored an mAP50 of 0.955, an mAP50-95 of 0.52, an F 1 of 0.99, and predictions done in less than 300 milliseconds. This model was utilized in a Python program. The program was automated only requiring an upload of CT Scans. The model annotates a path for the catheter and places multiple plausible points. A smoother algorithm adjusts the initial shape of the catheter into a catheter appropriate for the procedure where acute angles are removed and spacing from structures is maintained. Output catheters featured an appropriate length from the Superior Vena Cava to the site of RV-EMB, an average clearance of over 4 mm, the smallest clearance of over 1 mm, and any sharp angles being obtuse. The catheter tip is in contact with the septum. All these statistics show the appropriateness of this approach as well as the catheter sheath for RV-EMB procedures. This study was able to develop an automated and personalized approach to curating catheter sheaths for RV-EMB procedures.

authors: Aditya Ranjan, Daniel Silver, Tirthak Patel, Bhargav Chickmagalurnanjun, Devesh Tiwari

abstract: None

authors: Sunil Kumar, Vivek Kumar, Sridutt Bhalachandra
publish: ACM Transactions on Architecture and Code Optimization

title: A Profound Interest in the Fascinating World of Engineering [Pathways]
