title: Modifying Simple Preference Optimization with Adaptive Margins
authors: Yihang Cheng, Licheng Zhang, Shaohan Wang, Zhendong Mao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences remains an essential step for their practical deployment. Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has established itself as a widely adopted alignment methodology. Simple Preference Optimization (SimPO) further diminishes training expenses while enhancing alignment efficacy. However, SimPO’s performance is highly sensitive to data quality, and its predefined trade-off parameter of margin lacks adaptive adjustment across data of varying quality. In this paper, we propose an adaptive method wherein the target margin is dynamically modulated during training based on data quality. This approach enables the model to more effectively assimilate preference knowledge from highquality data while mitigating the detrimental impact of lowquality data on the model’s generative capabilities. Our method is built upon the SimPO framework, achieving performance improvements with minimal additional code, requiring no external knowledge, and incurring no additional training costs.

title: Modeling the Influence of Social Media Analytics on Consumer Purchase Decisions Using SEM
authors: Srinivas S, Lakshminarayana K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This study explores the stimulus of social media on consumer buying behavior, represented through key variables such as content quality, social commerce features, influencer marketing, and engagement mechanisms, with specific emphasis on purchase intention and purchase frequency. A quantitative research design is adopted, focusing on both direct and mediated pathways. Psychological factors including brand trust, emotional connection, perceived value, and fear of missing out (FOMO) act as mediators. Data were collected from 144 active social media users, and advanced statistical techniques such as Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), mediation, correlation, and regression analysis were employed to examine the relationships among social media, psychological mediators, and purchase outcomes. The findings indicate that social media significantly influences consumer buying behavior, with emotional connection and perceived value emerging as key mediators. SEM results further reveal that social media positively affects purchase intention mainly through mediated paths, while FOMO strongly motivates purchase frequency. These results highlight the importance of emotionally appealing content and urgency-based triggers. However, convenience sampling and a limited sample size restrict generalizability. Future research may address these limitations through larger samples and longitudinal designs.

title: Review Shield AI: Fake Review Detection and Deletion in E-Commerce Platforms
authors: Sandhya N, Vaishnavi, U Shashank Shenoy, Tharun Kumar G M, Vikram
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: False positive reviews kill credibility of online shopping. In this paper, a hybrid machine learning system called Review Shield AI is presented, which has the 92% accuracy score in real-time detection. It can achieve a precision of 93%, recall of 91% and identical error rate of 9% at a latency of only 85ms, unlike deep learning models which require high latency and continually test and refine their models. When 10,000 Amazon and Flipkart reviews are tested, results indicate a strong performance of cross-domain performance particularly in Electronics (94%) and Books (92%). The given hybrid protocol of labeling (60% expert-labeling, κ = 0.87, 40% heuristic) and whole-stack implementation with React.js, Flask, and MongoDB is described in the study, which indicates a clear framework of a scalable e-commerce moderation.

title: Digital Imaging System for High Throughput Plant Phenotyping Using Raspberry
authors: Manjunatha Siddappa, Sindhu R, Teja, Zaiba Khanum
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This project presents the development of a digital imaging system for high-throughput plant phenotyping using Raspberry Pi, designed to efficiently capture and analyze plant leaf characteristics. The system employs a Raspberry Pi-based imaging setup integrated with a high-resolution camera to acquire consistent and clear images under controlled lighting conditions. Advanced image processing algorithms are utilized to extract key phenotypic traits such as leaf size and leaf color, enabling accurate and automated analysis. The system facilitates rapid data collection and processing, making it suitable for large-scale phenotyping experiments. By combining compact hardware and efficient software integration, this digital imaging platform provides a reliable and scalable solution for plant researchers to monitor leaf development and color variation. The project emphasizes precision, automation, and adaptability, contributing to advancements in digital agriculture and plant science research.

title: Using Standard Ethernet and Hardware PTP for Distributed Control of Multi-Megawatt Converters
authors: Ralf Wegener, Stefan Soter
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper presents a cost-effective communication and measurement architecture for multi-megawatt power converters based on standard Ethernet. To reduce analog cabling, improve measurement robustness, and enable modularity, control units and ADCs are mounted close to the power modules, while digitized measurement data are exchanged over an Ethernet network. Precise time alignment of distributed measurements is achieved using hardware-assisted IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol (PTP). In contrast to common approaches relying on fully PTP-aware switches, the proposed system employs commodity unmanaged Ethernet switches and mitigates their non-deterministic behavior through per-link delay calibration, cyclic traffic scheduling, and application-level safeguards. A TDMA-like communication scheme with timestamped UDP or raw Ethernet frames bounds latency and jitter while avoiding retransmission-induced delays. Experimental results from a multi-converter wind-turbine installation demonstrate sub-microsecond synchronization accuracy under field conditions, confirming that standard Ethernet with appropriate system design can meet the timing requirements of phase-sensitive power-electronic control.

title: Construction of a Knowledge Graph for Job Skill Extraction and Curriculum Mapping from Recruitment Information
authors: Xinxin Xu, Meng Liang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of scientific and technological revolutions and industrial transformation, the competency requirements of industrial positions have become increasingly dynamic and composite, while traditional university curriculum systems lag behind these evolving demands. To address the misalignment between job requirement analysis and curriculum construction, this paper proposes a recruitment-information-driven approach for job skill extraction and curriculum mapping, and constructs a multi-layer knowledge graph. By leveraging natural language processing and semantic modeling techniques, job skills are automatically extracted and structurally represented, and semantic associations between job skills and course knowledge points are established through similarity measurement. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach improves both the accuracy and coverage of job skill extraction, while intuitively revealing the correspondence between job skills and curriculum content via a knowledge graph, thereby providing intelligent support for curriculum optimization and talent cultivation adjustment.

title: PixSA: Hardware Efficient Pixel-Level Systolic-Array Architecture Design for AI Workloads
authors: Vibushita S, Mohit Jagini, Nanda Mitra Pammi, Madhav Rao
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: The growing interest in convolutional neural networks (CNNs) has spurred the development of optimized hardware architectures to improve model performance, power costs, and computational latency. However, many designs still struggle with high memory access and constrained throughput. This work introduces a CNN hardware accelerator that addresses these limitations by employing a weight-stationary systolic array augmented with a pixel-level pipelined dataflow to reduce memory access. Instead of loading a full K×K window, the architecture processes only a 1×K vector using local buffering, eliminating interlayer buffering. All design files are made freely available for easy adoption to the research and design community.

title: Health Assistant for Disease Prediction Using Hetero-associative Memory and Large Language Models
authors: R Kumaraswamy, Keerthana M, Manjushree S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This paper proposes a lightweight hybrid healthcare assistant for disease prediction by integrating Hetero-Associative Memory (HAM) with compact transformer-based language models, MiniLLaMA and Gemma 2B. The system interprets patient symptom descriptions using multilingual language models and converts them into structured symptom vectors, which are then processed by HAM to perform deterministic symptom–disease mapping and ranked diagnosis. The framework combines the natural language understanding capabilities of small language models with the interpretability and computational efficiency of associative memory, enabling deployment on resource-constrained and offline healthcare systems. The model was evaluated on a dataset containing 102 diseases and 72 symptoms, using an 80–20 training–testing split. Experimental results demonstrate promising performance, achieving up to 96.8% accuracy, 91.4% precision, 97.2% recall, and 95.1% F1-score, outperforming traditional classifiers such as Decision Trees, SVM, and KNN. These results indicate that combining language model–based symptom interpretation with HAM-based reasoning provides an efficient and explainable approach for real-time healthcare assistance and preliminary disease prediction.

title: Radiation Evaluation of the TPS7H6101-SEP Radiation-Tolerant 200V, 10A GaN Half Bridge Power Stage
authors: T. Lew, M. Trevino, A. Marinelarena
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Single Events Effect (SEE) characterization and total ionizing dose (TID) results for the TPS7H6101-SEP GaN power stage are summarized, showing very robust SEE performance up to LETEFF=50 MeV-cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/mg and excellent TID behavior to 50krad(Si).

title: EnergAIzer: Fast and Accurate GPU Power Estimation Framework for AI Workloads
authors: Kyungmi Lee, Zhiye Song, Eun Kyung Lee, Xin Zhang, Tamar Eilam, Anantha P. Chandrakasan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: As AI workloads drive increases in datacenter power consumption, accurate GPU power estimation is critical for proactive power management. However, existing power models face a scalability bottleneck not in the modeling techniques themselves, but in obtaining the hardware utilization inputs they require. Conventional approaches rely on either costly simulation or hardware profiling, which makes them impractical when rapid predictions are required. This work presents EnergAIzer, which addresses this scalability bottleneck by developing a lightweight solution to predict utilization inputs, reducing the estimation walltime from hours to seconds. Our key insight is that kernels in AI workloads commonly employ optimizations that create structured patterns, which analytically determine memory traffic and execution timeline. We construct a performance model using these patterns as an analytical scaffold for empirical data fitting, which also naturally exposes module-level utilization. This predicted utilization is then fed into our power model to estimate dynamic power consumption. EnergAIzer achieves $8 \%$ power errors on NVIDIA Ampere GPUs, competitive with traditional power models with elaborate cycle-level simulation or hardware profiling. We demonstrate EnergAIzer’s exploration capabilities for frequency scaling and architectural configurations, including forecasting the power of NVIDIA H100 with just $7 \%$ error. In summary, EnergAIzer provides fast and accurate power prediction for AI workloads, paving the way for power-aware design explorations.

title: A Novel Deep Learning Ensemble for Cross-Protocol DeFi Risk Management: Liquidity-Aware Triangulation and Anomaly Detection using Attention-Weighted LSTM Autoencoder and Isolation Forest
authors: Aniketh Malipeddi, Aneesh Chatrathi
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Price disruptions in decentralized lending can precipitate cascading liquidations. Unlike existing single-oracle approaches, this study addresses pre-liquidation anomaly detection using triangulated signals from (i) a protocol oracle, (ii) an external market price, and (iii) an Automated Market Maker (AMM) on-chain price. We evaluate the approach on the Compound DAI incident (Nov. 26, 2020). The method is unsupervised: models are trained only on pre-incident data. First, we use a liquidity-aware sequence model, an attention LSTM autoencoder with feature-weighted reconstruction loss that emphasizes liquidation intensity and cross-venue log-divergences (market vs. oracle; AMM vs. market). AMM divergence is gated by AMM log-liquidity to limit sensitivity in thin pools. Second, we compute a nonparametric baseline (Isolation Forest) on window statistics. We then form a z-normalized linear ensemble of the two scores and sweep the mixture parameter alpha, selecting alpha equals 0.10. On the Compound DAI event, the ensemble attains an AUC of 0.97 and an AP of 0.75, exceeding each component (autoencoder AUC approximately 0.92, AP approximately 0.63; Isolation Forest AUC approximately 0.95, AP approximately 0.49). With an operating threshold chosen for precision greater than or equal to 0.95, the detector raises elevated scores before and during the incident while maintaining zero false alarms per day. These findings demonstrate that triangulating multiple price sources with liquidity awareness detects oracle failures and cross-venue inconsistencies invisible to traditional single-feed systems, enabling hours-earlier warning of liquidation cascades. This framework provides actionable early warnings for protocol risk teams. It can be deployed across lending platforms to prevent oracle-driven liquidation cascades, ultimately protecting borrowers and enhancing DeFi ecosystem stability.

title: Dynamic Pricing and Predictive Analytics in E-Commerce using Machine Learning: Analyzing Market Fluctuations and Demand Patterns
authors: Ghouse Bhasa Batlapadu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The dynamic consumer demand, unstable market conditions, and competitive pricing have become a major challenge to e-commerce platforms. Conventional methods of pricing that are usually in the form of static prices do not respond sufficiently to the fast changing customer demand patterns thus resulting in an inefficient revenue as well as dissatisfaction of customers. Current models of Pricing Like Linear Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Regression, Long Short-term Memory, and Reinforcement Learning-based pricing are only partially accurate but are regarded as having limitations which include inability to adapt to sudden market change, high prediction error, slow convergence, and lack of contextual market, behavioral data. In order to overcome these difficulties, the proposed paper suggests a Dynamic Pricing and Predictive Analytics framework incorporating machine learning-based demand forecasting with the analysis of fluctuations on the market and adaptive pricing policies. The suggested model uses multistage learning process, which takes into account intricate user-product interactions and dynamical adjustment of the price to optimize the revenue and customer satisfaction at the same time. A large scale e-commerce dataset size of more than 100,000 transactions was experimented with and compared the proposed framework to baseline models on various metrics of evaluation such as MAE, RMSE, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, ranking measures (NDCG@10, MAP@10, MRR), AUC-ROC and revenue uplift. The findings indicate that the proposed model is always more accurate at its predictive power, convergence, lesser error, enhanced revenue and reliability as decision making in the dynamic market conditions. The framework presents substantial advancements on the current methods, and it is promising to be utilized in the real world in a large and dynamic online retailing system.

title: Chain Accident Prevention Through Automatic Braking System Using V2V Communication
authors: Rachana. A, Munegowda. GN, Pavitra Tarakar, Vikas. HV, Asharani. M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: A new approach to V2V communication for chain of accidents is considered in this work. A wireless transmitter and receiver may communicate with a vehicle behind. The process will be no different for any car. Vehicle condition like steering, braking, speed and indication are provided to the microcontroller of this system. This system reports or transmits occurrence of the critical status from the leading vehicle to the following vehicle. A head-on car hit in the fog or another front vehicle swerving without warning are typical emergency situations. Such accidents due to a number of factors may be preventable for deployment in combination with an automatic braking device (speed modified system) and the like. The vehicle is governed by automatic braking system (ABS). The majority of the present system is based on sensors and sensor data in guiding remedial action. The problem arises when a sensor fails in the extreme conditions of muck or dust on its surface, or the sensor is too hot. However, our approach is predominantly communication-based instead of sensor-based and has a wider range of distance compared with these work. Moreover, to avoid accidents, a larger time response of the system is provided now. Moreover, the front car also sends a message to the back car regarding an imminent expected collision. One vehicle should also in that case supply the correct data to the following vehicle, when it wants to shift to one of its neighbour lanes. In the development of this project, we are developing the prototype, and use a method that will eliminate a further sequence of accidents between cars by having second vehicle able to slow down to avoid a collision knowing how first vehicle is moving.

title: Structure-Circuit Integrated Manufacturing of Conformal Conductive/Dielectric Electromagnetic Metamaterials for Aircraft Skins
authors: Hao Li, Kuan Lu, Baihong Chi, Wenhe Liao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Electromagnetic metamaterial skins have become the core carrier for optimizing the electromagnetic performance of aircraft owing to their superior electromagnetic modulation capabilities. However, traditional manufacturing processes struggle to adapt to the complex curved surface structures of aircraft skins. While emerging hybrid additive manufacturing technologies offer a breakthrough direction for these process bottlenecks, challenges such as insufficient printing precision, poor adaptability for multi-material synergistic jetting, and high difficulty in conformal manufacturing on complex surfaces remain. Addressing the manufacturing requirements of aircraft electromagnetic metamaterial skins, this paper proposes a conformal conductive/dielectric hybrid structure inkjet integrated manufacturing technology. A conductive/dielectric material hybrid curved surface inkjet system was developed, constructing a five-axis motion control architecture with upper-lower computer coordination and designing the orifice structure of the array printhead. Data loading, printing path planning, and data generation were performed for the curved surface of the aircraft flap metamaterial, realizing the integrated printing of conductive/dielectric hybrid structures on complex conformal metamaterial skins. Experimental results demonstrate that this manufacturing technology effectively enhances the precision of multi-material printing on curved surfaces, achieving high-precision deposition of conductive/dielectric structures on non-developable surfaces, thereby providing equipment support and technical solutions for the engineering manufacturing of aircraft electromagnetic metamaterial skins.

title: A Digital Twin Framework for Predictive Modeling and Intervention Optimization in Inflammatory Bowel Disease
authors: Prathyek Poojary
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is a complex, relapsing condition driven by interactions between genetics, lifestyle, inflammation, and environmental factors. Despite advancements in biomarkers and omics data, tools for individualized prediction and simulation remain limited. This study introduces a novel digital twin framework that integrates multi-modal data to simulate disease trajectories and optimize personalized interventions in IBD care. Using data from 10,286 UK Biobank patients with known or suspected IBD, a 64dimensional digital twin embedding was created via a variational autoencoder incorporating biomarkers, polygenic scores, lifestyle, medication use, and clinical history. Downstream model—sincluding GRUs and multitask neural networks-predicted flare risk, biomarker trends, and comorbidity incidence. Model interpretability was achieved via SHAP analysis and latent clustering with UMAP. A simulation engine tested hundreds of personalized counterfactuals per patient across modifiable variables including diet, supplements, exercise, smoking, BMI, and medication exposures. Optimized intervention sets were derived using Pareto front analysis. Simulated results showed up to 30% flare risk reduction under top-ranked interventions, with a 25% overall flare rate decrease in a virtual cohort (p < 0.001). A case study demonstrated how moderate lifestyle and medication adjustments significantly lowered predicted inflammation risk. This work offers the first scalable, interpretable digital twin framework for IBD, enabling precision forecasting and simulation-based treatment planning. It establishes a foundation for virtual experimentation in chronic disease management.

title: Smart Kitchenmate AI: An Intelligent Assistant for Cooking
authors: Divya V. K, Abisha K, Akshaya G
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The booming food-related online idea has shown the necessity of solutions that are focused not only on convenience and personalization but also on the accuracy in nutritional value and sustainability. This paper introduces the Smart Cooking AI Platform, an implementation of computer vision, natural language processing, and sustainability analytics to create an overall cooking assistant. The system contains deep learning using recipe classification to recognize the ingredients in images uploaded by users so that the inputs can be utilized in generating context-aware recipes through a transformer-based model of text. Nutritional analysis is conducted through the mapping of the identified food products to a validated nutritional database, which generates an exact estimation of calories and nutritional composition of food. In tandem, the platform also features an AI-aided waste management unit which advises on portion modifications, usage of leftovers, and composting systems to reduce food waste. The given system encourages healthy eating, meal planning (according to personal preferences), and sustainable kitchen. Combined with sustainability measures, the outcome of the experiment demonstrates that the platform can be used to make users more active, minimize food waste at households, and make environmentally conscious choices during the cooking process. This solution shows how everyday cooking can be turned into an intelligent, health-aware, and environmentally friendly experience by the means of AI.

title: Progressive U-NET with Soft-Gated Piecewise Polynomial Activation for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Dhanush C, Monica R Mundada, Sowmya B J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of retinal blood vessels is critical for early detection and diagnosis of various ocular diseases such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, cataract etc. In this paper, we propose a novel activation function called as Soft Gated Piecewise Polynomial Activation (SGPPA) integrated with Programmable Neuron-based U-Net architecture for precise retinal vessel segmentation from ocular eye images. The SGPPA activation function learns piecewise polynomial function, thereby extracts non linear patterns from the ocular images. We evaluate the performance of proposed method on the DRIVE dataset and compare the results of different polynomial degrees of SGPPA against conventional activation functions such as ReLU and Leaky ReLU. The experimental results depict that the SGPPA with polynomial degree of 1 achieves excellent performance with an accuracy of 95.5%, recall of 77.9%, F1 score of 81.5%, and an AUC of 0.978. This improved recall level reflects the improved sensitivity required to reduce false negatives in the diagnostic process. Based on the results, the Programmable Neuron-based U-Net model using SGPPA appears to be a powerful and effective approach to the problem of retinal vessels segmentation.

title: Addressing Data Scarcity and Interpretability in PV Fault Detection: A Hybrid AI Framework for African Energy Systems
authors: Katlego Ratsheola, Ditiro Setlhaolo, Akhtar Rasool
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: The rapid expansion of solar photovoltaic (PV) capacity in Africa presents huge opportunities for energy independence but is hindered by urgent operating and maintenance challenges. Effective fault detection and diagnosis (FDD) is key, but is hampered by the double challenges of scarce labeled fault data and the need for interpretable models that can be trusted by local technicians. This paper proposes and validates a hybrid Artificial Intelligence (AI) model to overcome these limitations. The model proposed here employs an unsupervised Dense Autoencoder for anomaly detection with near-perfect accuracy (AUC of 1.0000) using only healthy operation data, entirely circumventing the challenge of data unavailability. This is augmented with a feature-engineered Random Forest classifier that diagnoses the specific fault type with 90% overall accuracy and good precision on all individual fault classes. To enable trust and actionability, the model’s predictions are explained by SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), which confirms the model learns physically meaningful relationships from the data. Validated in a high-fidelity simulation of the 1.3 MW Phakalane PV plant in Botswana, our framework demonstrates a data-efficient, interpretable and highly accurate solution for improving PV reliability in the African context.

title: Closing the Yield Gap: A Scalable Computer Vision Pipeline for In-Field Detection of Devastating Banana Diseases
authors: H.B. Varshini Reddy, K. Shirisha, S. Rakshitha, I.V. Shashikala, V. Poojitha, Dhanunjay Sirigiri
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Although bananas are planted due to food security of the world, they are subject to numerous fungi, bacteria and viruses that reduce the production. Visual method is the normative way through which banana plant infections are identified, and this technique lacks scalability, and it is arbitrary. A new method of deep learning that enables automatic, fast, and accurate identification and diagnosis of the pathogens that affect banana leaves is presented in this paper. Our hybrid Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model was developed and compared with EfficientNetB0 and ResNet50. The model has been trained using a diverse set of images and augmented even more using data augmentation methods. The model achieved great results of 98.7 percent precision and F1score of 0.986. It was capable of locating Black Sigatoka, Fusarium Wilt and Banana Xanthomonas Wilt. Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) has validated that the model identifies biologically significant lesion areas, which enhance interpretability. The findings indicate that the framework is an effective and well-defined precision agriculture tool that simplifies easy response to quick action and possibly effective in identifying diseases in various crops.

title: Assessing the Helix-Destabilizing Effect of Parkinson's-Linked Mutation A30P on Alpha-Synuclein in Silico
authors: Aristaa Bhardwaj, Ulf Dettmer
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disorder marked by dopaminergic neuron loss and alphasynuclein (aSyn) aggregation. aSyn is a presynaptic protein that normally binds membranes via an N-terminal amphipathic alphahelix. Misfolding and cytosolic accumulation of aSyn are thought to be central to PD pathogenesis. The aSyn A30P missense mutation, one of the earliest identified genetic causes of PD, replaces alanine with proline within the membrane-binding region. This substitution partially disrupts the helical structure, reducing membrane affinity and shifting aSyn toward the cytosol, where it may be more prone to aggregation. To investigate how A30P affects helix stability, the in silico tools DynaMut2, FoldX, and Rosetta were employed to compare the energetic landscape of helical wild-type and A30P aSyn. Feeding the programs with both predicted and experimental aSyn structures, we consistently observed helix destabilization in the mutant, though the magnitude of the effect varied across programs. This variability reflects differences in underlying models and energy functions but underscores a shared conclusion: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A 3 0 P}$</tex> weakens the membraneinduced helical structure of aSyn. These findings support a structural mechanism for A30P-linked PD, where impaired helix formation causes cytosolic mislocalization and aggregation-prone conformations. This multi-tool in silico approach highlights the utility of computational modeling in understanding pathogenic protein conformations. It also provides a quantitative framework for future studies on how helix stability influences aSyn (mis)folding and neurotoxicity in PD.

title: Precise Prediction and Classification of Cardiomegaly Using an Autoencoder-CNN Integrated Deep Learning Framework on Chest X-Ray and Multimodal Imaging
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Cardiomegaly, which is one of the severe markers of cardiovascular disease, needs precise identification under the employ of sophisticated imaging technology. This experiment is an integrated Auto encoder-CNN deep learning model that aims at the accurate prediction and classification of cardiomegaly on multimodal imaging and chest X-ray data. The two data sets were scored through a set of metrics, such as precision, F1 score, specificity, accuracy, recall, kappa, and AUC. On Data Set 1, the model had an accuracy of 0.60 and recall of 0.80, with a small AUC of 0.70 and kappa of 0.20, and a moderate level of discriminative ability, but a relatively low level of agreement. Training and validation loss analysis, ROC curves and predicted class distribution showed sufficient sensitivity but low specificity and class imbalance. In conclusion, albeit with future refinements, the approach has potential, and greater optimisation needs additional work to reduce over fitting to reach a dependable enough level to be used in clinical practice.

title: Gesture-Controlled VLC Player: A Vision-based Human-Computer Interface
authors: Gondrala Saicharan, Guttapalem Saketh Kumar Reddy, S Hemalatha, D Deepa
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In the modern world of hectic life, it is important to interact with complex systems efficiently. As technology is rapidly changing, reducing response time and simplifying user operations are primary issues. This leads to human-computer interaction (HCI). This paper provides a low-cost system, which utilizes hand gesture recognition to operate VLC Media Player. The simplest media operations like volume up/down, play, pause, forward and backwards are managed by a webcam which recognizes the hand gestures. It is a system that works with computer vision and automatizes the control of media with PyAutoGUI, which allows users to use their device remotely. The system is based on machine learning gesture recognition. Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) was identified as the most effective among the other neural networks used in the extraction of spatial features of hand gestures. CNN was found to converge rapidly and was very accurate. A Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) was added in order to learn the temporal relationship between gesture frames. This combination has a great deal of recognition accuracy and responsiveness. Moreover, Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) were also studied to identify simpler and stationary gestures. ANNs do not require as much specialized as CNNs or RNNs, which makes them a lightweight solution with which the system can be more flexible. MediaPipe is a cross-platform platform used to implement it using MediaPipe. In general, the suggested gesture recognition system is accurate, fast, and efficient. It has massive opportunities in use as a hands-free controlled device with fine decision making in accessibility tool, entertainment systems, and intelligent environments.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Parameter Tuning for Backstepping Control of Autonomous Reach Stackers in Container Alignment
authors: Zhicheng Bi, Kun Chen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the demand for high-precision pose control during the automatic container alignment of autonomous reach stackers in smart port scenarios, this paper proposes an adaptive backstepping trajectory tracking control strategy based on Deep Reinforcement Learning. To overcome the limitations of traditional backstepping control, where fixed control gains hinder the balance between rapid convergence and high-precision steady-state alignment, the kinematic and pose error models of the reach stacker are established. Subsequently, an online parameter tuning framework based on Proximal Policy Optimization is constructed. Taking the lateral and heading angle errors as state inputs, this framework dynamically optimizes the critical gain parameters within the backstepping control law in real time. Validation results within the Webots high-fidelity physics simulation environment demonstrate that, compared to conventional backstepping methods, the proposed approach effectively suppresses overshoot, accelerates error convergence, and enhances both trajectory tracking precision and system robustness.

title: Deep Learning-Powered VGGNet Framework for Efficient Diagnosis and Stage Assessment of Bladder Cancer
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Bladder cancer is among the most common malignancies in the global society and there is a need to detect it with accuracy and at an early stage to enhance the prognosis of the patient. In this paper, a VGGNet-based deep learning model is proposed to achieve dependable diagnosis and classification of bladder cancer into various stages with clinical parameters. The model was trained and tested on a curated dataset and it exploited the convolutional neural network structures to extract and learn discriminative features. To measure diagnostic efficiency, performance evaluation metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, specificity, and AUC were evaluated. The proposed model had a total accuracy of 96, precision of 95, recall of 97, F1-score of 96, specificity of 95 and AUC of 0.99, which exhibited good predictive power. The confusion matrix and the ROC curve also confirmed strong classification performance. These findings emphasize the possibility of VGGNet-based framework as a reliable diagnostic instrument, which would allow clinicians to obtain early diagnosis and accurate staging of bladder cancer to plan therapy better.

title: Supervised and Reinforcement Learning for Grid-based Robotic Path Planning Using Structured Navigation Data
authors: Arbnor Pajaziti, Vanesa Sejdiu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: Autonomous navigation in structured environments remains a core challenge in robotics, requiring accurate path planning, efficient control, and robust obstacle avoidance. This paper presents a hybrid learning framework that integrates supervised learning with Reinforcement Learning (RL) to enhance navigation in discrete environments. The framework is trained and evaluated using a robot path-planning dataset generated in a 5×5 grid world with random obstacles. It first learns navigation policies from expert demonstrations, then refines them through autonomous exploration. A supervised module predicts optimal immediate actions by processing spatial features, while an RL module optimizes long-term navigation efficiency and safety. Experimental results demonstrate that this hybrid approach significantly improves action prediction accuracy, reduces the number of navigation steps, and increases obstacle-avoidance success rates compared to pure supervised or RL baselines. The findings indicate that hybrid learning is a promising strategy for developing intelligent, resource-efficient autonomous systems for applications in mobile robotics and human-robot interaction.

title: Adaptive V16: Cost-Aware Reinforcement Learning Control for Robust NISQ Variational Optimization
authors: Kondamudi Sivaparvathi, Cheppali Prabhavathi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Variational quantum algorithms (VQAs) are promising for near-term noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices, but their performance is often limited by device noise, finite-shot uncertainty, and nonstationary behavior that destabilize optimization. This paper proposes a cost-aware closed-loop hybrid framework (Adaptive V16) that augments a standard VQA loop with an online decision policy to adapt measurement effort and mitigation actions during execution. At each iteration, the controller uses runtime signals such as objective progress and uncertainty to select an action comprising the shot budget, mitigation mode, and step-scaling factor, explicitly trading solution improvement against resource overhead under a fixed budget. Experimental evaluation on a representative molecular Hamiltonian optimization task shows that Adaptive V16 achieves a lower final energy than a static VQE baseline and reduces the absolute error from 0.1391 to 0.0657. The controller’s decision module also demonstrates strong predictive performance with 0.9700 accuracy, 0.9595 F1-score, and 0.9951 ROC–AUC, indicating reliable adaptive behavior across thresholds. These results suggest that policy-driven adaptation can improve robustness and budget efficiency for hybrid quantum–classical optimization in realistic NISQ settings.

title: Performance Enhancement of Nanosheet/Nanowire RF Transistor Arrays by Backside Metal Layout
authors: Chih-Hsuan Lu, Hsin-Cheng Lin, Tao Chou, Ching-Wang Yao, C. W. Liu
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Parasitic resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">par</inf>)/capacitance (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">par</inf>) in the nanosheet (NS) and nanowire (NW) transistor arrays limit the RF performance. We propose a backside metal layout with additional lateral pitches for low-resistive (LR) Tungsten (W) fill, and employ S/D cuts to reduce R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">par</inf> and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">par</inf>. The backside processing can reduce gate height (H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf>), further lowering C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">par</inf>. The optimized NS and NW transistor arrays achieve maximum oscillation frequency (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MAX</inf>) of 600 and 860 GHz, outperforming conventional designs by 2.1X/2.3X, respectively.

title: Pipeline for Efficient Quantization of Large Language Models for Resource-Constrained Deployment
authors: Tapiwa M. Chiwewe, Adeiza Onumanyi
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Recent years have seen breakthroughs in large language models (LLMs), such as the GPT and LLaMA family of models that have transformed natural language processing. Despite this, their considerable computational and memory requirements inhibit their deployment in edge and mobile environments. In this paper we introduce a modular quantization pipeline that reduces the memory footprint of LLMs while preserving core performance. We evaluate the basic and advanced quantization techniques, including Absolute Maximum Quantization, Zero-Point Quantization, GPTQ, and NF4, and make use of popular toolkits that include bitsandbytes and AutoGPTQ. Experimental results on representative tasks show that 4- and 8- bit quantized models can be run on commodity GPUs and CPUs with acceptable quality loss. Our experiments demonstrated up to 8x compression, making our pipeline suitable for LLM deployment in both edge and industrial scenarios.

title: Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting Based on Joint Optimization of VMD and HHO-LSTM
authors: Fan Guo, Xingchao Zhang, Shiji Yang, Xiaodong Yu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Aiming at the limited prediction accuracy of a single deep learning network caused by the strong randomness and nonstationary fluctuations of wind power, as well as the problem that isolated parameter optimization in the traditional “decomposition-prediction” architecture is prone to falling into local sub-optima, this paper proposes an adaptive short-term wind power prediction model based on Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO) jointly driving Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. This model breaks the limitations of traditional empirical parameter setting and places the time-frequency decomposition scale of VMD and the network hyperparameters of LSTM in a unified highdimensional solution space for collaborative optimization. Validation results based on measured microgrid datasets show that under the optimal parameter combination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(K=4, \alpha=145)$</tex>, the RMSE and MAE of the proposed VMD-HHO-LSTM model drop to 3.4544 and 2.1946, respectively, and the coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> is improved to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0. 9 7 9 0$</tex>. Compared with the single LSTM model, the prediction accuracy is significantly enhanced. Comprehensive validation results demonstrate that the proposed model not only significantly outperforms fundamental baselines but also exceeds state-of-the-art hybrid methods (such as Informer and WT-LSTM) in forecasting accuracy and robustness across multi-step prediction horizons (1, 6, and 12-step ahead).

title: The Design of a Race-To-Sleep State Based Onboard Computer for a 1P Cube Satellite
authors: Timothy A. Crowell, Jared W. Hiller, Trout G. Marnell, Joseph L. Harounain, Kieran M. Shanley, Kyle P. Leger, James J. Broderick, Matthew C. Lombardo, Douglas E. Dow, Saurav Basnet
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: This paper describes the design and implementation of an on-board computer (OBC) for a small scale earth observation satellite. Small scale satellites such as the Wentworth PocketQube satellite provide cost-effective and efficient opportunities to gather low-Earth data. However, these satellites require reliable, power-efficient, and real-time computing management of its essential functions, such as communication, attitude control, power distribution, data storage, and task scheduling. Low-cost small scale satellites using commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) components often lack customization and consume excessive power. This paper shows the steps taken to implement a state-based operation, based on a race-to-sleep design. This design includes deployment and detumble using embedded magnetorquers, data acquisition, data transmission, emergency protocols, and de-orbit mode, all returning to an inactive sleep mode, controlled via a realtime operating system (RTOS). This paper serves as a detailed guide for emerging satellite developers on a custom, state based, OBC architecture.

title: Heavy Ions Single Event Latchup Test Results for the NA2200 Analog Front-End
authors: Tomasz Rajkowski, Grzegorz Szaciłowski, Andrzej Dziedzic
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Heavy ions single event latchup test results of the NA2200 analog front-end are presented. The component might be considered for use in some space applications, although further radiation characterization (including soft SEEs and TID effects) is required.

title: Redefining Intrusion Detection with CNN and the Law of Unity and Struggle of Opposites
authors: Motlatso S. Mokoele, Sello N. Mokwena
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This study applies a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to intrusion detection using the KDD99 dataset and CICIDS2017 DATASET, guided by the dialectical materialism principle of the unity and struggle of opposites. We frame cyberattacks and normal traffic as opposing forces and design the model to learn from their interactions. The datasets are preprocessed with SMOTE for class balance, normalized, and reshaped for CNN input. The model achieves 99.96% accuracy and 97.07% accuracy on KDD’99 and CICIDS2017, with strong precision, recall, and F1-scores for both benign and attack classes. These results reflect the system’s ability to resolve contradictions within the data and maintain detection consistency. The integration of dialectical reasoning into machine learning provides both a novel philosophical framework and practical impact for improving cybersecurity systems.

title: An Attention-Enhanced Deep Learning Model for Robust Identification of Atypical Mitotic Figures
authors: Vetcha Ashrita, Raksha Venkatesha, Lishanthan Sri Ayyanar, Binitha Shree Gogineni, Rimjhim Padam Singh
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Atypical mitotic figures (AMF) are valuable biomarkers for assessing tumour aggressiveness, but accurately detecting them is difficult because they appear infrequently and often differ only subtly from normal mitoses in histopathology images. To address this challenge, this work introduces an attentionaugmented deep learning framework built on a DenseNet-121 backbone and trained using the curated MIDOG Mitotic Figures Binary Classification Dataset. Several baseline CNNs and attention modules - CBAM, CCA, ECA, PSA, and Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) - were evaluated, with SE consistently delivering the best performance when applied before global average pooling. A compact SE1D non-local attention block was also inserted at various depths of DenseNet-121 to determine the most effective placement, with Position 3 providing the highest gains. The final model, combining a global SE module with SE1D at Position 3, reached an accuracy of 89.17%, a precision of 89.91%, and an F1score of 89.08%, outperforming all other configurations. These outcomes underscore importance of strategically placed channelattention mechanisms for classification of mitotic subtypes.

title: Autonomous Neural Vector Control for Hybrid AC/DC Microgrids
authors: Mustafa A. Kamoona, Juan Manuel Mauricio
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The use of hybrid AC/DC microgrids to combine power-electronic-interfaced generation units, DC loads, and renewable energy sources is growing. However, it is still a difficult control challenge to maintain stable DC-link voltage and highquality AC bus voltages under unbalanced and time-varying load conditions, especially when accurate system models are not available. This paper proposes a model-less autonomous learning-based Neural Adaptive Vector Control (NAVC) strategy for Grid-Forming Inverters (GFIs) in hybrid AC/DC microgrids. The proposed controller combines conventional vector control with an online neural adaptive mechanism that estimates and compensates lumped uncertainties and disturbances in real time. The effectiveness of the NAVC technique is demonstrated on an islanded hybrid AC/DC microgrid with unbalanced dynamic AC loads and variable DC power demand. Comprehensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed NAVC achieves AC bus voltage stabilization comparable to a well-tuned baseline PID controller, while offering improved adaptability and robustness without requiring an explicit system model. These features make the proposed approach attractive for practical AC/DC microgrid applications with uncertain operating conditions.

title: Condition Monitoring of Synchronous Reluctance Motors Using Random Forest Classification
authors: Lutendo Muremi, Elsie Swana
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This paper presents a Random Forest-based method for condition monitoring and early fault diagnosis in synchronous reluctance motors. A detailed finite element model of the synchronous reluctance was developed in ANSYS electronics to simulate motor performance under healthy and faulty conditions. The simulated data was processed with Fast Fourier Transform in MATLAB to extract key features, which were subsequently used to train and validate a Random Forest classifier for accurate online fault detection. The proposed approach enhances operational safety, reduces downtime, and prevents severe machine damage by enabling timely detection of incipient stator insulation faults. The data collected demonstrate that the Random Forest classifier achieves an accuracy of 97% with current signature features and 95% with voltage signature features.

title: EMCA: Embedded Multi-scale Causal Attention for Glioma Classification and Interpretation
authors: Hao Chen, Yifan Liu, Shuaipeng Ming, Bo Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Accurate prediction of MGMT methylation status is crucial for determining glioma treatment strategies, yet existing deep learning methods suffer from poor interpretability and insufficient robustness. This paper introduces EMCA (Embedded Multi-scale Causal Attention), the first end-to-end trainable causal attention mechanism that simultaneously enhances classification accuracy and interpretability. EMCA decouples confounding factors across semantic levels through multi-scale causal interventions and leverages learnable causal graphs to model inter-scale dependencies, enabling end-to-end optimization that aligns attention with diagnostic reasoning. Experiments on the BraTS-2021 dataset demonstrate that EMCA achieves a classification AUC of 0.653 (an 7.2% improvement) and a localization Dice score of 0.714.

title: Efficient Skin Disease Detection Using HGAN Augmentation and CQPSO-SVM Classification
authors: Vidhyashree B, Prasanna Lakshmi G S, Seshaiah Merikapudi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: One of the key facts is the early and correct identification of skin diseases to avoid development of progressive stages especially when it comes to rare or complex lesions. Nevertheless, conventional deep learning models frequently encounter such problems as data imbalance, deficient lesion localization, and overfitting. This paper suggests a very strong and effective deep learning model comprising of a Hybrid Generative Adversarial Network (HGAN) to augment the data and a hybrid CQPSO-SVM classifier to perform the appropriate classification of skin diseases. The HGAN uses the advantage of both Generative Adversarial Networks and Autoregressive models to produce good synthetic images, which reduces the problem of class imbalance. To perform fine mapping of lesion location, the framework has a Grasshopper Optimization algorithm (GOA) combined segmentation technique with U-Net architecture to enhance boundary detection and region of interest. The classification module uses a CNN based feature extractor where the hyper other parameter is optimized by Quantum-behaved Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO) to boost SVM in terms of accuracy and generalization. The model under consideration is tested on benchmark datasets, i.e., HAM10000 and ISIC-2018, and shows high results in terms of such metrics as accuracy, precision, recall, Dice coefficient, and Intersection over Union (IoU). Additionally, the model is developed as a mobile application that makes it easy and real-time to assess skin diseases. The study offers a viable and scalable approach to dermatological diagnosis, particularly in exportable and tele-areas with minimal resources.

title: Spectrum Sharing in PD-SCMA Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Gilbert Machani, Simon Chege, Tom Walingo
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: The scarcity of the radio spectrum and the increasing demand for higher data rates and quality of service (QoS) has necessitated the use of spectrum sharing techniques. Secondary users (SU) transmit using the primary user (PU) resources, under strict constraints. A highly promising technology is power domain sparse code multiple access (PD-SCMA) which transmits and receives the user signals in both power and code domains, to boost system capacity. The challenge is to maximize the SU sum-rate while adhering to the interference temperature set by the PU. This work utilizes an underlay cognitive spectrum sharing architecture where the SUs access the PU spectrum only if the interference to the PUs does not exceed a given threshold. A rate constraint is formulated to ensure a robust CRN performance while handling of the constraints and the sum-rate maximization is performed by using the proposed differential evolution (DE)-enabled penalty function. Numerical simulations are performed to evaluate the performance of the PD-SCMA CRN and demonstrate the advantages of the proposed DE-aided penalty function for sum-rate maximization compared to Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA). The PD-SCMA model demonstrated a 233% sum-rate superiority over CD-NOMA while the DE-aided optimizer attained a 5.3% average sum-rate and 46% stability more than PSO.

title: Repositioning Terahertz Wireless: Compact Beamforming for Medium-Range Mobile Connectivity
authors: Minoru Fujishima
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Once envisioned as a core enabler of 6G, terahertz wireless now faces a turning point. User demand for ever-higher data rates has plateaued, and limited range remains a key challenge. However, Shannon’s theorem highlights the close link between received power and achievable data rate, and optical satellite links demonstrate the real need for ultra-fast wireless on mobile platforms. This keynote redefines terahertz as a viable solution for medium-range, high-data-rate mobile connectivity by harnessing the wide 300-GHz band and electronically steerable 2D phased arrays. We introduce circuit innovations enabling ultra-compact transceivers for dense array integration.

title: Recent Advances in Charge Amplifiers for Particle Detection
authors: Yang Chu, Yixin Song, Jace Rozsa, Parker Allred, Aaron R. Hawkins, Shiuh-Hua Wood Chiang
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Charge amplifiers find applications in scientific instrumentation, sensors, and frontend circuits. This paper reviews two distinct technical approaches to CMOS integrated charge amplifiers. The first amplifier consists of an analog core that offers fully differential operation with small feedback capacitance and active reset for robust operation and high-gain. The second amplifier utilizes a "digital-assisted" core that achieves high open-loop gain, low power consumption, large output swing, and excellent CMOS scaling compatibility. Both amplifiers are fabricated in a 180-nm CMOS technology, and their measurement results demonstrate their performance and unique capabilities.

title: Secure and Resource Utilization-Aware Workflow Scheduler for Heterogenous Cloud Computing Platform
authors: Rashmi K A, Vikas Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Scientific workflows in cloud computing are increasingly executed on heterogeneous processing platforms that combine high-performance and energy-efficient cores to meet complex computational demands. However, optimizing energy consumption and execution time (makespan) while satisfying task deadlines and security requirements remains a significant challenge. This paper proposes Secure and Resource Utilization-Aware Workflow Scheduler (SRUWS), a novel scheduling framework that jointly optimizes energy and makespan under strict deadline constraints while ensuring secure execution in heterogeneous cloud environments. The proposed model leverages dynamic frequency-voltage scaling (DVFS) and task profiling to exploit core heterogeneity, thereby enhancing resource utilization and reducing energy consumption. Furthermore, SRUWS incorporates trust-aware metrics to evaluate the security reliability of virtualized resources, ensuring tasks are assigned to trustworthy computing nodes. A multi-objective optimization strategy is employed to balance trade-offs between energy efficiency, makespan minimization, and trust-level adherence. Extensive simulation experiments conducted on widely used scientific workflow benchmarks (e.g., Montage, Sipht) demonstrate that SRUWS significantly outperforms existing baseline schedulers in terms of total energy usage, makespan, and security compliance. These results affirm the efficacy of SRUWS in delivering sustainable, deadline-aware, and secure workflow execution, making it a suitable solution for modern energy-sensitive and trust-critical cloud computing applications.

title: Overhead Perching Strategy on Arbitrary Branches for Nano UAVs
authors: Abner Asignacion, Saad Hussain, Tomoyuki Matsuda, Satoshi Suzuki
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: Energy efficiency remains one of the primary limitations of nano unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), particularly in confined or forested environments where persistent observation is required. Perching enables aerial robots to transition from energy-intensive flight to passive resting states. However, existing perching mechanisms often increase payload mass, shift the center of mass (COM) for underbody perching, and are limited to specific branch geometries or orientations. This paper presents a flexible and lightweight overhead perching mechanism designed for nano UAVs that enables stable attachment to branches of arbitrary diameter, structure, and orientation. The mechanism is fabricated from thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) and designed to bend unidirectionally toward the perching surface, mimicking an octopus-like wrapping motion. The system design enables safe perching and unperching across multiple branches. Experiments using a Crazyflie Brushless platform demonstrate simultaneous perching on three distinct branches with varying sizes and orientations. Results validate geometric adaptability, repeatable branch-to-branch transitions, and structural compliance under nano-scale payload constraints.

title: Research on Knowledge Mining and Intelligent Diagnostic Decision-Making for Electric Drive System Failures in New Energy Vehicles
authors: Fanbiao Bao, Rongfeng Deng, Shaoyong Cao, Zhihong Xiong
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This electronic document is a live template. The various components of your paper are already defined on the style sheet, as illustrated by the portions given in this document. As a core component of new energy vehicles, the reliability of electric drive systems directly impacts driving safety and operational efficiency. This paper proposes an intelligent diagnostic framework integrating multimodal knowledge with domain-fine-tuned large language models. By synthesizing temporal detection data, maintenance record texts, and expert knowledge manuals, it constructs a unified representation system for multi-source heterogeneous knowledge through cross-modal feature alignment and knowledge graph modeling. The introduction of knowledge graph triplet constraints and fault causal inference loss functions enhances the model's professional knowledge comprehension and logical reasoning capabilities. An explainable diagnostic mechanism generates fault causal chains and reasoning processes aligned with cognitive logic. Experimental results demonstrate 94.7% diagnostic accuracy, over 92% accuracy for latent fault detection, and an 89.9 explainability composite score. This model provides an effective technical solution for precise fault diagnosis and preventive maintenance in electric drive systems, while also offering a new pathway for intelligent upgrades in industrial fault diagnosis.

title: Research on Intelligent Diagnosis of Crop Leaf Diseases and Interpretable Report Generation Based on Multi-Task Collaborative Learning
authors: Yuze Li, Bing Xu, Yu Zhang, Yizhe Song, Xueqing Zheng
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Currently, intelligent crop disease diagnosis based on multi-task learning is a prominent research focus in smart agriculture. This approach utilizes a shared feature extraction network to simultaneously handle multiple tasks-including disease identification and severity classification-effectively enhancing the model's generalization capability and diagnostic accuracy. By incorporating attention mechanisms, optimizing network architectures (e.g., integrating CNNs with Transformers), and implementing dynamic weight adjustment techniques, the study achieves synergistic optimization across tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the multi-task model outperforms traditional single-task models in recognition accuracy, lesion detection, and segmentation precision. This technology provides an efficient and reliable solution for precision agriculture, advancing the intelligent transformation of agricultural production.

title: Secure and Scalable Blockchain Architecture for Food Safety and Traceability of Supply Chain
authors: Manikantha K, Geetha S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Providing food safety and quality is a complex supply chain and it remains a major challenge due to limited transparency, data management, and delayed traceability in traditional systems. To solve these problems, this article suggests a safe and scalable blockchain-based system for end-to-end food traceability that makes sure data integrity, provenance, and regulatory compliance are maintained throughout the food product lifecycle. The suggested design combines data collection from the Internet of Things (IoT) with a hybrid on-chain and off-chain storage system. This makes it possible to handle massive amounts of sensor data, certification, and provide an overview of data without slowing down the blockchain. A permissioned blockchain network is employed to support role-based access control and privacy preservation among heterogeneous stakeholders, including producers, processors, distributors, retailers, regulators, and consumers. Smart contracts are the most important part of the system as they automate managing the tasks such as lifetime of products, check food safety criteria like temperature and humidity in real time, and make sure that food safety regulations are followed. If someone doesn't follow the rules, the framework may provide automatic notifications and make it easy to quickly track things back and forth to help with efficient recall processes. The layered architecture proposed here makes it easy for sensing devices and decentralized digital infrastructure to work together. This changes blockchain from a previously just to store data passively to a way that can enforce safety. The architecture is adaptable to various agriculture and food domains that provide a practical foundation for digital food safety and traceability systems.

title: DRAM Evolution and Challenges in Cloud Era
authors: Mingyuan Liu, Zongpei Li, Zean Guo, Chen Yang, Qiao Li, Junchao Zhang, Huihui Li, Hao Meng, Jiping Zheng, Kanyu Cao, Yiming Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: Driven by cloud computing, demand for high-capacity, high-bandwidth DRAM has surged. However, conventional planar DRAM scaling is approaching fundamental physical and economic limits, creating a critical memory wall bottleneck for Cloud development. Scaling trends and core technical challenges of advanced DRAM technologies were systematically reviewed. Furthermore, latest progresses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4} \mathbf{F}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> vertical channel transistor (VCT) and stacked channel transistor (SCT) architectures, which are recognized as the most promising solutions to break through scaling limits were analyzed.

title: Real-Time Collusion Detection in Multi-Agent Systems Via Interpretable Learning Models
authors: Allison Xin, Anushka Paulchoudhury
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Collusion among agents in strategic multi-agent systems poses significant threats to fairness and efficiency in domains such as financial markets, online platforms, and auction mechanisms. We introduce a real-time collusion detection framework that requires no labeled data at deployment and leverages interpretable behavioral features-such as reward, regret, and variance-derived from agent interactions. We evaluate three modeling approaches: unsupervised KMeans clustering, supervised XGBoost classification, and a hybrid ensemble combining Random Forests and SVMs. These models are tested across three custom simulation environments that vary in structural complexity and agent heterogeneity. Empirical results demonstrate that supervised learning enables accurate and low-latency detection, with XGBoost consistently achieving the highest accuracy and fastest convergence, even in noisy and low-signal settings. Our findings underscore the potential of interpretable, simulationtrained models to uncover subtle, dynamic collusive strategies in real-world multi-agent systems.

title: Aptamer-Mediated Inhibition of von Willebrand Factor (vWF) to Prevent Platelet Overactivation in Myocardial Ischemia-Reperfusion Injury: An in Silico Study
authors: Alina Sohail, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Myocardial ischemia-reperfusion injury occurs when blood returns to the heart after a period of oxygen deprivation, often worsening cardiac damage by causing stress and inflammation. Von Willebrand Factor (vWF), a protein involved in clot formation, binds to the GPIb-IX-V receptor on platelets, leading to platelet overactivation and vascular obstruction. Aptamers are short sequences of DNA or RNA designed to bind specific proteins, such as vWF, to block these interactions. We hypothesize that the aptamer A1 binds strongly to the predicted binding site of the vWF, thus preventing platelet overactivation. The vWF and GPIb-IX-V proteins were modeled using AlphaFold 3, while UNAfold and FARFAR2 were utilized to generate the aptamer tertiary structure. Molecular docking simulations were performed to study the vWF and aptamer interactions, followed by analysis using the PLIP interaction web server. The results show that aptamer A1 and A2 bind to the vWF protein. Aptamer A1 formed 12 hydrogen bonds and 4 salt bridges, while aptamer A2 formed 16 hydrogen bonds and only one salt bridge. However, since aptamer A1 binds to the predicted binding site, it was selected for further mutational analysis. To understand the vWFmediated activation of the GPIb-IX-V receptor, we modeled both the vWF-free and vWF-bound states of the GPIb-IX-V receptor using AlphaFold 3. To the best of our knowledge, this represents the first 3D structural model of the GPIb-IX-V receptor in both its vWF-bound and unbound states. These models were further validated by previously published experimental studies, which show that the vWF A1 domain binds to the N-terminal leucine-rich repeat domain of GPIbα and GPIbsp, thereby facilitating receptor activation. The current study demonstrates the promising potential of aptamers in inhibiting vWF, a key catalyst of platelet activation during myocardial ischemia-reperfusion injury. This approach may further improve treatment outcomes and recovery for patients suffering from myocardial ischemia.

title: RIS-Assisted Millimetre Wave MIMO Channel Estimation
authors: Juanyi Zheng, Jinge Du, Chenxi Zou
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: In RIS-assisted millimetre-wave MIMO communication systems under mobile scenarios, an OMPKF algorithm is proposed to address the challenge of estimating timevarying cascaded channels. The Orthogonal Matching Pursuit(OMP) algorithm is first employed to accurately recover sparse channel vectors as initial values. Subsequently, hyperparameters are introduced into the Kalman filtering (KF) algorithm to enable dynamic tracking of the sparse signal. A state space model is constructed to predict the channel based on its time correlation. The sparse structure is then adjusted by optimising the hyperparameters to adaptively vary the sparsity. Finally, the predicted value is corrected based on the observed value using the Kalman gain. Estimation and tracking of the time-varying cascaded channel for RIS-assisted millimetre-wave communication systems are achieved through continuous prediction, optimisation, and correction.

title: AI-Driven Sequential-Contextual Anomaly Detection Engine Based Improved Archimedes Optimization Algorithm for Data Quality Validation in Regulatory Filings
authors: VikramReddy Baddam, Spandana Sama
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: An AI-driven anomaly detection engine for data quality validation in regulatory filings is an intelligent system that tries to automate the detection of errors, inconsistencies and irregularities of large-scale financial disclosures to ensure accuracy, reliability and interpretability to the auditors and regulators. Current models tend to struggle with modeling temporal dependencies, sequential patterns, entity context, and subtle anomalies in high-dimensional and heterogeneous data, and also tend to be non-scalable, lack interpretability and realtime monitoring. In order to deal with these issues, this study suggests a new Sequential-Contextual Anomaly Detection with an Improved Archimedes Optimization Algorithm (SCAD-IAOA), framework, an AI-based hybrid model integrating reconstruction-based learning, sequential and temporal modeling, the analysis of global context and feature-level attention. The methodology involves detailed data gathering between 1993-2024 SEC filings, intensive preprocessing, sophisticated feature engineering with both Sentence-BERT and Graph Neural Networks, and dimensionality reduction with PCA. The IAOA improves feature selection and hyperparameter optimization, whereas the Explainable AI approaches such as SHAP, LIME, and attention heatmaps offer interpretable results of anomaly predictions. The results of experiments on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8}$</tex> million filings indicate a 97.5 per cent accuracy, 96.1 per cent precision, 95.7 per cent recall, 95.9 per cent F1-score, 0.972 per cent AUCROC, and a false positive rate of 2.8 per cent, plus a quick convergence with a 95-epoch convergence and inference latency of 0.31 seconds per filing.

title: Challenges and Innovations in High-Bandwidth DRAM: The Case for Z Angle Memory
authors: Stephen Morein
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: Modern DRAM technology faces fundamental challenges in power consumption, thermal management, repairability, and system integration that are increasingly difficult to address within existing architectural frameworks. Page-based activation wastes significant energy, stacked configurations suffer from poor thermal conductivity, and the economics of tightly integrated memory place unsustainable cost burdens on SoC vendors. Meanwhile, the capacity limitations of in-package memory and the performance penalties of traditional DIMMbased systems leave a widening gap between what applications demand and what current memory technology can deliver. This paper examines these challenges and details a novel memory architecture called Z Angle Memory (ZAM). ZAM employs a vertical slice topology combined with a via-in-one bonding process and inductive I/O edge connections to achieve high density, improved yield, and superior thermal performance compared to planar stacked DRAM such as HBM. By providing a continuous thermal conduction path through each independent slice and eliminating the need for through-silicon vias in individual DRAM layers, ZAM addresses the core limitations of current memory stacking approaches. The result is a commercially viable memory architecture offering improved power, bandwidth, latency, and thermal characteristics relative to state-of-the-art DRAM technologies.
