Firstly, the CBAM attention mechanism is introduced into the original YOLOv8 network structure to enhance the mode's ability to extract critical defect features and improve feature representation. Secondly, the Wise-IoU loss function is incorporated to optimize the bounding box regression process, thereby improving localization accuracy and overall detection performance. Experimental results show that, compared with the original YOLOv8 model, the proposed method achieves significant performance improvements in PCB defect detection tasks: the precision increases from 79.1 % to 89.6 %, representing an improvement of 10.5 %. In addition, after integrating the improved model into the PCB automated inspection system, it can accurately identify and locate various types of defects such as missing holes, mouse bites, and open circuits. In summary, the proposed algorithm and its system application provide an efficient and reliable solution for PCB industrial automated inspection, and also offer valuable references for the intelligent development of other industrial defect detection tasks.

title: Computation–Accuracy Trade-Off in Service-Oriented Model-Based Control
authors: Hazem Ibrahim, Julius Beerwerth, Lorenz Dörschel, Bassam Alrifaee
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Representing a control system as a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA)—referred to as Service-Oriented Model-Based Control (SOMC)—enables runtime-flexible composition of control loop elements. This paper presents a framework that optimizes the computation–accuracy trade-off by formulating service orchestration as an A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">★</sup> search problem, complemented by Contextual Bayesian Optimization (BO) to tune the multi-objective cost weights. A vehicle longitudinal-velocity control case study demonstrates online, performance-driven reconfiguration of the control architecture. We show that our framework not only combines control and software structure but also considers the real-time requirements of the control system during performance optimization.

title: Hybrid CNN–XGBoost Model for Endometrial Cancer Detection from Histopathological Images
authors: Bodem Niharika, Mohit Arora
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Endometrial cancer is the predominant gynecological malignancy on global scale and its prevalence has been steadily increasing, particularly in postmenopausal women. The early and correct analysis is crucial for positive treatment outcome and increased survival. Conventional diagnostic procedures, including ultrasound and biopsy are invasive and inefficient, with low sensitivity for diagnosis. In order to focus these issues, in this study, we propose an intelligent and interpretable model based on a combination of deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) techniques for the automatic endometrial cancer disease detection from histopathological images. The discriminative features are extracted by the convolutional neural network (CNN) and then classified into normal, hyperplastic and malignant subtypes according to an optimized XGBoost model. Better generalization of model is achieved through hyperparameter tuning. A comparative review against 15 research articles from 2021 to 2025 indicates that CNN-XGBoost hybrid model surpasses the traditional ML based models and results, with an accuracy of up to 97% and area under curve (AUC) greater than 0.996.

title: Adaptive Event-Driven Regime Modelling of Non-Stationary Rainfall Under Climate Variability
authors: Nandhakumar R, Subramanian P, Surendran R
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The variability of rainfall under the changing climatic conditions becomes a big challenge to the hydrological prediction, water resource management, and disaster risk reduction because of its intermittent and unstable nature. This paper suggests Adaptive Event-Driven Regime Transition Model (AERTM) in the context of a Regime-Conditioned Stochastic Event Dynamics Modelling (RC-SEDM) model to address the shortcomings of conventional continuous and fixed transition rainfall models. The model models rainfall using discrete stochastic events with duration, intensity and dry spells between them but latent rainfall regimes are automatically determined without any seasonal assumptions. The adaptively conditioned regime transitions are based on the features of the prior rainfall events, which makes it possible to simulate persistence, clustering, and regime transitions in a realistic way. The proposed framework has been tested with the long-term data of the daily rainfalls in Pune, India and compared with the Gaussian Mixture Models, Hidden Markov Models and Seasonal Regime Models. AERTM had the lowest Bayesian Information Criterion of all models which gives good balance between model complexity and explanatory power and lower rainfall reconstruction error with a RMSE of 8.9 mm/day. Further evidence of correct reproduction of extreme rainfall behavior was obtained by upper-tail quantile analysis of extreme behavior at the 95 th and 99 th percentile, as compared to observed extremes. These results indicate that adaptive eventconditioned regime modelling is effective in non-stationary rainfall dynamics in climate variability.

title: Comprehensive Voice-Based Pain Level Classification Utilizing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
authors: Andrew Y. Lu, Wei Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Accurate classification of pain level is a persistent challenge in both clinical and non-clinical settings. Traditional approaches such as self-reporting, physiological signal monitoring, and facial expression analysis often suffer from limitations related to accessibility, equipment cost, and professional support. In this work, we propose a lightweight framework for real-time pain level classification using vocal signals. The proposed framework exploits the unique spectrogram features of voice signals to train Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models. Our framework has been validated through simulations in Jupiter Notebook and a Raspberry Pi based prototype. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms existing methods by more than 20 % in accuracy. Our case studies also examined the impact of sample size and label imbalance on classification accuracy, showing that biased training leads to overfitting and a significant decrease in classification accuracy. Our hardware prototype, built from commercial off-the-shelf components for under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> USD, achieves real-time processing speeds ranging from approximately 6 to 22 seconds. In addition to CNN models, our experiments demonstrate that other machine learning algorithms such as Artificial Neural Networks, XGBoost, Random Forest, and Decision Tree have also proven applicable within our pain level classification framework.

title: Smart Farming: Eggplant Leaf Disease Detection through Fine-Tuned DenseNet201 Deep Learning Model
authors: Khushdeep Kaur, Husanpreet Kaur, Avneet Kaur, Varun Gupta, Shobhit Tomar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In the majority of developing countries, farmers use eggplant that is readily infected by different diseases that affect leaves. The early and correct detection of these diseases will help in provision of food, sustainable agriculture and cost reduction. A precise fine-tuning of the DenseNet201 and a deep learning methodology allowed scholars to differentiate the eggplant leaves state of a healthy plant, insect pest disease, leaf spot disease, mosaic virus disease, small leaf disease, white mold disease and wilt disease. Overall, 3552 images of open-source Kaggle platform were selected and divided into the following subsets: training, validation and testing: 70, 15 and 15 percent. The given system demonstrated a 86% hit rate with test data and a high score in precision, recall and F1scores on most of the classes. The analysis of the confusion matrices and the training curves show clearly that there is no error rate or the model is firm. Artificial intelligence in plant pathology can help find a faster solution to plant diseases, responsible eating patterns, and fight climate change and hunger. The study satisfies the objectives of sustainable development by contributing to the effectiveness of the farming process, by facilitating the new ideas in the process of finding the solutions and effective use of the resources. The recommended method can enable farmers and other agriculturalists to identify diseases at an earlier age, shift toward low-pesticide practices and achieve greater yields.

title: Revolutionizing Cloud Security through Federated Learning for Next-Gen Healthcare Systems in South Africa
authors: Ramahlapane Lerato Moila, Mthulisi Velempini
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Our study presents an innovative federated learning (FL) approach secured by a hybrid zero-trust cloud architecture, specifically designed for the healthcare system in South Africa. Amid increasing cybersecurity threats and growing privacy concerns in hospitals, our approach enables collaborative model training across decentralized medical institutions without the sharing of raw patient data. This study used the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset to simulate distributed environments and evaluate the performance of various algorithms, including logistic regression, random forests (RF), and deep learning, in both local and federated environments. Simulation results show that while the deep learning model performs well during local training, it degrades under global aggregation due to data heterogeneity. RF and Logistic Regression (LR) demonstrate stronger, more stable performance during FL. Compared with existing studies, our proposed scheme achieves higher accuracy—96% in LR and 97% in RF, improved scalability, and optimized computational efficiency, positioning it as a viable and secure foundation for next-generation healthcare systems in South Africa.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Aerodynamic Shape Optimization: A Machine Learning Approach to Accelerating Aircraft Design
authors: Akshay Lakkur, Benjamin Schmidt, Linus Feng, Aarav Dixit, Chloe Lui
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Aerodynamic Shape optimization (ASO) is a computationally challenging but critical component of modern aircraft design, which involves modifying an airfoil to minimize the drag-to-lift ratio. Traditional methods for this problem utilize a simulation-based approach known as computational fluid dynamics (CFD) to solve the Reynolds-Averaged Navier-Stokes (RANS) equations and extract pressure values, which can be used to calculate lift and drag. These values are then iteratively updated to better suit turbulence conditions. The problem with this approach lies in the time complexity of iteratively computing solutions to the RANS equations required for ASO to converge. To exponentially speed up the process of ASO, we propose a novel algorithm that replaces a CFD solver with a physics-informed neural network (PINN), along with a comparison between two parameterization techniques to optimize an airfoil, both utilizing a PINN instead of a CFD solver. The model shows a speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1, 3 1 0. 2 4 \%}$</tex> with the risk of only a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 \%}$</tex> decrease in accuracy compared to a CFD solver.

title: Modelling Predictive Uncertainty for Reliable Traffic Flow Forecasting Using UA-STTFF Framework
authors: Hemavathi N, R Sumathi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) rely on precise and reliable traffic flow forecasting (TFF) to alleviate congestion, optimise routing, and allow safe traffic management. Nevertheless, most current spatiotemporal forecasting models do not well model complicated spatial interactions between intersections and changing time dynamics, and do not pay much attention to predictive uncertainty, which is essential in decision-making in the presence of variable traffic conditions. To overcome these difficulties, the present paper introduces an Uncertainty-Aware Spatiotemporal Traffic flow Forecasting (UA-STTFF) framework that simultaneously trains spatial dependencies with the help of graph convolutional networks and time dynamics with the aid of gated recurrent units (GRUs). The structure also uses probabilistic output heads and Monte Carlo (MC) dropout to enable the uncertainty-aware traffic forecasting. The proposed UA-STTFF is strictly tested on simulated multi-intersection time-series data of traffic. Detailed ablation experiments show that the spatial graph convolution and the temporal GRU block are fundamental in the enhancement of predictive accuracy, whereas probabilistic heads and MC dropout considerably enhance the representation of uncertainty. The experiments have demonstrated that UA-STTFF outperforms a deterministic spatiotemporal graph neural network baseline by 26.4%, 25.6%, and 26.4% in mean absolute error (MAE), root mean square error (RMSE), and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE). Further, the model has a prediction interval coverage probability (PICP) of 93.7%, which is a good prediction of uncertainty. Visualising uncertainty at the node level also allows the identification of intersections that are likely to be highly risky, which can be used in risk-conscious traffic control. In general, the suggested framework eliminates the significance of uncertainty-sensitive modelling in dependence on credible and extensive ITS.

title: Research Review on UAV-Based Air-Ground Small Target Detection and Tracking
authors: Shaoqiong Duan, Shixiao Wu, Xi Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: UAV air-to-ground small target detection and tracking is the core enabling technology for low-altitude intelligent perception, widely used in security monitoring, energy inspection, emergency search and rescue, agricultural monitoring and other engineering fields. Current research still faces key challenges: inherent feature scarcity of small targets, strong clutter interference from complex backgrounds, limited on-board computing resources, and insufficient detection-tracking synergy. Aiming at the deficiencies of existing reviews (inadequate elaboration on lightweight algorithm adaptation for UAV scenarios, insufficient discussion on detection-tracking collaborative optimization, and lack of systematic quantitative evaluation of state-of-the-art algorithms), this paper first clarifies the core scientific issues and five key technical bottlenecks in this field. Secondly, it systematically analyzes the core mechanism, technical advantages and limitations of various algorithms from six core technical dimensions: backbone network optimization, multi-scale feature fusion and enhancement, attention mechanism introduction, loss function improvement, lightweight model design, and detection-tracking collaborative optimization. Subsequently, it completes the quantitative performance comparison of mainstream improved algorithms based on the VisDrone2019 dataset. Finally, it dissects the core deficiencies of current research and prospects key future breakthrough directions.

title: The On-Robot Computing Needs of Embodied AI
authors: Shaoshan Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Embodied AI spans a broad range of robotic forms, including mobile robots, manipulators, quadrupeds, humanoids, and other task-specific machines, rather than humanoids alone. Across these embodiments, a common systems challenge remains: intelligence must ultimately be supported by efficient on-robot computation. This article examines the shared on-robot computing needs of embodied AI through four tightly coupled layers: actuator and servo loops, multimodal sensing, whole-body and core robotic control, and foundation models. We argue that the central bottleneck is not only model capability, but also how onboard compute is architected, distributed, and coordinated under strict constraints in latency, power, thermal budget, reliability, and cost. In particular, embodied AI workloads are fundamentally heterogeneous, ranging from deterministic real-time control and sensor fusion to optimization-heavy robot functions and data-intensive model inference. These diverse requirements cannot be addressed by scaling foundation models alone. Instead, progress in embodied AI will increasingly depend on cross-layer co-design of algorithms, compute architectures, memory and communication systems, and robot form factors, so that robotic platforms can achieve responsive, robust, and energy-efficient intelligence in real-world deployment.

title: Interpretable Cross-Corner Timing Prediction Based on XGBoost-SHAP Solution
authors: Keqing Ouyang, Qi Wei, Weiran Gou, Chenfei Wu, GuoHua Zhou
publish: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems
abstract: None

title: Random Dense Knowledge Distillation for Continual Learning
authors: Jie Chu, Pei Liu, Tong Su, Yunpeng Wu, Yu Song, Zenglin Shi
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Compensation of Frequency Shift Induced by the Electron Layer in Relativistic Magnetron
authors: Dong-Hyun Oh, Woo-Sang Lee, Jung-Hoon Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: The relativistic magnetron (RM) has attracted significant attention as a high-power microwave (HPM) source. Due to its narrowband characteristics, achieving a precise target frequency is a critical requirement in the initial design process. However, a frequency shift phenomenon observed in eigenmode and particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations complicates accurate frequency tuning. This shift is primarily attributed to the formation of a dense electron layer around the cathode during operation, which acts as a virtual conductor and effectively expands the cathode radius. To address this discrepancy, we propose two geometric compensation techniques: a posteriori and a priori methods. The a posteriori approach determines the effective extended boundary by explicitly analyzing the simulated space-charge distribution. In contrast, the priori method analytically predicts this effective expansion by utilizing the double-stream characteristics of the space-charge density, thereby eliminating the need for preliminary PIC simulations. The proposed compensation methods were verified using four magnetron models with distinct structural parameters. While the uncompensated resonant frequencies exhibited significant discrepancies ranging from 28 to 85MHz compared to the actual oscillation frequencies, the application of the proposed methods successfully reduced this frequency difference to within 25MHz across all models.

title: Conference Calendar
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Industry Applications Magazine

title: Shadow Distance Analysis of Agrivoltaic System in Bangladesh: From Geometry to Simulation
authors: Zihan Rejwana, Mohammad Tasnimul Hasan, Md. Mosaddequr Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This study compares two methods for calculating shadow distance in agrivoltaic systems: geometrical analysis and 3D modeling. Simulations were conducted for an agricultural site during the extended shading period of winter to assess model performance. A MATLAB program was developed using modified equations for geometrical calculations, while SketchUp was employed for 3D simulation and validation. The results indicate that the geometrical model maintains an average error of less than 6 % compared to the 3D approach. These findings suggest that geometrical modeling can serve as a reliable tool for shadow assessment in agrivoltaic system design.

title: Adaptive IR-HARQ System With Deep Learning-Based Early Prediction for URLLC Service
authors: Narayan Prasad Kusi, Dong Ho Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Ultra-high reliability and low latency communication (URLLC) is a crucial concept in the realm of 5G and beyond. URLLC has a demand for short packets with an end-to-end latency of 1 ms with a success probability of 99.999%. The stringent reliability and latency requirements of URLLC significantly affect the hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) mechanism at the PHY/MAC layer. In this paper, we propose an adaptive incremental redundancy HARQ (IR-HARQ) scheme that leverages deep learning techniques to predict the decoding reliability of a received codeword before the actual decoding process. Additionally, we propose novel packet transmission schemes suitable for IR-HARQ. In particular, the proposed concept enables the transmitter to respond more quickly by receiving early feedback (FB) on decoding reliability and simultaneously transmitting the required incremental redundancy versions (RVs) using the proposed transmission schemes. We also propose an early prediction method based on long short-term memory (LSTM) using novel features to accurately predict decoding reliability. This method achieves more than 98% prediction accuracy for a code rate of 2/5, and over 96% for code rates of 5/6 and 3/4 in quasi-static fading channel environment. The proposed adaptive IR-HARQ, combined with the packet transmission schemes, significantly improves throughput and bandwidth efficiency compared to conventional IR-HARQ, while meeting the latency requirements of URLLC.

title: PreSFC: Predictive SFC Migration via Multi-slot Mobility Forecasting in MEC Networks
authors: Ji Li, Songtao Guo, Quanjun Zhao, Guiyan Liu, Liang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Network Function Virtualization (NFV) is a foundational technology for Mobile Edge Computing (MEC). It delivers network services by chaining Virtual Network Functions (VNFs) into sequential Service Function Chains (SFCs). One of the most critical challenges in MEC is how to provide continuous and stable services to high-mobility user, such as intelligent vehicles and drones. However, current mobility-aware SFC migration methods remain constrained by either post-hoc reaction or myopic prediction horizons, failing to reconcile the divergent timescales of network services and user mobility, thus resulting in suboptimal resource allocation and service delivery. In this paper, we first formulate the predictive mobility-aware SFC migration problem as an NP-hard Integer Linear Programming (ILP) problem. Aiming to mitigate service disruption for mobile users in MEC networks, we propose PreSFC, a predictive SFC migration framework that integrates multi-slot mobility forecasting with fine-grained network state tracking. We first design Gformer, a deep learning-based long sequence time-series forecasting model, which operates on short time slots (less than 200 ms) to sensitively capture network dynamics while predicting over multiple slots (e.g., 50 slots) to effectively track user mobility. This dual-scale design explicitly addresses the temporal disparity between mobility patterns and service requirements. Based on the predictions, we further propose an Optimal Sub-period Partitioning Migration (OSPM) algorithm to determine migration timing and locations. Extensive simulations show that our approach reduces the maximum and average downtime by approximately 55% and 40%, respectively, compared to benchmark methods.

title: Enhanced Circle Method for Discriminating Incipient Faults in Power Transformers Using Dissolved Gas Analysis
authors: Abdelmoumene Hechifa, Chouaib Labiod, Abdelaziz Lakehal, Redha Meneceur, Ali Bebboukha, Said Djaballah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Power transformers are essential components in electrical power systems, ensuring the continuous transmission and distribution of electricity. Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) has become a widely accepted method for monitoring the condition of transformers and detecting incipient faults. Among the various DGA methods, graphical techniques have gained significant popularity in recent years. In this study, an Improved Circle Method is proposed as an enhanced extension of the Circle 1 and Circle 2 methods to address the limitations of traditional DGA methods in distinguishing overlapping fault types. The method employs a sigmoid activation function to control the convergence and divergence distances between fault samples, significantly improving the separation of electrical and thermal faults. By utilizing Gray Wolf Optimization (GWO), optimal angles are determined for the best fault separation. Experimental validation using the IEC TC10 database demonstrated that the Improved Circle Method achieves an overall accuracy of 79.49%, which can be considered an improvement over both Circle 1 and Circle 2 methods. The results confirm that the proposed method serves as a reliable alternative to other conventional graphical techniques.

title: Reconstruction of curing temperature field evolution in epoxy composite insulators based on sparse monitoring data and reduced order modeling_supp1-3696940.docx
authors: Ying Lin
publish: N/A
abstract: Non-uniform temperature distribution during epoxy resin curing is a primary source of internal stress development. However, existing experimental techniques cannot capture the global evolution of internal temperature fields, while conventional numerical simulations are challenged by strong coupling among material properties, temperature, and degree of cure. In this work, a global reconstruction model for curing temperature fields is developed for cycloaliphatic epoxy insulators based on sparse monitoring data and reduced-order modeling. The reconstruction accuracy and generalization capability of the proposed model are systematically evaluated. The results confirm that the proposed model enables accurate reconstruction of the curing temperature field evolution, with a reconstruction time of less than 0.05 ms. Reconstruction accuracy improves with increasing numbers of monitoring points and snapshots, although the improvement gradually saturates. Further improvements are achieved through optimized snapshot selection and reduced monitoring errors. The model developed at 130 °C successfully predicts temperature fields at lower curing temperatures with a maximum error below 1.6 °C, and maintains errors within 2.6 °C for epoxy systems exhibiting different curing kinetics. Higher curing temperatures yield more diverse thermal field characteristics, resulting in improved generalization performance. This work establishes a global curing temperature field reconstruction strategy, providing a quantitative foundation for enhancing the reliability of epoxy composite manufacturing.

title: Dynamic Escaping Population Driven Evolutionary Algorithm for Multi-objective Optimization Problems with Deceptive Constraints
authors: Xinyu Zhou, Weifeng Lin, KunJie Yu, Kangjia Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Deceptive constrained multi-objective optimization problems (DCMOPs) pose severe challenges to constrained multi-objective evolutionary algorithms (CMOEAs), as constraint violation (CV) values become unreliable. In this paper, a dynamic escaping population-driven CMOEA, named DEPEA, is proposed by maintaining three cooperative populations: an escaping population (EP), an auxiliary population (AP), and a main population (MP). The EP employs slow and rapid escape modes to regulate its escaping behavior at different evolutionary stages, the AP aims to converge toward the unconstrained Pareto front (UPF), and the MP focuses on approximating the constrained Pareto front (CPF). To avoid mutual interference between the AP and EP while controlling the escaping behavior of the EP, a one-way fine-grained cooperation mechanism is designed. In addition, a boundary diversity enhancement strategy is proposed to strengthen exploration along boundary regions. Experimental studies conducted on four benchmark suites and six real-world constrained optimization problems demonstrate that DEPEA achieves superior performance compared with thirteen state-of-the-art CMOEAs.

title: Towards a Data-Driven Approach for Early Identification of at-Risk Students in Higher Education
authors: Ahmed Abrar Zayad, Ashim Dey, Annesha Das
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Predicting students attrition is key for higher education institutions, as it allows for timely and proper intervention for those identified as dropout students. This study proposes a machine learning procedure to assess the likelihood of student attrition using academic records, demographic features, and macroeconomic indicators (i.e., GDP and inflation) at the time of enrollment. In contrast to prior work that predicts Graduate, Enrolled, or Dropout outcomes, this study focuses on distinguishing the pupils who actually discontinued their studies from the students who graduated instead. Predictions were made after the first two semesters to facilitate early intervention. Since the dataset is highly imbalanced, SMOTE was applied for class balancing. Hyperparameter tuning using RandomSearchCV was performed, which helped improve the model's performance. Several classification algorithms, including XGBoost, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and a Voting Classifier, were evaluated using multiple performance metrics. The tuned Voting Classifier attained the maximum level of accuracy of 92.15 %, demonstrating the effectiveness of machine learning algorithms with optimized parameters. The feature importance chart generated according to the voting classifier also shows that the average approval rate and whether a student is aware of their tuition payments are some of the most important factors for a student's outcome. These results demonstrate the possibilities of data-driven approaches in effectively helping students at risk and using resources.

title: Knee Point-Guided Online Cooperative Path Planning for UAVs in Dynamic Multi-Objective Environments_supp1-3694877.pdf
authors: YUE XU
publish: N/A
abstract: Online path planning for unmanned aerial vehicles requires dynamic trade-offs between path length and safety risks in complex and dynamically changing threat environments. However, most of recent researches either do not consider multi-UAV cooperative operations or do not incorporate online responses to dynamic threats. To address these limitations, a knee point-guided dynamic multi-objective path planning method is proposed in this paper. The main contributions are as follows: 1). A dual-UAV cooperative jamming mechanism is proposed to actively respond to threat risks and improve mission success rates; 2). A dynamic role-switching mechanism is developed under a leader-follower framework to enhance the system’s flexibility in responding to threats; 3). A knee point-guided change response mechanism is constructed, which integrates neighborhood sampling and imbalanced transfer learning to adaptively adjust the response strategy based on the degree of environmental change. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed mothed can effectively and rapidly respond to environmental changes, enabling UAVs to generate feasible and efficient online flight paths under complex dynamic threat conditions.

title: Communication-Constrained Remote Power Grid Resilience Enhancement: Coordinated Planning of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles and Mobile Energy Storage Vehicles
authors: Hongzhen Wang, Boyu Qin, Shidong Hong, Fan Li, Tao Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: The underdeveloped power and communication infrastructures in remote areas are prone to simultaneous failure during extreme disasters, exacerbating the difficulty of power system recovery. Mobile energy storage vehicles (MESVs) and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) can achieve the coordination of power and communication, providing precise and effective power support for remote power grid. However, the coordinated planning of MESVs and UAVs is constrainted by factors such as endurance, coverage, and connection. The relationship between these constraints and the performance of dispatching flexibility and resilience support has not been well investigated. This paper proposes a deployment-dispatching two-stage coordinated planning framework of UAVs and MESVs to enhance the remote power grid resilience. The topology-based UAV deployment model and the power flow based MESV deployment mode is proposed. The coordinated dispatching model for UAVs and MESVs considering communication availability is proposed. The test case on IEEE 33 node system and application case on a remote power grid in Northwest China is conducted. The results show that the spatial dispatching flexibility in exchange for communication and traffic constraints significantly improves the resilience support performance of MESVs.

title: A Compact Design of Branch-Line Coupler Based Reactively Compensated Parallel Coupled-Lines
authors: Niwat Angkawisittpan, Somkuan Srisawat, Somchat Sonasang, Ravee Phromloungsri
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: A planar 3-dB quadrature branch-line coupler (BLC) employing simultaneous inductive and capacitive compensation of parallel coupled microstrip lines is proposed for size-constrained RF front-ends. Closed-form synthesis equations derived from even/odd-mode analysis enable a first-pass design without electromagnetic optimization. Fabricated on Rogers AD260, the 3.0 GHz prototype occupies only 0.54 λ2g , corresponding to a 46% size reduction compared to the conventional λ/4 implementation, while achieving |S11| < −23 dB, isolation > 25 dB, insertion loss of 3.25 ± 0.25 dB, amplitude imbalance ≤ 0.4 dB, and phase error < 1.2° over a 27% fractional bandwidth. Sensitivity analysis confirms stable operation under ±10% component tolerances, and the measured results deviate from theory by less than 3%. Compared with recent compact BLC designs, this work achieves the highest bandwidth-to-area ratio using only two chip inductors and two planar capacitors, while remaining fully compatible with single-layer PCB fabrication and extensible to multiband or reconfigurable hybrid circuits.

title: Temperature-Insensitive Pressure Sensor Based on Bending-Induced Strain Gradient in Fiber Bragg Gratings
authors: Wei-Chen Li, Yu-Chung Chang, Wen-Fung Liu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: high-sensitivity, temperature insensitive fiber Bragg grating (FBG) pressure sensor based on bending-induced nonuniform strain is demonstrated. External force applied to the loading plate is converted into controlled bending deformation of an elastic steel plate, generating an axial strain gradient along the embedded FBG that produces significant broadening of the Bragg reflection spectrum. Unlike conventional sensors that rely on Bragg wavelength shifts, this unique configuration employs spectral bandwidth as the primary sensing metric. This bandwidth-based approach provides a sensing mechanism that is expected to reduce temperature sensitivity. Through systematic optimization of the steel plate thickness, adhesive con-figuration, and support spring stiffness, the maximum sensitivity configuration achieves a bandwidth sensi-tivity of 0.181nm/N, which yields an equivalent pressure sensitivity of 9.05 nm/MPa. To prioritize mechani-cal durability and repeatability, the final design configuration provides a bandwidth sensitivity of 0.091nm/N, equivalent to 4.55 nm/MPa, with enhanced structural integrity. Experimental results confirm that the pro-posed sensor offers high sensitivity, superior repeatability, and robust structural stability, making it a prom-ising candidate for compact, high-dynamic-range force-based pressure sensing applications.

title: Integrating Pathway Enrichment with Graph Attention Networks for Pan-Cancer Classification
authors: Anika Tasnim, Propa Punam, Md. Salahuddin Fahad, Tasnim Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Large-scale pan-cancer transcriptomic resources such as TCGA provide unprecedented opportunities to identify features that are both predictive and biologically meaningful. However, the high dimensionality and heterogeneity of RNA-seq data pose challenges for robust feature selection and generalizable classification. In this study, we present a hybrid computational framework that integrates mutual information and LASSO filtering with pathway-guided Graph Attention Networks (GATs) to extract a compact set of significant features from TCGA PanCancer RNA-seq data spanning 33 tumor types (n = 10,826 samples). Our pipeline reduced more than 60,000 genes to 32 key features while preserving predictive power. Using SMOTEbalanced training and multiple classifiers (Random Forest, Light-GBM, XGBoost, DNN, and an ensemble model), we achieved 96.21% overall classification accuracy and consistently high precision, recall, and F1-scores across cancer types. Functional enrichment analysis of the selected features revealed biologically coherent pathways associated with tumorigenesis, highlighting the potential of our approach to identify functionally relevant and highly discriminative transcriptomic signatures in pan-cancer studies.

title: Genetic Algorithm with Optimised Extra Tree Ensemble for Enhanced Intrusion Detection in IoMT Networks
authors: Rubiat Jahan Momtahina, Abdullah Rajib, Ashraful Goni, Fuad Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Clinical efficiency has been remarkably enhanced by the integration of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) into the healthcare system. But at the same time, due to the integration of IoMT, the attack surface for cyber threats is also increased. In the case of domain-specific environments, current machine learning and deep learning models have shown a deficiency in computational scalability and adaptability. To reinforce intrusion detection in IoMT networks, a Genetic Algorithm with Optimised Extra Tree Ensemble (GA-ExtraTree) is developed in this work. For selecting discriminative features from the MedSec-2025 dataset, the Genetic Algorithm is used, and this also reduces dimensionality and redundancy. On the other hand, highly accurate classification with decreased computational load has been guaranteed by the optimised Extra Tree ensemble. Compared to the current baseline approaches, the proposed model performs better by achieving 99.35% detection accuracy. According to experimental outcomes, GAExtraTree is identified as a high-performing, low-overhead, and domain-transferable architecture for intrusion detection in realtime IoMT, and it will strengthen the robustness of the security infrastructure of modern medical network systems.

title: Enhancing Drug-Induced Liver Injury Prediction via Multi-Representation Molecular Images with Grad-CAM Explainability and Functional Group Attribution
authors: Hoang Phi Yen Duong, Nghia Trong Vo, Tuan Thanh Nguyen, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) constitutes a critical challenge in pharmaceutical development and accounts for over 50% of acute liver failure cases. This study employs deep learning (DL) methodologies for DILI prediction utilizing convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and multi-representation molecular imaging. We employ ResNet (18, 34, 50) and EfficientNet (B0-B3) architectures on three molecular image representations, such as normal images (NM), heatmap 1 (HM1) derived from Crippen logP contributions, and heatmap 2 (HM2) extracted from bioconcentration factor analysis. Experimental outcomes on 475 compounds using 4-fold cross-validation demonstrate that heatmap representations substantially outperform conventional molecular images. ResNet-34 attains optimal performance with HM2, achieving an AUC of 0.8853 and a Recall of 0.8410. This significantly enhances performance compared to conventional images (AUC: 0.8604, Recall: 0.8295). ResNet-34 and EfficientNet-B0 with HM1 exhibits superior functional group identification capabilities with a Recall of 0.8499 and 0.8682. Grad-CAM visualization illustrates that HM1 effectively emphasizes specific functional groups while HM2 facilitates comprehensive molecular analysis. Functional group analysis indicates that nitro groups (93.75%), sulfones (92.31%), and urea derivatives (91.67%) are associated with the highest DILI risk, while sulfur-containing moieties broadly serve as toxicity indicators. In contrast, iodine-containing compounds and ketones exhibit notably lower toxicity rates. Our multi-representation methodology exhibits competitive performance while delivering enhanced interpretability through explainable AI techniques. This framework presents considerable potential for pharmaceutical toxicity assessment and diminishes dependence on animal testing protocols.

title: Accelerating Time-Evolving Multiphysics Uncertainty Quantification of Millimeter Wave Components Using Reusable Preconditioning
authors: Hao-Xuan Zhang, Shi-Wei Guo, Lin-Peng Yu, Yi-Yao Wang, Hao Xie, Liang Zhou, Wen-Yan Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Transient electromagnetic (EM)–thermal– mechanical uncertainty quantification (UQ) is computationally demanding because it requires repeated solutions of many closely related EM systems across time steps, coupling iterations, and random samples. This article presents an efficient finite-element multiphysics framework that couples the frequency-domain Helmholtz equationwith time-domain heat conduction and evaluates thermomechanical responses through small-deformation thermoelasticity. To avoid repeated remeshing in large-sample studies, thin metallization layers are modeled by a reduced-dimensional thermal formulation on a fixed mesh. To accelerate the repeated EM solving process, a reusable LU-based preconditioning strategy is incorporated into the coupling loop. UQ is then performed by nonintrusive polynomial chaos expansion (PCE), from which confidence bands and Sobol sensitivity indices are extracted for both time- and frequency-domain quantities of interest (QoI). Numerical results on a substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) filter and a silicon-based D-band diplexer confirm that reusable preconditioning and fixed-mesh thin-layer modeling can substantially reduce the computational cost of large-sample transient UQ. This makes the proposed framework suitable for reliability-oriented analysis and design of millimeter-wave components operating under high-power and high-temperature conditions.

title: Analysis and Optimization of Fluid Antenna-Aided Cell-Free Massive MIMO With Imperfect CSI
authors: Feiyang Li, Qiang Sun, Dong Li, Jiayi Zhang, Chan-Byoung Chae, Kai-Kit Wong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF massive MIMO) is regarded as a promising technology for next-generation wireless communication systems. However, conventional CF massive MIMO systems typically employ fixed-position antennas (FPAs) at access points (APs), which limits the exploitation of spatial degrees of freedom (DoFs) for antenna position optimization. To address this issue, we propose the use of fluid antennas (FAs) in place of FPAs, enabling more DoFs at APs and leading to a novel FA-aided CF massive MIMO (FA-CF) architecture. In this paper, we investigate the uplink spectral efficiency (SE) of FA-CF systems with imperfect channel state information (CSI). We design a minimum mean-square error (MMSE)-based channel estimation scheme to estimate the aggregated channels between APs and user equipments (UEs). We further derive achievable SE expressions for both centralized and distributed processing schemes, including fully centralized processing (FCP), large-scale fading decoding (LSFD), and equal-gain decoding processing (EGDP). Moreover, we formulate a mean-square error (MSE) minimization problem based on the signal transmission model. To solve this problem, we develop an efficient algorithm that combines orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) with binary search to jointly optimize FA positions and the combining matrix. In addition, we propose a protective weak-ordering (PWO) strategy to enhance the SE of the FCP scheme. Numerical results demonstrate that FA-CF significantly outperforms conventional CF systems in terms of SE, even with a limited number of antennas, and maintains strong robustness under imperfect CSI or heavy UE loads by adaptively adjusting antenna positions. These results highlight FA-CF as a promising architecture offering enhanced SE and robustness for future wireless systems, particularly in scenarios where large-scale AP deployment is infeasible or cost-constrained.

title: Resolving the Buildup Dynamics of Noise-like Pulse in Mamyshev Oscillator
authors: Xiangzhen Huang, Ning Li, Tiantian Zhou, Xinrui Chu, Jia Cheng, Lili Miao, Chujun Zhao
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: The Mamyshev oscillator (MO) operating in the noise like pulse (NLP) regime can produce laser pulses with broad bandwidth and high pulse energy, which is crucial for many applications in optical communication, supercontinuum generation and micromachining. However, experimentally verifying the buildup dynamics of NLP in MO remains a significant challenge. Here, the all-fiberized ytterbium-doped MO has been designed to deliver NLP with 3-dB bandwidth exceeding 140 nm experimentally, and the buildup dynamics of NLP in the MO has been characterized by the time-stretch dispersive Fourier transform (TS-DFT) technique. Unlike the NLPs from conventional mode-locked fiber laser, the generation of NLP in MO is from the survived seed pulse in MO, and the transition from external seed pulses to stable NLP operation in MO involves four ultrafast phases, including seed pulse injection, amplified seed pulse, NLP generation, and stable NLP state. The numerical simulation results further validate the NLP buildup mechanisms in MO. The results can deepen the understanding of the NLP operation in MO, and may support the generation and manipulation of the pulse dynamics in MO.

title: Event-Triggered Impulsive Control for Nonlinear Systems: A Q-Learning-Enhanced Memory-Based Method
authors: Zidong Ai, Bao-Lin Zhang, Qing-Long Han, Lianghong Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: An intelligent memory-based event-triggered impulsive control (METIC) scheme is proposed to address the stabilization problem for a class of nonlinear systems while accounting for exponential convergence, dynamic performance, and control frequency. The contribution of the scheme is the incorporation of weighted historical data into the triggering condition, using both fixed thresholds and adaptive thresholds based on Q-learning. By utilizing the system states at the two most recent triggering instants to construct the triggering condition, several exponential stability criteria are first established via an iterative approach. Then, in the general case in which additional historical states are included, a comparison system approach is employed to derive new stability conditions. Furthermore, to adaptively tune the event-triggering thresholds and improve system performance, a Q-learning-based optimization algorithm is developed, and a set of easily verifiable stability conditions is derived within the framework of switched system theory. For both fixed-threshold and adaptive-threshold cases, Zeno behavior is rigorously excluded through theoretical analysis. Finally, comparative simulation results are presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Personalized Lattice Influence Maximization: Approximation Guarantees and Robustness
authors: Jun Wang, Qixin Zhang, Hongbin Zhang, Yu Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Recently, personalized influence maximization, which generalizes classic influence maximization by wisely allocating personalized discounts to users, showed its superiority in achieving cost-effective marketing plans over traditional influence maximization techniques. However, under continuous cost reaction scenarios, it is hard to obtain the approximation guarantee. To this end, we first propose a personalized lattice influence maximization (PLIM) problem, which can be approximately guaranteed through a greedy algorithm from an innovative perspective, i.e., match item selection with a knapsack constraint (MIS-K). Furthermore, to address the practical challenges associated with accurately learning user purchase probabilities, we extend the proposed PLIM framework in the form of robust optimization. We develop a robust algorithm that achieves a solution-dependent approximation guarantee and analyze the computational hardness of approximating RPLIM. By introducing an innovative Re-arrangement Sampling technique, we demonstrate that sampling can effectively mitigate uncertainty in purchase probabilities, enabling a strong robust ratio of (1 −ε)(1 − 1/e) with high probability. Experimental results on multiple benchmark datasets confirm the effectiveness and efficiency of our proposed methods.

title: VTMedSeg: Geometry-guided Vision-Text Model with Concept-aware Fusion for Medical Ultrasound Image Segmentation
authors: Huan Wan, Wujian Xu, Yiwen Zou, Jinshan Zeng, Xin Wei
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Medical ultrasound image segmentation is a vital diagnostic technique for identifying abnormalities, including tumors, cysts, and inflammation. To obtain accurate segmentation efficiently, many methods have been proposed. Amongst, vision–text models have shown promising progress; however, their performance is limited by the lack of high-quality paired text descriptions in ultrasound datasets. To address this issue, we propose VTMedSeg. In VTMedSeg, a geometry-guided text generator is developed to automatically synthesize medical descriptions by using connected component analysis and multi-dimensional geometric analysis. Then, the text features of these descriptions and the visual features of the corresponding ultrasound images are effectively integrated into a concept-aware cross-modal fusion module through multi-scale cross-modal alignment and medical knowledge graph modeling. Extensive experiments on four public medical ultrasound datasets demonstrate that VTMedSeg outperforms state-of-the-art vision–text models across multiple metrics.

title: Probabilistic Sparse Attention and Adaptive Domain Modeling for External Defect Detection in Transformers
authors: Ning Sun, Jiabao Zhang, Sicheng Zhu, Zihao Wang, Hucheng Xu, Penghui Liu, Guangan Ding, Ying Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The images of the external equipment of the transformer obtained by the cameras and other devices have complex backgrounds, and the small targets with minor defects or small images have low resolution, resulting in a decrease in the recognition rate. To address the environment with complex feature backgrounds, this paper proposes an adaptive detection network for external transformer defects. The network architecture incorporates a specialized stage structure, an enhanced transformer layer, an efficient layer aggregation mechanism, and a real-time detection transformer decoder. Initially, raw images of transformer equipment are utilized as input. The primary module, designated as the probabilistic sparse transformer layer, integrates a novel encoder module as the core channel mechanism. This module includes a patch embedding layer, a sparse multi-head probabilistic attention layer, a residual connection structure, a layer normalization operation, and a feedforward layer. This design efficiently extracts irregularly varying feature information under limited fault samples while globally capturing data anomalies induced by external defects. Additionally, the incorporation of an adaptive activation function augments the expressive capacity of nonlinear mappings. The piecewise linear properties of the function prevent extreme fluctuations in output values, thereby mitigating gradient vanishing issues while enhancing expressive capability. Furthermore, the integration of domain-adaptive batch normalization technology reduces feature distribution discrepancies between source and target domains. This study uses real engineering data sets, and the results show that DEIM-PPSformer, based on DEIM, increased mAP@0.5 by 3.1%, reaching 92.1%. mAP@0.5-0.95 increased by 5.8%, reaching 71.6%. This indicates that the proposed method has a significant advantage in detecting defects in external transformer equipment.

title: Modular Synthesis of Coupled-Resonator Filters Using Divide-and-Conquer Paradigms
authors: Qiuyi Wu, Feng Tang, Xiaoqi Yang, Zihan Wang, Yimin Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a one-step method for decomposing a network into cascaded independent subnetworks, contrasting with the conventional “gather-in” approach and following the divide-and-conquer (D&C) paradigm. By modeling the network with ABCD matrices and leveraging the invariance of external Y-parameters, the decomposition reduces to a simple system of linear equations, requiring minimal numerical effort. The method applies to all typical configurations—including fully canonical or nonfully canonical networks, with or without frequency-dependent couplings (FDCs)—and can be extended recursively to handle complex high-order networks with arbitrary orders and transmission-zero allocations. Its effectiveness is demonstrated through various examples, including a dual-band filter with a hybrid cascaded–parallel topology, a structure that challenges traditional analytical techniques. Simulation and measurement results confirm the robustness of the algorithm and its potential for practical engineering applications.

title: Design and Characterization of a Tendon-Driven Soft Robotic Gripper for Lightweight Manipulation
authors: Matthew Lee, Indie Heinemann-Rueda, Yen-Lin Han
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication, computational, and experimental validation of a tendon-driven soft robotic gripper for lightweight manipulation tasks with relevance to assistive hand function. The gripper features a bio-inspired three-joint finger design fabricated as a single monolithic piece from thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) via fused deposition modeling (FDM) 3D printing, eliminating assembly requirements and potential failure points. Comprehensive finite element analysis using hyperelastic Mooney-Rivlin material models validates the mechanical design through full-finger and individual joint simulations. Full-finger analysis under 1 N tensile load characterizes overall bending behavior, while individual joint simulations under a 0.1 N load reveal detailed stress distributions. The gripper integrates with a HiWonder xArm robotic arm platform in a three-finger radial configuration, actuated by a single servo motor via fishing line tendons. Preprogrammed motion sequences enable execution of manipulation tasks without requiring low-level servo control. Experimental pick-and-place testing across objects of varying weights and geometries demonstrates the gripper's functional manipulation capabilities, with qualitative observations confirming passive conformability to object contours and stable grasping for cylindrical, spherical, cubic, and irregular objects. The computational and experimental findings validate the design's fundamental grasping mechanics and establish the viability of single-piece 3D-printed tendon-driven grippers as an accessible platform for future soft robotic assistive technology research.

title: A Vision-Based Four-Cable-Driven Positioning System with Cascade Control: Experimental Evaluation
authors: Gianmarco Humpiri, Piero Reyes, Medard Quispe, Elvis J. Alegria
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Effective water quality monitoring in large-scale aquaculture remains a challenge due to the limited spatial coverage of fixed sensors and the operational constraints of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). To address this, this paper presents the design and validation of a scalable four-cable-driven parallel robot (CDPR) with a four-corner architecture tailored for autonomous sensor deployment. The system implements a cascade control strategy, integrating a low-level motor current loop with a high-level vision-based kinematic loop to ensure precise end-effector positioning under dynamic uncertainties. Although the mechanical design supports five degrees of freedom (5-DoF), this study focuses on validating the translational performance required for environmental sensing. Experimental validation was conducted using square and circular trajectories under varying payloads. Results demonstrate that the system maintains a consistently low normalized tracking error even under maximum load conditions, confirming the robustness and feasibility of the proposed architecture as a cost-effective solution for smart aquaculture monitoring.

title: Stomata-Inspired Reconfigurable Square Ring Slot Antennas for 5G Applications
authors: Buthaina Al Siyabi, Hassan Al-Lawati, Mohammed Bait-Suwailam, Joseph A. Jervase
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: This paper presents two antennas of a bioinspired reconfigurable square-ring slot for sub- 6 GHz 5 G applications, functionally motivated by the dynamic opening and closing behavior of plant stomata. By employing a single PIN diode, the proposed antennas simultaneously enable frequency and polarization reconfiguration, thereby reducing circuit complexity, biasing overhead, and power consumption. The antennas are fabricated on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 6 - m m}$</tex> thick FR4 substrate with a relative permittivity of 4.4 and a loss tangent of 0.02. The first antenna is for frequency-reconfigurable mode, the antenna operates in a triple-band state at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.6 \text{GHz}, 4.95 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 6.85 GHz when the diode is ON, and in a dual-band state at 3.55 GHz and 6.8 GHz when the diode is OFF. Furthermore, the second antenna is for polarization reconfiguration, which is achieved by switching between linear (ON state) and circular polarization (OFF state), with a minimum axial ratio of 0.62 dB at 3.5 GHz. Owing to their compact geometry, low implementation complexity, and multifunctional reconfigurability, the proposed biomimetic antennas represent an efficient and cost-effective solution for adaptive sub-6 GHz 5G and Internet-of-Things (IoT) wireless systems.

title: Dynamic Beam Position Partitioning and Layout Adjustment in LEO Beam Hopping Systems
authors: Lu Wang, Jifeng Liu, Yao Zhou, Tingting Wang, Cheng Wang, Weidong Wang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: In Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication systems, beam-hopping (BH) technology significantly enhances spectrum resource utilization by dynamically activating different beam positions (BPs) across distinct time slots. However, in addition to the fact that BH decisions can affect system performance, the number of BPs can also impact the system latency and their layout influences system throughput performance. Therefore, this paper proposes a dynamic BP partitioning and layout adjustment method for LEO satellite communication systems. First, user adjacency is analysed via a KD-tree to determine initial BP centers, and a minimum enclosing circle algorithm is applied to maximize user coverage under radius constraints. Then, a virtual force field algorithm is proposed to adjust the BP layout by modelling the interactions between overlapping beams. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to existing methods, this approach reduces algorithmic complexity while enhancing system latency and throughput performance.

title: DDoS Attack Detection in Cloud Systems Using a Hybrid Stacked Ensemble
authors: Nematullah Khan, Raad Alharthi, Fahad Alzahrani, Mourad Benmalek, Abdelouahid Derhab
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks remain a major threat to cloud infrastructure, while many existing defenses provide only coarse protocol-level detection and struggle to adapt to evolving attack patterns. This paper proposes an AI-driven stacked ensemble for cloud DDoS detection and finegrained TCP-based attack typing. The framework uses two stages: (i) an SVM for binary flow classification (benign vs. non-benign) and (ii) three deep meta-learners (LSTM, CNN, and Autoencoder) to classify 17 TCP-based DDoS variants and suspicious traffic, with majority voting for the final decision. Experiments on the AWS-collected BCCC-cPacket-Cloud-DDoS2024 dataset show 94.28% accuracy for the SVM, 94.20% for the best meta-learner, and 92.72% accuracy for the ensemble, achieving a weighted F1-score of 0.92. Results indicate strong performance on volumetric attacks and stable behavior across diverse categories, supporting the practicality of heterogeneous ensembles for cloud DDoS defense.

title: Pixels to Pursuit: Reinforcement Learning-Based Control Strategies for UAV-AGV Collaborative Systems
authors: Ankit Mehra, Darshankumar Prajapati, Pushkar Kumar, Ashish Rana, Urvashi Goswami, Amit Shukla
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This paper presents reinforcement learning-based control strategies for vision-based UAV tracking by AGVs. We developed custom YOLOv8 object detection models achieving 98 % accuracy and implemented DQN and DDPG controllers trained in simulation environments. Experimental validation demonstrates DDPG outperforms PID, SMC, and DQN controllers with superior tracking accuracy and reduced settling time. The approach enables robust UAV-AGV collaboration in GPS-denied environments with potential applications in agriculture and search-and-rescue operations.

title: Adaptive First-Person Shooter: Real-Time Game Difficulty Adjustment Based on Player Stress Levels
authors: Georges Chaiban, Nabil Moussa, Bilal Zahraman, Joseph Azar
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Video games, especially first-person shooter (FPS) games, often involve intense gameplay that can elicit aggressive responses. This paper proposes a stress-adaptive FPS game that dynamically adjusts gameplay based on the player's real-time emotional state. The system combines speech analysis via a large language model (LLM), heart rate monitoring, and in-game performance metrics to compute an aggression score using a weighted algorithm. Based on this score, the game applies inverse difficulty scaling and, when extreme aggression is detected, transitions the player to a calming environment with relaxation tasks. Grounded in established psychological frameworks including the General Aggression Model and Flow Theory, this work demonstrates how real-time multi-modal emotion monitoring can be integrated into the gaming domain to create an adaptive, personalized experience focused on player well-being.

title: A Hyper-Heuristic Framework for Human-In-The-Loop Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Kassem Danach, Jomana Al Haj Hassan, Samir Haddad, Jinane Sayah, Merhej Joseph, Kallab Chadi
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Human-in-the-loop combinatorial optimization problems are characterized by dynamic objectives, evolving constraints, and frequent human intervention, posing significant challenges to classical automated optimization methods. This paper proposes a categorized selection hyper-heuristic framework designed to explicitly incorporate human feedback into the optimization process. Low-level heuristics are organized into constructive, improvement, perturbation, and reconstructive categories, enabling adaptive control of search behavior in response to both algorithmic performance and human acceptance signals. The framework integrates a two-stage heuristic selection mechanism, interaction-aware acceptance criteria, and termination conditions tailored to non-stationary optimization environments. The proposed approach is evaluated on a nurse rostering problem, a representative NP-hard combinatorial optimization task. Experimental results indicate that the proposed framework reduces the average number of human interventions by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5 \%}$</tex> compared to a noncategorized hyper-heuristic, while improving solution stability by more than 50 % and maintaining competitive objective values. These results demonstrate that explicit heuristic categorization and acceptance-aware control provide effective mechanisms for addressing the challenges of human-in-the-loop combinatorial optimization.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for RADAR-Based Warhead Target Classification Under Clutter Conditions
authors: Md. Abdur Rouf, Md. Selim Hossain, Mst. Azmira Khanam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning method that effectively classifies warhead-type targets from non-warhead-type targets using a simulated radar dataset. Key radar characteristics, including Radar Cross Section (RCS), Doppler shift, speed, angle, frequency, micro-Doppler signatures, and range profiles, were incorporated into a synthetic dataset. The dataset is generated in two different scenarios in realistic conditions with additional environmental clutter, such as rain and urban reflections, and another one in ideal conditions without clutter. A Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier showed notable performance degradation 53.75 % when trained on cluttered data, but it achieved nearperfect accuracy of 99.5% on the clean dataset. The Principal Component Analysis was used as a feature optimization method to suppress the clutter, including variance, and reduce feature redundancy. The classification accuracy for the clutter condition increased to 85.7 % with the incorporation of PCA. These findings underscore the importance of feature optimization in enhancing radar-based classification performance in challenging operating environments.

title: SCIGaussian-D: Dynamic Scene Reconstruction from a Single Snapshot Compressive Image
authors: Yunhao Li, Yuze Yang, Yanan Hu, Xiaoyue Li, Yong Tang, Xin Yuan, Peidong Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: In this paper, we explore the potential of snapshot compressive imaging (SCI) for dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from a single temporal compressed image. SCI is a low-cost imaging technique that captures high-dimensional information-such as temporal data-using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> sensors and coded masks, significantly reducing data bandwidth while offering inherent privacy advantages. While recent advances in Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have enabled 3D reconstruction from SCI measurements, these methods are fundamentally limited to static scenes and fail to generalize to dynamic content. To address this, we propose SCIGaussian-D, a novel framework that enables dynamic 3D reconstruction from a single SCI image. Our method represents the scene with 3D Gaussians defined in a canonical space and models motion using learnable deformation fields. By incorporating the SCI imaging model into the training loop, SCIGaussian-D directly reconstructs the dynamic 3D scene and recovers the corresponding camera motion from a single SCI. We evaluate our method on both synthetic and real SCI datasets, demonstrating significant improvements in reconstruction quality over existing baselines. Our results establish a new state of the art for dynamic scene reconstruction within the SCI framework, paving the way for practical applications in high-speed imaging and real-time scene rendering.

title: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: GALA: Guided Attention with Language Alignment for Open Vocabulary Gaussian Splatting
authors: Elena Alegret, Kunyi Li, Sen Wang, Siyun Liang, Michael Niemeyer, Stefano Gasperini, Nassir Navab, Federico Tombari
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: 3D scene reconstruction and understanding have gained increasing popularity, yet existing methods struggle to capture fine-grained, language-aware 3D representations from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> images. In this paper, we present GALA, a novel framework for open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding with 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). GALA distills a scenespecific 3D instance feature field via self-supervised contrastive learning. To further extend this to generalized language feature fields, we introduce a core contribution of GALA, a cross-attention module with two learnable codebooks that encode view-independent semantic embeddings. This design not only ensures intra-instance feature similarity but also supports seamless 2D and 3D open-vocabulary queries. It reduces memory consumption by avoiding perGaussian high-dimensional feature learning. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate GALA's remarkable open-vocabulary performance on both <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> and 3D.

title: MawaNet: A Custom Deep Classifier for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Md Nuruzzaman, Afsana Nadia, Md Masum Billal, Khan Md Shibli Nomani, Md Tarikul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Credit card fraud detection is still challenging in the financial industry. It indicates the necessity of modern machine learning techniques to detect abnormal transactions without erroneously classifying normal account activities. The existing studies heavily rely on traditional machine learning and deep learning approaches, with a lack of optimization and regularization for tabular data. We propose a deep learning architecture called MawaNet for the detection of credit card fraudulent activity with proper regularization to work effectively on tabular data. The extensive experiments have been applied to ten different machine learning algorithms. MawaNet (our proposed model) has been compared with the most popular machine learning models, such as AdaBoost Classifier, Decision Tree Classifier, Gaussian Naïve Bayes, Gradient Boosting Classifier, Logistic Regression, Passive Aggressive Classifier, Quadratic Discriminant Analysis, Ridge Classifier, Self-Training Classifier, and SGD Classifier. The performance has been evaluated through precision, recall, F1-score, and accuracy. MawaNet achieves the highest score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.99 \%)$</tex> in all performance evaluation metrics. The proposed architecture outperforms other traditional methods. The Decision Tree Classifier performs well with the score of 99.78 % across all metrics. These results demonstrate that the deep learning architecture is able to capture the non-linear and obscured patterns in transactional data, offering financial institutions a strong candidate for use in real-time systems to detect fraud.

title: Optimizing Dropout in LLM Training: Performance Comparison of Fusion and Overlap
authors: Haiyue Ma, Jian Liu, Ronny Krashinsky
publish: Abstracts of the 2026 ACM SIGMETRICS International Conference on Measurement and Modeling of Computer Systems
