title: Fault Analysis and Improvement of the Load Reducer for Bolter Miner
authors: Xiaoxia Liu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: The load reducer of a bolter miner is characterized by high load-bearing capacity, strong reliability, and complex internal transmission and external structures. However, factors such as load shocks, harsh underground operating conditions, and difficult maintenance severely affect its reliable operation. This paper addresses issues including static seal oil leakage, dynamic seal oil leakage, spline gear rolling, torque shaft fracture, and misalignment between input and output shafts caused by deformation of the motor connecting plate. Comprehensive improvement measures are proposed, focusing on material selection, heat treatment processes, manufacturing and assembly, as well as maintenance of the internal components of the load reducer, aiming to enhance its reliability and service life. Through loading tests on a transmission test bench and underground industrial trials, the precisely designed load reducer has demonstrated adaptability to impact and heavy-load conditions, meeting all performance requirements for the bolter miner.

title: Defect Detection of Photovoltaic Cells Based on Improved YOLO11n
authors: Zijian Zhou, Jiaojie Li, Shiyuan Yu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenges of low-contrast, tiny, and slender defects in photovoltaic electroluminescence (EL) images, this paper presents an improved lightweight model based on YOLO11n. Traditional detection methods often suffer from severe feature information loss during feature fusion and insufficient localization accuracy for slender micro-defects, leading to frequent missed and false detections. To alleviate these issues, the content-aware dynamic upsampling (DySample) module is introduced to enhance multi-scale feature fusion and preserve fine-grained details of tiny defects. Meanwhile, the Focaler-Shape-IoU loss is adopted to optimize bounding box regression and improve localization accuracy for irregular and slender defects. Experimental results on the PVEL-AD dataset show that the proposed model achieves 92.6% mAP@0.5 and 71.4% mAP@0.5:0.95, which are 1.2% and 0.8% higher than the baseline YOLO11n, respectively, with only a slight increase of 0.01M in parameters and unchanged GFLOPs. The proposed model effectively improves detection accuracy while maintaining high efficiency.

title: A Structured Three-Stage Pipeline for Compositional Text-to-Image Generation with Editable Layouts and Object-Wise Attention
authors: Anshu Raj
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: When the prompt is prompting several objects in the scene, text-to-image models still fail because they often mismatch attributes, place objects incorrectly, or are left without important relationships. That makes them inadequate for real-world design, visualization, or creative work where users require precise, predictable control. Existing strategies, such as prompt tricks, attention tweaks, or layout-guided diffusion help in some ways, but they tend to fail: they not ground attributes properly, they deteriorate on complex scenes, or they become challenging to edit after generation has begun. In order to overcome these limitations, we provide a three-stage compositional synthesis pipeline for fine-grained, variable image generation. First, a language model looks at the prompt in terms of clean, sequenced presentation of entities, attributes, and spatial relation. Next, the system uses this structure to predict an editable intermediate layout and an attribute map for each object. Finally, the image is generated by diffusion model accompanied by attention-masking system controlling the level of objects during synthesis. The user can then edit layouts before rendering, ensuring that the final output matches the prompt and the structure. Our approach consistently improves object count accuracy, attribute accuracy and spatial coherence within experiments, and at the same time without compromising visual quality.

title: Linguistic Biomarkers for Dementia Detection: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Models
authors: Nandu Dammalapati
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Dementia is one of the most common types of progressive neurodegenerative disorders, with Alzheimer being one of the most prevalent. Currently, research and diagnostic methods often explore only clinical interviews, cognitive testing, and neuroimaging approaches that can be difficult to receive due to their cost and inaccessibility. This study aims to evaluate the effectiveness of machine learning-based pipeline for early dementia detection through speech transcript analysis. Utilizing the Pitt Corpus from the DementiaBank database, patient interactions with clinicians are transcribed for training and testing. The dataset is composed of 1541 samples, with 955 healthy and 586 samples with dementia. These trials are preprocessed and transformed through multiple feature extraction strategies including linguistic feature engineering, TF-IDF vectorization, Word2Vec, GloVe, and sentence embeddings. The dataset was split into 75-25 train-test split; specifically, 75% of the data used for training and 25% used for testing. The models utilized were Support Vector Machines (SVM), KNearest Neighbors (KNN), and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) were evaluated using cross-validation across different embedding configurations. Model performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The ANN consistently achieves top-tier performance, with accuracies surpassing 97% across embedding types. Suspecting data leakage through these highly-accurate results, the trials were isolated in preprocessing and then deemed the same results. This research demonstrates the potential of computational linguistics for scalable, early-stage dementia screening, and possible real-world clinical deployment.

title: A Topology-Oriented Review of GaN-Based High-Frequency DC-DC Converters
authors: Nurhafizah Elias, Nurhakimah Mohd Mukhtar
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Gallium nitride (GaN) power devices are an advanced technology for high-frequency, high-efficiency DC–DC converters, due to their fast switching capability, low parasitic capacitance, and negligible reverse-recovery loss. GaN devices in DC–DC converters can operate at megahertz switching frequencies, improving efficiency and achieving higher power density than conventional silicon devices. Previous studies indicate that the use of GaN devices to improve system performance is strongly influenced by the converter topology rather than by device replacement alone. This paper presents a topology-oriented analysis of GaN devices in high-frequency DC–DC converters, including non-isolated, isolated single-ended, and isolated multiport converter topologies. A comparative analysis evaluates these topologies in terms of efficiency, soft-switching capability, switching-frequency scalability, and the benefits of using GaN devices across different converter topologies. The analysis of soft-switching topologies, active-clamp, and LLC resonant converters exhibits strong synergy with GaN devices, achieving high efficiency and power density at high switching frequencies. In contrast, bridge-based and multiport converter topologies offer greater flexibility in power flow but constraints in control complexity and electromagnetic interference (EMI). This review also highlights GaN-based isolated multiport converters as a promising direction for future high-density power conversion applications.

title: Correct Wrong Path Simulation
authors: Chrysanthos Pepi, Krishnam Tibrewala, Bhargav Reddy Godala, Sankara Prasad Ramesh, Alberto Ros, Daniel A. Jiménez, Gilles A. Pokam, Paul V. Gratz
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Modern OoO CPUs employ deep pipelines with high branch misprediction recovery penalties. Instructions speculatively executed along mispredicted paths can significantly alter microarchitectural state. During design space exploration, architects often rely on trace-driven simulators, which are significantly faster than execution-driven models but trade accuracy for speed. Despite this benefit, trace-driven simulation often fails to adequately model the effects of wrong-path execution because traces are typically collected only from the correct-path. While prior work can accurately model wrong-path effects on the instruction stream, it often makes unrealistic assumptions when modeling the impact on the data stream. In this work, we examine the effects of wrong-path execution and present an infrastructure for enabling its modeling in a tracedriven simulator. Our analysis shows that wrong-path execution extensively affects structures on both the instruction and data sides, yielding performance variations ranging from $-3.6 \%$ to $85.7 \%$ compared to a baseline that ignores these effects. To benefit the research community and enhance the accuracy of simulators, we provide our traces and tracing utility. We aim for this to encourage industry to provide wrong-path traces generated by internal simulators, enabling fast academic research without exposing industry proprietary IP.

title: LightAD: A Lightweight Vision-Based Autonomous Driving System
authors: Yunge Li, Zhaodong Zhou, Shaibal Saha, Ali Irshayyid, Keer Chen, Lanyu Xu, Jun Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Autonomous driving systems deployed in the real world suffer from excessive hardware redundancy and prohibitive deployment barriers. To address these challenges, LightAD is proposed as a lightweight, vision-based autonomous driving system framework tailored for simple, low-speed environments such as confined work zones and low-speed logistics. Diverging from industrial-grade solutions that rely on costly proprietary sensors and high-performance computing, LightAD implements system-level simplification. By leveraging consumer-grade hardware and lightweight algorithms for perception, planning, and control, LightAD significantly reduces costs and integration complexity while satisfying necessary autonomous driving functionality. Field trials at the Intelligent Ground Vehicle Competition on a physical vehicle confirmed the feasibility of LightAD and revealed practical implementation lessons. These results indicate that by combining low-cost general-purpose hardware with lightweight algorithms, LightAD effectively supports fundamental autonomous driving functionality in simple, well-defined, and low-speed environments, offering a feasible engineering pathway for cost-effective deployment of autonomous driving.

title: Evaluating the Risk of Storing and Transporting the Hydrogen Energy in Oman Using Multi-Criteria Decision Making
authors: Sara Al Hosni, Sina Sayardoost Tabrizi, Reza Babazadeh, Mehdi A. Kamran
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Hydrogen energy is widely recognized as a key component of the global transition toward low-carbon energy systems; however, its large-scale deployment remains constrained by safety risks associated with storage and transportation. These risks are particularly critical in high-temperature regions such as the Sultanate of Oman, which is emerging as a major hydrogen producer. Despite growing interest in hydrogen safety, existing studies largely focus on technical failures while overlooking the interdependent and systemic nature of risk factors. To address this gap, this study proposes a hybrid multi-criteria decision-making approach based on the integration of the Decision-Making Trial and Evaluation Laboratory (DEMATEL) and the Analytic Network Process (ANP) to evaluate the Risk of Storing and Transporting Hydrogen (RSTH) in Oman. The proposed framework models causal relationships among five major risk dimensions (personnel, storage, transportation, environmental, and management risks) using expert-based assessments. The results reveal that management-related factors, particularly the effectiveness of feedback mechanisms, dynamic monitoring, and standardized management, act as primary causal drivers within the RSTH system. These factors exert significant influence on downstream technical, human, and environmental risks, indicating that many technical failures are consequences rather than root causes of risk. The findings highlight the critical role of integrated management strategies in reducing hydrogen-related risks and demonstrate that improving management structures can lead to a systemic reduction in overall risk levels.

title: Traffic Sign Detection Using CNN in Adverse Imaging Environments
authors: Prathibha R, Yogaraja G S R, Amrutha N, Bhavya Shree D, Charitha N R, Harika K S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traffic sign detection plays a very important role in modern transportation technologies, especially in systems designed for autonomous driving and driver assistance. These signs provide timely guidance that helps vehicles make safe and informed decisions on the road. Earlier recognition methods relied heavily on manually designed features, which often struggled when signs appeared blurred, partially hidden, or affected by changes in lighting or weather. In this work, a CNN-based architecture is proposed for the detection and classification of traffic signs using the Indian Traffic Sign Computer Vision dataset. The results indicate that CNN-based detection systems are well-suited for real-world deployment in driver assistance systems and autonomous driving, contributing to enhanced road safety and efficient traffic management.

title: Intelligent IoT-Driven Predictive Indoor Air Quality Management Framework for Educational Environments using Multi-Sensor Integration and Cloud-based Machine Learning Analytics
authors: Sarmisha Mary G, Shifani J, Hemalatha R
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The deterioration of the indoor air quality (IAQ) in schools is a major causative health problem and interferes with the efficiency of learning. The paper will describe an IoTbased IAQ management system based on the ESP32 to monitor in real-time, where the MQ2, MQ7, and MQ135 sensors are required to identify the presence of smoke, carbon monoxide, and dangerous gases. The sensor data is sent to a cloud solution where MATLAB is used to analyses the sensor data and machine learning models predict the trends of IAQ and preempt anomalies. The system makes automatic warning and suggestions to assure the best air quality, which allows effective prompt interventions. Complex analytics are used to detect patterns, correlations and source of pollutants enabling administrators to adopt specific action strategies. The experimental validation proves to be very accurate to detect pollutants and predict them. This open-source framework is scalable and provides real-time, intelligent decision support that can result into healthier school environments and provide the future basis of school IAQ optimization.

title: Performance Evaluation of Reliability Indices in Islanded Microgrids Under Renewable Uncertainty
authors: Ali Q. Al-Shetwi
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper presents a reliability assessment of a standalone hybrid microgrid designed to supply a 5 MW community load using photovoltaic (PV), wind turbine, battery energy storage system (BESS), and a backup diesel generator. The system operates in off-grid mode, requiring high reliability to ensure uninterrupted supply. Six key reliability indices are evaluated: Loss of Load Probability (LOLP), Loss of Load Expectation (LOLE), Expected Energy Not Supplied (EENS), System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI), System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI), and Customer Average Interruption Duration Index (CAIDI). A baseline system is first analyzed without optimization, followed by comparative reliability improvement using three metaheuristic algorithms: Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO). Simulation results show that optimization significantly reduces unserved energy, with EENS decreasing from 420 MWh/year in the baseline case to 95 MWh/year under GWO. Similarly, LOLE improves from 62 hours/year to 14 hours/year and both SAIDI and SAIFI are noticeably reduced, reflecting a lower impact on customers. The results highlight the potential of metaheuristic approaches in enhancing the reliability of off-grid hybrid microgrids for rural and remote applications.

title: Novel Insights into the Role of Turbulent Fluxes and Diabatic Heating in Shaping the Spatiotemporal Structure of the Hadley Cell
authors: Tanay Panja
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Due to global warming, the Hadley cell is expanding, significantly impacting environmental patterns worldwide. The effects of eddy momentum flux, turbulent heat flux, and diabatic heating on the Hadley cell are understudied issues, and could provide insights into the structure and behavior of global air circulation patterns. However, the limitations of current methods do not fully capture the time-dependent structure of the Hadley cell because they use ideal distributions of the eddy fluxes and diabatic heating in simulations. We propose functional distributions for eddy momentum and heat flux parameterizations using a combination of symmetric and asymmetric components to more effectively model the non-linearities. For this, we developed a novel computational framework using the mixed finite difference-spectral representation to model the spatiotemporal structure of the Hadley cell. Our results demonstrate that a) increased diabatic heating led to pronounced long-term Hadley cell expansion, b) asymmetric components of the eddy momentum flux significantly influence the strength of the Hadley cell, and c) a larger asymmetric component of the turbulent heat flux changes the meridional position. These findings reveal the complex inter-play between atmospheric heat transport and climate forcing mechanisms, which we believe could provide critical insights for improving climate model accuracy and long-term weather prediction capabilities.

title: AI -Driven Cloud Storage Latency Reduction
authors: Kshama L, Rida Fathima, Varsha S, Dhanyashree, Manjula L, Pratyaksha S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: systems support data-intensive applications such as real-time analytics, multimedia streaming, and IoT platforms. Despite advances in cloud infrastructure, latency remains a major challenge, particularly under dynamic workloads where traditional static and rule-based optimization techniques fall short. This paper proposes an AI-driven framework for cloud storage optimization that integrates real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and intelligent resource management. Machine learning models analyze latency metrics to detect performance trends, predict congestion, and enable proactive optimization. Experimental evaluation using simulated workloads demonstrates a latency reduction of up to 28% during peak periods, along with improved scalability and system stability. These results highlight the potential of AI-based approaches to transform cloud storage management into a proactive and self-optimizing process.

title: Hybrid CNN Architectures for Bengali Pitha Recognition: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Dewan Rita Faria Prachi, Pabon Shaha, Muhammad Minoar Hossain, Yadab Sutradhar, Md. Moradul Siddique, Md. Sadiq Iqbal
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The Bangla terms pitha, pithe, and peetha refer to traditional native foods of Bangladesh and certain regions of India, particularly in areas where Bangla is the predominant language. Pithas hold a significant place in Bengali and Bangladeshi culture, encompassing a diverse range of traditional delicacies. Accurately classifying different types of pitha is essential for advancing the food industry and preserving cultural heritage. The goal of this research is to create an effective model for image-based classification of traditional foods, specifically focusing on various types of pitha. With the rapid progress of deep learning, remarkable advancements have been achieved in computer vision and related fields. Building on this, the present work provides a comprehensive evaluation of CNN-based techniques for recognizing and classifying pitha images.In this study, several deep learning architectures—ResNet50, DenseNet121, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2—were implemented alongside two hybrid combinations: MobileNetV2 + DenseNet121 and InceptionV3 + DenseNet121. The hybrid model achieved a robust performance with an accuracy of 97.00% across diverse testing scenarios, underscoring its potential for real-world applications. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of CNN-based methods in food image classification, contributing to both technological innovation and the preservation of cultural heritage. Furthermore, this research supports future developments in educational tools, automated food labeling systems, and digital food preservation initiatives.

title: A Multi-Level Debiasing Framework for Mitigating Cultural Bias in Reviews Sentiment Analysis
authors: Bhavya Sree S, Lena Merin Viju, Hemanth K S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Online review sentiment analysis systems are common platforms used to derive insights out of online reviews, but commonly, they are biased in culture because of language variation in linguistic expression, intensity of emotion and rating behavior is also varied between different geographical areas. Biases may cause biased sentiment predictions especially the underrepresented cultural groups. In an attempt to reduce cultural bias in airlines review sentiment classification, the Multi-level Debiasing Framework presented in this paper combines interventions on the data, model, and prediction levels. We first compare the performance of baseline classifiers such as Logistic Regression, Linear Support Vector Machine, and MultinomialNaive Bayes using Indian domestic airline review data and Western airline review data, and measure cultural bias by using measures of boundary probability (BP), true positive rate (TPR) gap, and false positive rate (FPR) gap. The findings of this experiment indicates that there are significant differences in favor of the Western reviews in the scenario of baseline models. The suggested unbiased framework integrates culturally balanced sampling of data, linguistic tailored enhancement, sample weighting, and culture-sensitive probability calibration. It was proven that the offered method helps to decrease BP by more than 90 per cent and significantly decrease the difference in TPR and FPR and retain comparable classification rates. Culturally consistent sentiment prediction across groups is also confirmed by qualitative analysis. The findings demonstrate the usefulness of multi-level mitigation in constructing just and culturally resilient sentiment analysis models and provide useful implications on utilizing equitable NLP solutions in an international setting.

title: ChaosRetina: Chaos-theory Based Feature Extraction for Multi-Label Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Sindhu P Menon, Subash S, S A Shanjith, Harshith K N, Yashas P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The early-stage retinal diseases usually manifest in subtle and hard-to-recognize visual patterns, which are challenging for even the experienced clinicians to identify successfully. Despite the success of CNNs in automatic retinal image analysis, they struggle to recognize faint and irregular pathological cues, especially in a multi-label and heavily imbalanced context. Contribution: This paper introduces ChaosRetina, a two-stage deep learning architecture which incorporates chaos theory–inspired feature transformation speaker identification module into traditional CNN architectures. The proposed ChaosFEX constructs deep features by using iterative logistic map dynamics allowing complex minuscule details to be acknowledged after being suppressed by traditional activations. Experiments on the RFMiD multi-disease fundus dataset show that the proposed method achieves AUROC of 0.957 in binary disease detection and macro-AUROC of 0.869 in multi-label classification, outperforming multiple CNN based baseline methods. Extensive ablation studies show consistent improvement from ChaosFEX, especially for rare diseases. The findings indicate that chaos-based nonlinear transforms can be effective combined with deep learning representations for medical image analysis.

title: LaViCoN: Language and Vision Cooperative Navigation for Assistive Mobile Robots
authors: Tony Yang, Ren Zhong, Weisong Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: This paper presents LaViCoN: a lightweight modular framework that enables indoor robots to perform real-time navigation from natural language commands such as "go to the door" or "go around that chair." The system focuses on navigation toward reference items or objects indoors and heavily prioritizes real-world deployment. LaViCoN consists of three main modules: natural language understanding (NLU), visual perception, and robot action. Since large end-to-end vision-language models require massive amounts of training data and are essentially black boxes, LaViCoN focuses on breaking down the task into interpretable parts for a relatively lightweight deployment. To enable NLU, a custom-modified BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) architecture designed for slot-filling is fine-tuned on a dataset that is synthetically created in this work by using GPT-4o, achieving a performance of 93% semantic frame accuracy. To allow the robot to visually perceive its environment, the system uses YOLOv8 object detection. The robot action module performs specific movements (approaching objects, avoiding obstacles, navigating between objects, etc.) through mapping the different slot values received from the NLU module to objects detected by the visual perception module. Lastly, LaViCoN is tested on a physical vehicle indoors with 20 novel indoor navigation tasks, achieving an average of 87% task success rate. The results show that a resource-efficient system like LaViCoN can be practical and achieve competitive performance in the real world, which offers an alternative to solutions that require vast resources or data.

title: AI in the Antenna Design Life Cycle: An AI-Assisted Systematic Review
authors: Ike Mafa, Simon Winberg
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: The increasing integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in antenna design presents opportunities for optimizing the design process. This paper presents a comprehensive AI-assisted systematic review of the use of AI in the antenna design life cycle. A Systematic Mapping Study (SMS) is first employed to map the landscape of AI applications in antenna design, identifying key research areas, trends, and gaps. The SMS categorizes studies into three primary domains: AI for Optimization, AI for Synthesis, and AI for Performance Prediction. In the second phase, a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) is conducted to critically evaluate and synthesize empirical findings from the selected studies. The SLR examines the various AI techniques used in each step of the lifecycle. The results from both methods provide a comprehensive overview of AI’s current applications in the field, highlighting key challenges and opportunities for future research. This hybrid approach, combining AI-assisted SMS and SLR, offers both a broad mapping of AI applications and a deep, critical analysis of their real-world effectiveness, making it a valuable contribution to the field of AI-enhanced antenna design and AI-assisted systematic reviews.

title: Multi-Axis Vision Transformer with Optimised Temporal Convolution for Oral Diagnosis
authors: I. Sherif Baig, K. Vivekanandan
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Oral cancer now represents a major worldwide public health threat. Cell mutations in the lips or mouth cause oral cancer. The prevalence and incidence frequencies of oral cancer are rising globally, despite progress in research and therapy. There has been no substantial improvement recently in the recognition of oral cancer, leaving it a long-standing challenge in biomedicine. In oncology, artificial intelligence has made remarkable achievements in recent years. Computer-aided diagnostic systems are enhanced by using machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) when provided with larger training datasets. This article presents a Histopathology Image-Based Oral Cancer Detection Framework Using a Transformer and a Temporal Convolutional Network (HIOCD-TTCN). The aim is to support pathologists in the early, accurate detection and treatment of oral cancer. Initially, image pre-processing uses the Gaussian filter (GF). Furthermore, the multi-axis vision transformer (MaxVit) method is used to extract meaningful image features. For classification, a temporal convolutional network is employed to effectively learn complex representations from the extracted features. Finally, the chicken swarm optimisation algorithm (CSOA) is used to select the parameters. The comparison analysis of the HIOCD-TTCN methodology showed a superior accuracy of 96.30% compared to other models on the NDB-UFES dataset.

title: A Vision-Guided Bin-Picking Method for Asymmetric Metallic Tubes Based on Image Segmentation and Convolutional Template Matching
authors: Yuyang Gao, Xiaoran Zhang, Ligang Jin, Rui Song
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenge of automated grasping of asymmetric metallic tubes for automotive air-conditioning systems in randomly stacked scenarios, this paper proposes a vision-guided grasping method that combines depth-image segmentation with convolutional template matching. First, a PickNet-based segmentation network is developed to perform accurate target selection, instance segmentation, and grasp-point prediction for tube parts. Subsequently, a local refinement method based on convolutional template matching is introduced to further optimize the grasping position and gripper orientation in the neighborhood of the predicted grasp point. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a grasping success rate of over 85 % in randomly stacked scenes, with good real-time performance and robustness. The method provides a feasible technical solution for flexible automated assembly in manufacturing fields such as automotive components.

title: Comprehensive Inspection Techniques for Evaluating Mg Activation Anneal in p-GaN of GaN Power Devices
authors: Pao-Chi Yao, Ru-Zheng Lin, Chung-Kai Hu, Hua-Mao Chen, Chih-Ming Lai, Yu-Jen Chen
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: This study investigates Mg activation in the p-GaN layer of vertical GaN-on-GaN devices using integrated photoluminescence (PL) and electrochemical capacitance–voltage (ECV) analyses. Rapid thermal processing (RTP) at 825–850 °C for 20 min effectively dissociates Mg–H complexes, enhancing hole concentration from 8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">15</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> (unannealed) to over 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> at 850 °C. The corresponding sheet resistance decreases from 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup> Ω/sq to 1.2×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> Ω/sq, indicating improved p-type conductivity. PL spectra further confirm reduced defect-related yellow emission after activation. The optimized RTP at 850 °C achieves uniform Mg activation and low resistivity, providing a guideline for high-performance vertical GaN power devices.

title: MechGNN: A Graph Neural Network Framework for Learning Nanoparticle-Tumor Mechanical Interactions in Targeted Drug Delivery
authors: Khartik Uppalapati, Bora Yimenicioglu, Shakeel Abdulkareem, Adan Eftekhari
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Mechanical heterogeneity and collagen topology in tumor extracellular matrix (ECM) hinder nanoparticle (NP) transport and uptake, motivating models that couple NP mechanics with measured tumor mechanics. Non-invasive stiffness mapping (e.g., MR elastography) and histology-derived stiffness inference (STIFMap) enable learning on patientproximal mechanical maps. We introduce MechGNN, a dualgraph framework with cross-attention that represents NPs (core/shell/ligands) and ECM fiber networks as interacting graphs, and embeds physics-informed edge functions derived from Hertzian contact and receptor-ligand binding kinetics to regularize message passing. Using only public re-sources-STIFMap and open SHG collagen images quantified with CT-FIRE/CurveAlign/TWOMBLI-plus open FEM/BD simulations (FEniCS; Brownian dynamics) and open NP/ligand repositories (caNanoLab, BindingDB, IUPHAR/BPS Guide to Pharmacology), MechGNN achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 - 1 6 \%}$</tex> reduction in penetration-depth RMSE relative to the strongest baseline on synthetic-from-real ECM testbeds and sustains an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 - 1 3 \%}$</tex> RMSE gain in leave one tumor type out tests; physics losses reduce constraint violation rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 35 \%$</tex>. The pipeline provides a reproducible bridge from measured ECM mechanics to mechanically designable NP properties.

title: Exploring Interface State Density Variation in SiC MOSFETs Under Constant Voltage and Constant Current TDDB Stress Conditions
authors: Yu-Chieh Chen, Wei-Cheng Lin, Seokhuey Soo, Yu-Jie Chiu, Pin-Shiuan Kuo, Surya Elangovan, Chia-Lung Hung, Yi-Kai Hsiao, Hao-Chung Kuo, Tian-Li Wu
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this work, both CVS-TDDB and CCS-TDDB measurements are conducted to compare the underlying breakdown degradation mechanisms. The extracted Weibull shape parameters (β) are 3.872 for CCS and 2.377 for CVS, confirming that the two stress methods induce fundamentally different TDDB behaviors. Moreover, under high-voltage CVS stress (49V), the interface trap density (D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</inf>) increases significantly. In contrast, D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</inf> degradation under high-current CCS stress (467 nA, corresponding to V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> = 49 V) is much smaller and remains comparable to that at normal operating voltages (e.g., V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> = 20 V). These results indicate that CCS can evaluate TDDB robustness without introducing substantial D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</inf> degradation, providing a reliability assessment that more closely reflects normal device operation conditions, in which the D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</inf> degradation is not severe.

title: Heart Disease Prediction Using Hybrid Quantum-Classical Machine Learning Models
authors: Pavan Reddy T, Vishwachetan D, Rajarajeswari S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases remain the leading cause of mortality globally, necessitating accurate diagnostic tools for early detection and intervention. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative and hybrid analysis of quantum and classical machine learning models for heart disease prediction using the UCI Cleveland dataset. We implement classical baseline models including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost, alongside quantum models: Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) with statevector fidelity kernels, Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC) with data-reuploading ansätze, and Quantum Convolutional Neural Networks (QCNN) with parametric pooling. The primary contribution is the development of a hybrid quantum classical ensemble meta-classifier that fuses predictions from all classical and quantum learners using XGBoost as a meta learner. Experiments conducted in Google Colab using Qiskit 1.2+ and Penny Lane 0.36+ demonstrate that the proposed hybrid ensemble achieves superior performance with 98.33accuracy, 100perfect AUC-ROC of 1.0, substantially outperforming standalone classical models (best: 88.33(best: 80hybrid model’s superiority. These results underscore the potential of quantum feature spaces combined with classical decision boundaries in healthcare prediction systems, and demonstrate the immediate utility of hybrid quantum-classical integration in medical diagnostics on near-term quantum hardware.

title: Hybrid Transformer Models for Multimodal Brain Tumor Detection and Grading
authors: Prathiba R, Sunitha B S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Because of the complexity of brain tumor structures, variations in imaging data, and common modality absence, it is a challenging task to detect and grade brain tumors using MRI scans. Although conventional CNN models have shown great ability to learn local structures, such as edges and textures, the attention mechanism in transformer models has proven successful for learning broader contextual relationships but not without high computational resources. In an attempt to overcome these limitations, the current study proposes a hybrid framework that mixes CNNs and transformers for better performance. The model implements CNNs for the detailed extraction of spatial features and uses transformer encoders to learn global contextual information. Then, an attention based fusion mechanism is proposed to handle the multi-modal MRI images such as T1, T2 and FLAIR modalities when some of them are missing. As the experimental results show, our method achieves higher accuracy in detecting and grading tumors even with lower false positive rate and clearer boundary delineation when compared to previous methods. In summary, provides a robust and scalable framework to assist decision-making in the diagnosis of brain tumors.

title: Study of Temporal Deixis on the Corpus of LDP and Its Application to The LDP Identity Construction Analysis
authors: Zhengmao Wu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: The Liberal Democratic Party of Japan (LDP) publishes its “Party Statement” on its official website during major festivals or commemorate days to express viewpoints of the Party with rich temporal deixis, so it is helpful to construct its institutional identity. This study establishes a corpus (2013-2025) through the New Year’s congratulations, employing KHCoder3.0 software to identify high-frequency words, temporal deixis, and co-occurrence networks, while conducting contextual introspective analysis to elucidate how the LDP constructs its institutional identity through temporal deixis. The study reveals that the LDP constructs a negative institutional identity of ‘the Other’ by past temporal deixis, while building a positive institutional identity of ‘the Self’ by future and absolute temporal deixis. Therefore, the use of temporal deixis is a strategy for identity construction. By analyzing the use of temporal deixis, we can reveal the diachronic construction of identity, providing a new approach for identity theory research.

title: Lithium-Ion Battery Capacity Degradation Prediction Based on Variational Mode Decomposition and Cross-Modal Attention Network
authors: Ruifeng An, Yuqian Fan, Shuangjun Zhou, Yawen Liang, Mingshen Hong, Quanxue Guan, Xiaojun Tan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Precise prognosis of battery Remaining Useful Life (RUL) serves as a cornerstone for guaranteeing operational safety and refining maintenance protocols in energy storage systems. However, battery capacity trajectories are difficult to model because they contain long-term degradation trends together with short-term regeneration and measurement fluctuations. Furthermore, this degradation results from the synergistic effects of multiple physical fields, such as voltage and current. Existing models often overlook the dynamic physical coupling relationships among these multi-modal features, limiting prediction accuracy. To address these challenges, this study proposes a model based on Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and a Cross-Modal Attention Network (CMAT). First, high-dimensional Health Features (HFs) are adaptively decomposed into Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs) at different frequency scales via VMD, effectively decoupling long-term degradation trends from shortterm capacity fluctuations. Subsequently, a dual-stream attention mechanism is designed: an intra-IMF attention module adaptively extracts multi-scale time-frequency features, while an interfeature cross-attention module explicitly captures deep coupling mechanisms between different physical modalities. Finally, a Bayesian optimization strategy is introduced for adaptive hyperparameter optimization. Experiments on two open datasets demonstrate that the proposed VMD-CMAT model outperforms baseline models, including Transformer, Informer, and Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN).

title: PPO-Based Dual-Layer Dual-Hole Pallet Insertion Without Inter-Layer Constraints Via Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yinuo Wu, Lu Wang, Xinzhi Wei, Yuhang Li, Longjia Sang, Rui Song
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Dual-layer dual-hole pallet insertion under completely unconstrained environments without inter-layer fixtures, connecting walls, or linkages presents critical challenges for robotic assembly. The two layers of holes are free-floating through-holes with no mechanical constraints between them, leading to dynamic pose deviations that cannot be handled by conventional pre-programmed methods. This paper proposes a reinforcement learning method based on Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with hierarchical architecture, which enables robust dual-gripper collaborative insertion without any interlayer positioning or guiding constraints. We construct a 77dimensional state representation that integrates end-effector relative poses, arm kinematics, force/torque feedback, and hierarchical insertion states. A staged reward mechanism is designed to guide the policy progressively: the first-layer alignment and insertion rewards are activated initially, and the second-layer rewards are triggered only after the first layer is successfully completed, with additional terms for force stability and action efficiency. The policy network adopts a 256-128-64 fully-connected structure with LeakyReLU activation and decaying Gaussian exploration noise. Multi-environment parallel training is performed in IsaacLab simulation with randomized initial end-effector pose perturbations.

title: Absolute Phase Refinement Network Based on Multi-Task Joint Learning
authors: Minjie Zhang, Jia Zhao, Pan Yue, Fuyou Jiang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Digital Fringe Projection Profilometry (FPP) is a key non-contact optical metrology technique. However, traditional multi-step phase-shifting methods struggle to adapt to dynamic scenarios, and existing single-frame absolute phase retrieval methods based on Multi-Task Learning (MTL) suffer from pixellevel feature misalignment, leading to abnormal points in phase maps and impairing 3D reconstruction quality. This study proposes an absolute phase refinement network via multi-task joint learning, integrating the Fringe Order Refined Module (FORM) and a pre-training-freezing-refinement three-step strategy. FORM establishes feature correlations through a Dynamic Channel Feature Mask and achieves alignment optimization with relevant sub-modules. Experiments show the method is compatible with multiple networks, reducing RMSE, improving stability and unwrapping accuracy, and reducing abnormal points near phase jumps. Physical system verification confirms its high reconstruction accuracy for complex structures in real scenarios, providing a high-precision solution for singleframe FPP and enhancing MTL’s practicality in industrial inspection.

title: AI-Vision: Deep Learning Based Facial Recognition System
authors: Ajay N, Monisha M, Shrihari M R, Namratha S M, Navya K, Shwetha B V
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This paper focuses on real-time face recognition in videos using a user-uploaded target face image. It operates entirely on a CPU, eliminating the need for GPU hardware. The system leverages advanced face recognition models like ArcFace and robust detectors such as MTCNN and RetinaFace to ensure accuracy. Key features include adjustable thresholds, frame skipping, and resizing for performance optimization. The application is built on a modern Flask-based web interface, allowing users to upload videos and view matched frames with bounding boxes and similarity scores. The backend processes video frames using OpenCV and DeepFace, extracting embeddings and comparing them to the target face.

title: NeuroDL-Net: A Unified Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Disease Neurodegenerative Detection Using EEG Signals
authors: Siddharth Chintawar, Tejas Pendse, Sandeep Sangle
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), and Frontotemporal Dementia (FTD) are different neurodegenerative disorders that affect millions of individuals over time. Early and accurate identification is crucial to slowing the progression of illness and improving the quality of life for those affected. Electroencephalography (EEG) is a non-invasive and cost-effective method for analyzing changes in brain activity associated with various disorders. Researchers are designing various deep learning (DL) models and systems to detect individual neuro disorders. In this study, design a single DL model to detect different neurodegenerative disorders using EEG signals. For this, we utilized signals for AD, FTD, and PD by selecting their common channels. Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) layers were employed to extract spatial and temporal information from the provided EEG data by combining two different datasets with selecting similar channels. This helped the model distinguish between patients with AD, FTD, PD, and healthy individuals. The CNN model achieves a micro-accuracy of 96.67% and an F1 Score of 93.33%, and it can classify various brain diseases using EEG records.

title: An Intelligent and Efficient Framework for Brain Tumor Detection and Classification Leveraging Swin Vision Transformer and Quantum-Inspired Support Vector Machine
authors: S. Gayathri, Santhi Baskaran
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Detecting and classifying tumors in the brain is essential to diagnose it early and plan the treatment. The main challenges associated with the existing imaging and machine learning methods include the inability to represent features properly, high computational efficiency, and low accuracy with heterogeneous Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) datasets. The proposed research aims to address these shortcomings by introducing a smart and effective system to incorporate the Swin Vision Transformer (SViT) to extract relevant features and a Quantum-Inspired Support Vector Machine (QISVM) to precisely classify. The SViT employs hierarchical attention to extract the local and global contextual information of MRI scans and the QISVM enhances the accuracy of the classification with an efficient exploration of the solution space based on quantum-inspired optimization principles. The main target in the proposed framework is to have high sensitivity, specificity and general accuracy in identifying and classifying brain tumor at the lowest possible computational cost. The experimental results on the benchmark datasets prove that the proposed framework is significantly better than the current convolutional neural networks (CNNs), the current SVMs, and existing hybrid models in that it possesses a higher detection accuracy, a lower training time, and a better generalization to form a promising solution to real-time clinical decision support systems.

title: An IoT-Based Online Detection and Diagnosis System for Water Ingress Risks in Elbow-Type Cables
authors: Xinyu Luo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Water ingress faults in elbow-type cables frequently occur in power systems and pose a serious threat to the operational safety of the power grid. This paper presents a design for an Internet of Things (IoT)-based online detection and diagnosis system for water ingress risks in elbow-type cables. Using a distributed sensor network architecture and a cloud-edge collaborative computing model, the study creates a multi-source data fusion algorithm and a water ingress risk assessment model. The system uses a combination of wavelet transform and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks to identify abnormal water ingress characteristics early on. Validation of the system demonstrates that this method can effectively provide early warnings of water ingress risks in elbow-type cables. This offers a new approach to preventive maintenance in power systems and significantly enhances the operational reliability of cables.

title: Design and Implementation of an Intelligent Educational Chatbot using Natural Language Processing
authors: D. Ganesh, M. Sunil Kumar, S. Vamsi Kesava, S. Sravani, T. Mahendra Reddy, S. Ankitha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Modern educational environments struggle to accommodate the diverse learning requirements of individual students. Traditional classroom instruction often follows a uniform approach, which fails to address varying comprehension speeds and learning preferences among students. This work focuses on developing an intelligent conversational system that adapts to individual learner needs through natural language understanding and personalized response generation. The system analyzes student interactions to identify knowledge gaps and adjusts its teaching approach dynamically. During development, we integrated transformer-based language models with custom intent recognition modules to enable context-aware conversations. The architecture supports real-time adaptation based on student performance metrics extracted from ongoing dialogues. Testing across multiple subject domains revealed that learners engaged more consistently when receiving personalized feedback compared to static content delivery. The system demonstrated scalability across different educational levels and subject complexities. Implementation challenges included managing conversation context over extended sessions and calibrating difficulty levels appropriately. Future enhancements will incorporate multimodal learning support and expanded subject coverage to serve broader educational scenarios.

title: Integrating Sentiment Analysis with Machine Learning for Cyberbullying Detection
authors: Tadala Durga Srikar Pranav, Sarang Shahare, P Meghana, B Mamatha, Jangam Subbarayudu, G Sumalatha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Due to the intensive growth of social media, cyberbullying has emerged as a major problem in the digital era, and thus, this constitutes an immediate need of an automated and fast-tracking detection system of such malicious behavior. Conventional techniques for cyberbullying fail to incorporate the underlying sentiment in the context of text classification. Using textual normalization followed by linguistic features extraction, and sentiment polarity calculation, this paper aims to compare a wide range of classification algorithms & their ability to distinguish between different cyberbullying types with & without the information of the sentiment. Using F1-Score & Matthews Correlation Coefficient as evaluation metrics, proposed approach does not only emphasize the importance of emotional context in identifying abusive behaviors.

title: Ranking of Electrical Substation Sites using Simple Multi-Attribute Rating Technique (SMART)
authors: Thandeka Lerato Mbundu, Nhlanhla Mbuli
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: An electrical substation is a vital component of power systems. It supports the integration of renewable energy, monitoring and automation, power quality and voltage regulation, system protection, power flow management, and voltage transformation, thereby improving stability and reliability. Placing electrical substations in unsuitable locations has caused significant challenges. Several criteria must be considered when selecting a substation site, and a multi-criteria decision analysis (MCDA) can assist in this process. A simple multi-attribute rating technique is one type of MCDA. Weights are crucial as they reflect the relative importance of each criterion. The advantage of weight ranking methods is that they include detailed information about the importance of each criterion. Different approaches for determining weights include the rank order centroid, equal, inverse or reciprocal, rank sum, and rank exponent weight methods. Based on the case study conducted, all these weight determination techniques resulted in the same preferred option or alternative. The simple multi-attribute rating technique is easy to use, straightforward, and requires minimal effort.

title: Exploratory Data Analysis(EDA) and Air Quality Forecasting for a City Using Random Forest
authors: Bandi Varshitha, P. Malin Bruntha, Gonuru Siva Dinesh, Angel Mariya, Maghi Sathya J K, Chinnam Jasil Nissia
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Air pollution forecasting is an important component of urban environmental management and the protection of public health. In this paper, a machine learningbased framework is proposed to analyze and forecast Air Quality Index (AQI) levels in Delhi using the publicly accessible Air Quality Data in India (2015-2020) dataset. A thorough Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) is conducted to discover the pollutant trends and to determine which pollutants have the most substantial impact on AQI changes. A Random Forest Regression model is used to predict the numerical AQI values from the main pollutants such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PM} 2.5, \text{PM} 10, \text{NO}_{2}, \text{CO}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex>. In contrast, a Random Forest Classification model is utilized to allocate the predicted AQI to the standard CPCB-defined air quality categories. Experimental results show that the proposed method is capable of modeling nonlinear interactions between pollutants and gives accurate short-term and mid-term predictions. The classification model has a high accuracy in determining the AQI categories, which shows that ensemble learning methods are the most appropriate for air quality monitoring and decision support systems in practice.

title: Linearizability Alone Is Not Enough for Strict FIFO Semantics
authors: Ryan Huard, Md Sabbir Hossain Polak, Joshua Jurgenson, David Troendle, Byunghyun Jang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Modern designs of concurrent FIFO queues often improve their scalability through executing multiple simultaneous operations or reducing cache coherence traffic, yet their ordering semantics under contention remain inadequately characterized. While the linearizability model ensures correctness, it allows arbitrary reordering of overlapping operations, masking significant deviations from ideal FIFO behavior. We introduce fairness as a measure of how closely concurrent enqueue and dequeue operations respect their invocation order. We propose two complementary metrics, overtake depth, which quantifies the severity of per-element ordering violations and overtake percentage, which measures the frequency of such violations system-wide. We apply our metrics to benchmark several modern concurrent queues under balanced and imbalanced workloads on an 8-core AMD Ryzen processor. Our results reveal that fairness violations are pervasive: under high contention, queues exhibit overtake percentages up to 68%, with individual elements bypassed by an average of 25 others (overtake depth) in severe cases. Our analysis shows that the common causes of unfairness are 1) retrying compare-and-swap (CAS) operations, 2) combiner threads delaying their own operations, 3) delays between invocation and linearization that allow operations to be descheduled. Additionally, we observe a strong correlation ($R^{2}=0.98$) between L1 cache miss rates and overtake percentage, indicating that operations delayed by cache coherence stalls are more likely to be overtaken.

title: Sentinel: A Human-in-the-loop AI Framework for Electoral De-Duplication
authors: Suhas U, Sujay Mudakappa Matur, Zaiba Farheen, Shabana Sultana
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Ensuring the precision of elections is crucial for maintaining respect and trust in democracy and other governmental procedures. Duplicate voter representations, arising from numerous sources like administrative problems and identity fraud, present a significant challenge in large scale democracies. But facial recognition systems present in the contemporary world may not solve all the issues of de-duplication, new issues can develop from it like removal of real and genuine voters. This research paper introduces Sentinel, an AI-enabled technology and innovation that uses the combination of deep learning as well a human in the loop mechanism. The system uses an SSD MobileNet V1 for face detection, and a fully implemented ResNet-34 architecture to generate 128-dimensional facial embeddings, which are then compared using the Euclidean distance to identify any duplicates in the database. Instead of automatically rejecting suspicious records they will be resolved by human intervention.

title: A Survey on Computer Vision Framework for Joint Detection in Athletic Movements
authors: E.M Malathy, Varshini S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: A comprehensive review of computer vision frame- works designed for joint-level error localization in athletic movements. Accurate identification and analysis of joint er- rors identified in crucial for performance enhancement, injury prevention and rehabilitation in sports. Examining state-of-the- art methodologies in human pose estimation, motion tracking and error detection which leverage advances in deep learning, 3D pose reconstruction and biomechanical modeling. Capturing techniques includes high-speed athletic movements, localization accuracy challenges and datasets specific to sports are discussed. The research paper highlights the integration of error localization systems in real-time feedback mechanisms for athletes and coaches, also current research gaps and future directions to improve accessibility and impact in the field.

title: Multimodal Knowledge Distillation on MRI-based Alzheimer’s Disease Detection with Clinical-Guided Teacher Learning
authors: Vaishnavi A Jadhav, Shrusti M Jamakhandi, Shrinivas A Naganur, Kaushik Malhibhat
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurode-generative disease, Existing clinical manifestations of AD are heterogeneous and incomplete making it difficult to accurately detect the condition because of lack of access to multi-modal data. The paper presents a multi-modal knowledge distillation model to be applied in the detection of AD, based on the OASIS dataset. Multi-modal teacher network is trained to learn together with MRI images and structured clinical features in order to capture disease-specific representations that are not complementary. The acquired knowledge is condensed to a student model of light weight, which is limited to the MRI data. The accuracy, AUC, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix, ROC analysis, inference time and model complexity are used to test the proposed framework. The experimental results indicate that the teacher model has high performance and the distilled student model has better generalization and efficiency than the traditional single-modal training. The findings indicate that knowledge distillation is an effective technique with respect to balancing diagnostic performance and computational efficiency in clinical practice.

title: Design of Radix-4 Booth (Radix 256 booth encoding) Multiplication Algorithm
authors: Patnala Uma Mehar Sai Nagesh, Vella Satyanarayana
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Almost allelectronic systems, including notably portable ones like smartphones, tablets, and the other devices, have energy reduction as one of their primary design considerations. These handheld gadgets rely on digital signal processing (DSP) blocks to power their multimedia capabilities. Mathematical logic units, which are at the heart of these blocks’ processing, are responsible for the vast majority of DSP systems’ arithmetic operations, the most common of which is multiplication. For low power, low complexity applications, an effective approximate 8 bit multiplier is presented using the Radix-4 Booth (Radix 256 booth encoding) multiplication approach. Some have suggested using approximate Booth encoders to lower the PDP and simplify MBE by inserting false entries into the truth table. The partial product accumulation array makes use of an approximation of the Wallace tree structure. Radix 16 booth multiplication, which improves hardware efficiency with low latency, is also used to enhance this project.

title: Improving Brain State Classification using Physics-Informed Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Sathvik Surapaneni, Srihan Sankepalle, Rahul Ghanta
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Millions are afflicted by serious neurological conditions that impact emotion regulation, cognition, and even sleep. While electroencephalogram (EEG) analysis provides insights into brain function, current approaches to multi-dimensional classification of brain states are not very reliable; importantly, no existing method achieves truly parallel detection of emotional state and cognitive focus from EEG. Our dual-branch architecture consists of (1) an EEGNet-inspired convolutional stream that learns rich spatiotemporal patterns across channels, and (2) a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) branch that imposes the 1D wave-equation residual via Random Fourier Feature (RFF) positional encodings over densely sampled collocation points. This close coupling of datadriven representations with fundamental wavepropagation dynamics imbues an interpretable inductive bias, drastically mitigating overfitting while aligning learned features with known neural signal physics. Tested under strict leave-one-subject-out cross-validation on the focus-vs-rest stimulus dataset (91.8% accuracy) and under a subject-half/subject-half split on the emotion dataset (90.7% accuracy), our framework's two pipelines both considerably outperform purely empirical baselines. This study is the first to augment an EEGNet classifier with RFF-based PINN constraints, proving that incorporating domain-specific physical laws directly into deep models can bring about both better performance and mechanistic insight. These findings pave the way for robust, generalizable EEG analysis and highlight the transformative promise of physics-guided machine learning in neuroengineering.

title: Feature-guided non-normalized transformer optical image fusion
authors: Yao Tang, Feifei Hu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: The fusion of infrared and visible light images aims to leverage their complementary spectral data to enhance the quality, clarity, and content of the output images. However, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) cannot capture long-range dependencies, while Transformer architectures face the challenge of consuming significant computational resources. To address these critical challenges, we propose an end-to-end training fusion network framework based on feature-guided non-normalized transformers to reduce manual intervention. A branch network feature-guided module (FGM) is introduced to guide the network to focus on important local complementary features; a denormalized transformer module (DTTM) is designed to establish longrange contextual relationships and focus on global information. By combining the advantages of both, the framework retains local complementary information while leveraging global contextual features. The fusion framework was evaluated using public domain datasets TNO and Roadscene. Experimental results show that, objectively, MI, VIF, and Qabf improved by $46.12 \%$, $1.705 \%$, and $3.96 \%$, respectively, compared to the next-best method, while Nabf and MSE decreased by $11.11 \%$ and $2.38 \%$, respectively, compared to the next-best method. Subjectively, the fused images exhibit rich texture details, demonstrating superior fusion performance and stronger generalization capabilities.

title: Error Suppression and High-Precision Inversion Model for Infrared Measurement of Semiconductor Epitaxial Layer Thickness under Multi-Beam Interference Disturbance
authors: Ganggang Lian, Yizhe Song, Bing Xu, Yu Zhang, Haiyan Xu, Xueqing Zheng
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Semiconductor epitaxial technology serves as the core process for manufacturing third-generation wide-bandgap semiconductor devices, integrated circuits, and optoelectronic devices. The uniformity of epitaxial layer thickness and measurement accuracy directly determine the electrical performance, reliability, and service life of these devices. Infrared grazing incidence interferometry has become the mainstream technique for epitaxial layer thickness measurement due to its non-contact, non-destructive, rapid measurement speed, and suitability for online detection. However, during actual measurements, infrared light undergoes infinite reflections and transmissions at the three-layer structure interface of “air-epitaxial layer-substrate,” generating multi-beam interference interference. This results in spectral peak/valley shifts and fringe distortion, leading to reduced accuracy in traditional dual-beam interferometry models and failing to meet the semiconductor industry's high-precision requirement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{2} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> relative error. To address this challenge, this study focuses on two critical objectives: multi-beam interference suppression and high-precision thickness inversion. We have established three core modules- “physical modeling at the fundamental level, interference suppression, and thickness inversion” -while proposing an error suppression method and high-precision inversion model for infrared measurement of semiconductor epitaxial layer thickness under multi-beam interference conditions. Specifically, this study establishes an infrared refractive index dispersion model for semiconductor materials based on Sellmeier dispersion equations, and develops a dual-beam interference reference model incorporating dispersion characteristics through Fresnel reflection laws and Snell's law. An adaptive bandwidth OPD-domain filtering algorithm is proposed to achieve precise separation of main interference signals from multi-beam interference, demonstrating adaptability across diverse measurement scenarios. The second-order difference method is employed for automatic interference peak extraction, with thickness inversion formulas derived from correlation parameters and enhanced stability achieved via dual incidence angle consistency constraints. To validate the proposed methodology, experiments using Si and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 H}$</tex>-SiC epitaxial layers were conducted on a dedicated MATLAB simulation platform, generating four independent visualizations. Results demonstrate that the method effectively suppresses multi-beam interference interference, reducing interference spectrum peak/valley offsets by over 85%, maintaining thickness inversion accuracy within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1.5 {\%}$</tex> with measurement repeatability exceeding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.05 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. The approach successfully accommodates thickness measurements ranging from 2 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> for both Si and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \mathrm{H}-\text{SiC}$</tex> epitaxial layers, outperforming traditional methods. Balancing theoretical rigor with engineering applicability, the model exhibits high computational efficiency for direct application in semiconductor industry online inspection. This provides novel technical pathways and theoretical foundations for high-precision epitaxial layer thickness measurement, holding significant engineering value and academic implications for improving wide-bandgap semiconductor device quality and advancing high-quality semiconductor industry development.
