title: AdPrST:An Adversarial Graph Deep Learning Pre-clustering Framework for Deciphering Spatiotemporal Structures in Spatially Resolved Transcriptomics
authors: Haoyuan Ma, Shensi Huang, Haiyun Wang, Jianping Zhao, Junfeng Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Spatially Resolved Transcriptomics (SRT) has revolutionized our understanding of gene expression within tissue microenvironments, yet accurately deciphering spatiotemporal structures—encompassing spatial domain identification, trajectory inference, and pseudo-spatiotemporal map construction—in complex tissues remains a formidable challenge. AdPrST begins with a pre-clustering process on gene expression data to establish initial domain groupings. It then constructs dual-view graph structures using K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) for local similarities and r-radius for broader spatial contexts. Through adversarial self-supervised contrast, leveraging Wasserstein distance-based GANs and contrastive learning, AdPrST generates robust low dimensional embeddings for each view. These embeddings are fused via a dot-product attention mechanism, guided by pre clustering labels, to achieve accurate spatial domain identification. Benchmarking across multiple datasets demonstrated AdPrST's superior performance over state-of-the-art methods, highlighting its potential to advance spatial transcriptomics research by elucidating spatial functional patterns and developmental trajectories. In particular, AdPrST excels in infer encing spatiotemporal structures, reconstructing developmental sequences and temporal features in complex tissues.

title: Spatiotemporal Ensemble Deep Learning for Sea Surface Temperature Forecasting
authors: Lalita Chaudhary, Shakti Sharma, Gaurav Kumar Dashondhi, Sudipan Saha, Arnab Muhuri
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Sea Surface Temperature (SST) in the Bay of Bengal exhibits rapid variability driven by freshwater discharge, monsoon winds, and frequent atmospheric disturbances. Accurate short-range forecasting of this field is important for marine operations and climate-related studies over the region. This letter presents an ensemble deep learning framework that integrates a Long Short-Term Memory network, a Gated Recurrent Unit, and a transformer-based TimeSformer branch. All three components are trained using satellite-derived SST fields from the SEVIRI Indian Ocean product to capture complementary temporal and spatial characteristics of the signal. The individual forecasts are combined using a weighted fusion strategy to generate SST predictions for a fourteen-day horizon. The proposed approach is evaluated over the Bay of Bengal and compared against several widely used deep learning models, including CNN-Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), ConvLSTM, Bi-LSTM, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), ViT-LSTM, TimeSformer, and the earlier DualSeq model. The ensemble framework achieves the lowest prediction error, with a root mean square error of 0.55°C, a mean absolute error of 0.42°C, and an R-squared value of 0.96. Compared with DualSeq, the root mean square error is reduced by approximately eleven percent, indicating improved skill in short-range SST forecasting for this dynamically evolving region.

title: Vibrations of a Delta Robot in the Perspective of Energy Harvesting
authors: Michele Tonan, Matteo Bottin, Alberto Doria, Giulio Rosati
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Delta robots are characterized by large accelerations and vibrations, hence they are a benchmark case for the application of Vibration Energy Harvesting (VEH) technologies in the field of robotics. Experimental modal analysis is used to find the natural frequencies of the first modes of vibration of a Delta robot. The comparison with the frequency spectrum of the acceleration due to the planned motion shows that resonance phenomena are not present, and the robot experiences low frequency forced vibrations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(f<20 ~\text{Hz}$</tex>) with harmonics that depend on the shape of the planned trajectory. A piezoelectric vibration harvester is designed to scavenge energy from such low-frequency forced vibrations. Experimental tests showed that the harvester is able to generate large voltages <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 20 \mathbf{V}$</tex>) that by means of a proper converter will make it possible to feed sensor nodes.

title: Identifying Patterns in Bangla Fake News Using Word2Vec and Attention Based CNN
authors: Rakibul Hasan Nirob, Jeba Wasima, Francis Rudra D Cruze, Fizar Ahmed, Md. Nasimul Kader
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The rapid dissemination of misinformation poses a significant challenge to the integrity of online information, especially for low resource languages such as Bangla. Despite being spoken by millions, Bangla still lacks reliable systems for automated fake news detection. This work presents a novel framework that combines multiple feature extraction methods with deep learning (Dl) architectures to address this gap. We employ Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TFIDF), CountVectorizer(CV), Global Vectors for Word Representation(GloVe) and Word2Vec embeddings for text encoding and utilize an attention based Convolutional Neural Network (AttCNN) to enhance contextual understanding and capture subtle linguistic patterns. The proposed method demonstrates scalability for large scale datasets while maintaining strong classification performance. Through extensive experimentation, our model has been tested and proven robust with the Att-CNN using Word2Vec embeddings achieving state of the art accuracy of 98.42 % in Bangla fake news detection.

title: Agentic AI in Distributed SDN: Synchronization and Design Framework
authors: Rawan Alsheikh, Etimad Fadel, Nadine Akkari
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Large-scale Software-Defined Networking (SDN) deployments are increasingly adopting Physically Distributed but Logically Centralized (PDLC) control planes, in which multiple controllers must coordinate to maintain a consistent global view of the network. In practice, this creates a persistent trade-off between state consistency and synchronization overhead under limited control-plane budgets. Existing AI-based synchronization solutions often rely on centralized learning and decision components, which can reduce scalability and introduce single-point-of-failure-like dependence. This paper presents a design framework for adaptive synchronization in PDLC SDN from an agentic AI perspective. The objective is to express synchronization as a budget-aware decision process that can be learned by controller-resident agents while enabling cooperative learning without centralized run-time action selection. We model synchronization as a budget-aware Markov Decision Process (MDP) with explicit state, action, and reward definitions that capture staleness, constrained neighbor selection, and benefit-cost tradeoffs. Based on this formulation, we adopt Centralized Training and Decentralized Execution (CTDE): decentralized Actors make local synchronization decisions at run time, while a trainingtime Critic provides coordination signals to stabilize multi-agent learning. The framework is instantiated with Communication Multi-Agent Actor-Critic (C-MAAC) and extended with an Attention-Based Critic (ABC-MAAC), where attention suppresses low-impact inter-controller relations and emphasizes influential ones before value estimation. Complexity verification shows linear per-update scaling for C-MAAC and an additional quadratic interaction term for ABC-MAAC due to pairwise attention, while preserving decentralized, budget-aware run-time execution.

title: Satellite-IoT Access Technologies and Simulation Frameworks in 6G Non-Terrestrial Networks: Limitations and Modeling Challenges
authors: Khaoula Kacem, Nawel Zangar, Soumaya Hamouda
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTNs) are expected to play a key role in sixth-generation (6G) systems by extending Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity beyond terrestrial coverage. Satellite-based IoT enables large-scale deployments in remote and underserved regions; however, it introduces structural challenges related to large coverage footprints, time-varying propagation conditions, and predominantly ALOHA-based uncoordinated random access under massive device density. This paper presents a structured survey of NTN-IoT access technologies and their associated simulation frameworks, focusing on scalability limitations and modeling constraints, particularly those induced by large-scale random access contention in satellite beams. Cellular and non-cellular solutions, including LoRaWAN Chirp Spread Spectrum (CSS) and Long Range Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum (LR-FHSS), are comparatively analyzed in terms of access robustness and density-dependent performance trade-offs. Existing simulation tools are further examined with respect to their ability to represent NTN geometry, dynamic propagation behavior, large-scale random access contention, and waveform-specific mechanisms. The study highlights persistent fragmentation in current evaluation approaches and emphasizes the need for unified and reproducible modeling methodologies to enable fair performance comparison of future NTN-IoT systems.

title: Fillerbuster: Unified Generative Scene Completion Model for Casual Captures
authors: Ethan Weber, Norman Müller, Yash Kant, Vasu Agrawal, Michael Zollhöfer, Angjoo Kanazawa, Christian Richardt
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: We present Fillerbuster, a unified model that completes unknown regions of a 3D scene with a multi-view latent diffusion transformer. Casual captures are often sparse and miss surrounding content behind objects or above the scene. Existing methods are not suitable for this challenge as they focus on making known pixels look good with sparse-view priors, or on creating missing sides of objects from just one or two photos. In reality, we often have hundreds of input frames and want to complete areas that are missing and unobserved from the input frames. Our solution is to train a generative model that can consume a large context of input frames while generating unknown target views and recovering image poses when camera parameters are unknown. We show results where we complete partial captures on two existing datasets. We also present an uncalibrated scene completion task where our unified model predicts both poses and creates new content. We open-source our framework for integration into popular reconstruction platforms like Nerfstudio or Gsplat. We present a flexible, unified inpainting framework to predict many images and poses together, where all inputs are jointly inpainted, and it could be extended to predict more modalities such as depth.

title: Time-Optimal L1 Guidance for Straight-Line Path Following in 3D
authors: Pradyumn Mahajan, Sayantan Pal, Sikha Hota
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This work presents a modified L1 guidance law that generates smooth and time-efficient trajectories for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) to converge to straight-line paths in three-dimensional (3D) space. This approach employs the L1 guidance law to converge to the straight-line segment of the time-optimal but curvature-discontinuous 3D Dubins path before transitioning to the desired straight line path. In this way, this law significantly reduces the convergence time of L1 guidance while maintaining its curvature continuity (C2-continuous path) and respecting the curvature bounds defined by the minimum turn radius of the UAV. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed guidance law through comparisons with classical L1 guidance and Dubins paths.

title: The Role of Model Architecture in Breast Cancer Classification: A Comparison of CNN and Transformer Paradigms on Ultrasound Data
authors: Siam Tahsin Bhuiyan, Halima Khatun, Samiul Karim Mazumder, Fatin Israq, Riyadul Islam, Rashedur Rahman, Ashraful Islam, Saadia Binte Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Breast cancer is the leading malignancy in women globally, and early detection is critical for improving survival. Deep learning has shown promise in medical image analysis, yet the effects of model architecture remain underexplored. This study conducts a comparative evaluation of four models: DenseNet121 (pure CNN), ConvNeXt (modernized CNN with transformer-inspired design), ViT-B/16 (pure Vision Transformer), and Swin-B (hierarchical transformer with CNN-like inductive biases) for breast ultrasound classification using the public BUSI dataset of 780 images. Results demonstrate that DenseNet121 exhibits limitations due to its reliance on local receptive fields, which restricts its ability to capture distributed pathological features. ConvNeXt outperforms DenseNet121 across all metrics, with 83% accuracy and 91% AUROC, highlighting the benefits of transformer-inspired refinements within CNNs. ViT-B/16 achieves the highest AUROC of 94%, underscoring the strength of global self-attention for capturing distributed pathological cues. Swin-B attains the most balanced performance, with 86% accuracy, 85% recall, and 0.92 AUROC, reflecting the effectiveness of combining global attention with hierarchical locality. These findings suggest a clear performance hierarchy, where hybrid transformer architectures such as Swin-B offer the most clinically reliable balance between sensitivity and specificity. Future research should explore hybrid models that integrate CNN inductive priors with transformer-based global reasoning to further enhance robustness in real-world diagnostic applications.

title: Syntheto-PCOS: A Fairness-Aware Generative Learning Framework for Robust Assessment of Metabolic Dysfunction in Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
authors: Syeda Zaina Rohana Sneha, Mumtahina Mozumder, Farida Akter Banu, Rafshia Mahbuba Ayshe
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) diagnosis using machine learning is often hindered by limited and imbalanced clinical data, leading to biased and unreliable models. This paper introduces Syntheto-PCOS, a fairness-aware framework that addresses this challenge through generative data augmentation. We employ a Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (CTGAN) to create high-fidelity synthetic data, effectively doubling our dataset and balancing the class distribution. A suite of five machine learning models was trained on this augmented data, with RandomForest and LightGBM optimized via Bayesian techniques. Our results show that the ensemble models achieved superior performance, with the optimized LightGBM model attaining an AUC of 0.893 and a clinically significant diagnostic recall of 68.8%. Feature importance and SHAP analysis confirmed that the model's predictions are driven by established clinical markers (e.g., follicle counts, AMH), ensuring both interpretability and clinical relevance. The Syntheto-PCOS framework demonstrates a robust methodology for developing accurate and trustworthy diagnostic tools in the face of common medical data limitations.

title: Introducing the New IAS Membership Department [Membership News]
authors: Payman Dehghanian
publish: IEEE Industry Applications Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: Thrill of the Build
authors: Gary Singh
publish: IEEE Computer Graphics and Applications

title: Programmable In-Network Aggregation for Communication-Aware Federated Learning in 5G RANs
authors: Emilio Paolini, Andrea Pinto, Luca Valcarenghi, Flavio Esposito
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data, making it attractive for privacy-preserving applications at the wireless edge. However, when executed over real 5G networks, FL performance degrades due to uplink congestion, heterogeneous client capabilities, and intermittent connectivity. Most existing approaches attempt to mitigate these issues indirectly by optimizing clients (through adaptive participation, local training, or selection strategies) or by optimizing models (via pruning, quantization, or compression), but they ignore potential network bottlenecks. This paper introduces FLAG, an FL architecture that embeds innetwork aggregation directly into 5G gNodeBs, transforming the network into an active participant in the learning process. In particular, FLAG performs parameter aggregation at line rate within the 5G Service Data Adaptation Protocol layer and incorporates three mechanisms: Partial-Contribution Correction for loss-tolerant averaging, a timer-driven pipeline for real-time scheduling, and a deadline-based grouping strategy to mitigate stragglers. Experiments with realistic wireless emulation show that FLAG achieves up to 5.1× faster time-to-accuracy and maintains accuracy within 0.8% of a loss-free baseline, while reducing gNB-to-server bandwidth by aggregating pergNB rather than per-client. FLAG requires no modifications to clients or the parameter server, demonstrating how 5G-aware system design can make federated learning scalable, efficient, and resilient under real-world wireless conditions.

title: FreeFix: Boosting 3D Gaussian Splatting via Fine-Tuning-Free Diffusion Models
authors: Hongyu Zhou, Zisen Shao, Sheng Miao, Pan Wang, Dongfeng Bai, Bingbing Liu, Yiyi Liao
publish: 2026 International Conference on 3D Vision (3DV)
abstract: Neural Radiance Fields and 3D Gaussian Splatting have advanced novel view synthesis, yet still rely on dense inputs and often degrade at extrapolated views. Recent approaches leverage generative models, such as diffusion models, to provide additional supervision, but face a tradeoff between generalization and fidelity: fine-tuning diffusion models for artifact removal improves fidelity but risks overfitting, while fine-tuning-free methods preserve generalization but often yield lower fidelity. We introduce FreeFix, a fine-tuning-free approach that pushes the boundary of this trade-off by enhancing extrapolated rendering with pretrained image diffusion models. We present an interleaved 2D-3D refinement strategy, showing that image diffusion models can be leveraged for consistent refinement without relying on costly video diffusion models. Furthermore, we take a closer look at the guidance signal for 2D refinement and propose a per-pixel confidence mask to identify uncertain regions for targeted improvement. Experiments across multiple datasets show that FreeFix improves multiframe consistency and achieves performance comparable to or surpassing fine-tuning-based methods, while retaining strong generalization ability. Our project page is at https://xdimlab.github.io/freefix.

title: Dynamic Modeling and Transmission Characteristic Analysis of Integrated Linear Joints
authors: Shengbei Wang, Yan Huo, Huijun Wang, Ning Dong, Shangjun Ma
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: To analyze the coupled behavior of integrated linear joints in precision robotics, this study develops a multibody dynamics framework to perform dynamic modeling and transmission characteristic analysis. The proposed model integrates the inverted planetary roller screw (IPRS) with a frameless motor, taking into account the centripetal excitations caused by rotor eccentricity and utilizing a continuous friction model. A set of global dynamic equations is established to comprehensively describe the radial, axial, and torsional interactions within the system. The results characterize the non-uniformity in internal load distribution and the coupling effects between radial and axial motions. Furthermore, a comprehensive analysis across time-domain, frequency-domain, and phase-space indicates that the joint maintains stability with quasi-periodic motion under complex excitation. This work establishes the fundamental connections between internal mechanical excitations and motion accuracy, providing a theoretical basis for refining the structure and precision control of integrated robotic actuators.

title: Freshness Assessment of Fruits and Vegetables Using Dual Attention Vision Transformer
authors: Rupa Rani Paul, Rajkumar Biswas, Chinmoy Modak Turjo, Tonmoy Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Accurate assessment of produce freshness plays a critical role in maintaining food safety standards, ensuring quality assurance, and minimizing post-harvest economic losses. This research presents an automated classification framework utilizing a Dual Attention Vision Transformer (DaViT) architecture to tell the difference for identifying whether fruits and vegetables are fresh or decayed. The methodology leverages a comprehensive dataset comprising 28,189 images across 14 distinct fruit and vegetable categories, with each category containing of fresh and rotten classes, making it a total of 28 classes. The dataset distribution follows a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7 1 \% - 9 \% - 2 0 \%$</tex> divided for training, testing, and validation phases, respectively. The DaViT model undergoes fine-tuning procedures using pre-trained ImageNet weights as initialization parameters. Performance evaluation demonstrates exceptional classification capabilities with 99.04% accuracy, alongside 99% recall and 99% precision metrics. These results surpass the present advanced techniques methodologies in the domain. The proposed automated system reduces reliance on manual inspection processes while minimizing human-induced errors, offering a robust and scalable solution for integration into modern agricultural monitoring systems and intelligent food supply chain management frameworks.

title: Dependent Task Offloading Based on OPQ-SubTransformer and Genetic Optimization for Vehicular Edge Computing
authors: Ronghui Cao, Hanyun Xiao, Tan Deng, Xingyu Du, Ying Li, Xiaoyong Tang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The development of intelligent driving and vehicle-road collaboration has increased the computational pressure on in-vehicle systems, and VEC is considered an important solution to address this issue. However, current research on edge task offloading often treats tasks as a whole without performing fine-grained optimization. A few studies decompose tasks into dependent subtasks for offloading, but the complex prioritization and dependency relationships make task scheduling and management more complicated. Meanwhile, the trade-off between energy, delay, and completion rate further complicates the optimization objectives. To address these issues, this paper constructs a joint optimization framework based on fine-grained task partitioning, utilizing a hierarchical optimization strategy to globally minimize cost. Based on this framework, the paper designs a task offloading strategy generated by a SubTransformer neural network with an Order-Preserving Quantization (OPQ) mechanism, which fully considers task priority and subtask dependency relationships. Additionally, an improved genetic algorithm (GA) is integrated to achieve adaptive allocation of compute resource blocks (CRBs) on edge servers, thereby explicitly handling task priority constraints and resource competition during scheduling. This two-tier mechanism holistically addresses constraints like task priority and subtask dependencies, achieving collaborative multi-objective optimization. Experimental results show that, compared to baseline methods, the proposed solution reduces cost by 11.75%, delay by 11.38%, and energy by 11.06%.

title: Image Segmentation With Large Language Models: A Survey With Perspectives for Intelligent Transportation Systems
authors: Sanjeda Akter, Ibne Farabi Shihab, Anuj Sharma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) with computer vision is profoundly transforming perception tasks like image segmentation. For intelligent transportation systems (ITS), where accurate scene understanding is critical for safety and efficiency, this new paradigm offers unprecedented capabilities. This survey systematically reviews the emerging field of LLM-augmented image segmentation, focusing on its applications, challenges, and future directions within ITS. We provide a taxonomy of current approaches based on their prompting mechanisms and core architectures, and we highlight how these innovations can enhance road scene understanding for autonomous driving, traffic monitoring, and infrastructure maintenance. Finally, we identify key challenges, including real-time performance and safety-critical reliability, and outline a perspective centered on explainable, human-centric AI as a prerequisite for the successful deployment of this technology in next-generation transportation systems.

title: Hybrid CNN Framework Leveraging VGG16 for Multi-Class Brain Tumor Classification
authors: MD. Emtiaz Ahmed, Md Sifat Ullah Sayem, Amjad Hossain Piash, Afroza Akter
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Brain tumors are a critical health issue with a significant impact worldwide. Their detection and classification are crucial for effective treatment. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) serves as a reliable diagnostic tool for visualizing brain anomalies. To enhance diagnostic efficiency and reduce human error, automated computer-assisted approaches are essential. In this research, we introduce a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model that combines features from a pre-trained VGG16 model and a custom CNN architecture to classify brain tumors using MRI images. The custom CNN is composed of several convolutional layers with increasing filter sizes. Two publicly available datasets were utilized: one from Kaggle, comprising 6,217 images across four classes, and another from Figshare, containing 3,064 images across three classes. Once the data was preprocessed and divided into training and testing sets, our proposed model was trained using a multi-input fusion approach. We obtained an accuracy, precision, recall, and f1-score of 95.12%, 93%, 94%, and 93%, respectively, from our proposed model using the Kaggle dataset and 95.31%, 95%, 97%, and 96% by using Figshare dataset. These results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed model for reliable brain tumor diagnosis using MRI images.

title: An Efficient Approach with Theoretical Guarantees to Simultaneously Reconstruct Activity and Attenuation Sinogram for TOF-PET
authors: Liyang Hu, Chong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: In positron emission tomography (PET), it is indispensable to perform attenuation correction in order to obtain quantitatively accurate activity (tracer distribution) in the body. Generally, this is carried out based on the regulated or synthetic attenuation map obtained by computed tomography or magnetic resonance imaging respectively. However, except for errors in the attenuation correction factors obtained, the additional scan not only brings in more radiation dose and/or increases the scanning time but also leads to severe misalignments induced by the body motion during and between the two sequential scans. To address these issues, based on Maximum Likelihood estimation, we propose a new mathematical model for simultaneously reconstructing the Activity and Attenuation Sinogram, referred to as MLAAS, from time-of-flight (TOF)-PET emission data only. Particularly, in the proposed model, we make full use of the exclusively exponential form of the attenuation correction factors, and consider the constraint on a total amount of the activity in some mask region. Furthermore, we analyze the well-posedness of the model, including the existence, uniqueness and stability of its solution. We propose an alternating update algorithm to solve the model, and also analyze its convergence. Finally, numerical experiments with various emission data demonstrate that the proposed method MLAAS is of numerical convergence and robust to noise, and outperforms some state-of-the-art methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency, and enables TOF-PET to have the capability of autonomous attenuation correction.

title: A Novel SVPWM Strategy for Asymmetric Cascaded H-Bridge Inverters Considering Power Balancing and Current Backflow Elimination
authors: Xiaogang Wang, Ziyan Zhou, Ru Yang, Yingyi Liang, Xiaobo Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The Asymmetric Cascaded H-bridge (ACHB) inverter under SVPWM faces three key issues: high modulation complexity with poor scalability, power imbalance, and current backflow. Power imbalance results in uneven thermal stress, increased junction temperature, reduced device lifespan, and degraded reliability. Current backflow causes dc-link voltage fluctuations and extra power losses. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel SVPWM strategy. The strategy combines a hybrid high-low frequency modulation architecture with the staircase modulation principle and presents a novel voltage vector selection method, effectively reducing the computational complexity of the modulation algorithm and improving its scalability to higher-level topologies. In addition, based on the SVPWM strategy, a space vector power balancing control strategy is proposed. By investigating the intrinsic relationship between output power and space vectors, the strategy dynamically switches output modes, accurately monitors and proportionally distributes power among cells within the same phase, thereby enhancing the active power output capability of the inverter and system reliability. Furthermore, a strategy is designed to eliminate current backflow, so as to avoid unnecessary power losses while maintaining the quality of the output waveform. Both simulations and hardware experimental tests demonstrate the effectiveness and superior performance of the proposed SVPWM strategy compared to conventional modulation schemes.

title: Additively Manufactured X-Band Diplexer using Laser Beam Powder Bed Fusion Technique
authors: Aleksandr Voronov, Vicente Boria, Lluc Sempere, Mirko Kunowsky, Carmen Bachiller
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This work presents the additive manufacturing of an X-Band diplexer composed of two filters: one centered at 9.5 GHz and another at 10.5 GHz, both with a bandwidth of 250 MHz, plus a power divider. The whole set was implemented in rectangular waveguide technology, manufactured in a single block. Good agreement between simulated and measured results is achieved, with return losses (RL) lower than 20 dB, insertion losses (IL) around 0.3 dB in both filters, and isolation of 30 dB between them. For demonstration purposes, the set was used for the characterization in anechoic chamber of the directivity, gain and radiation efficiency of an additive manufactured horn antenna. The diplexer was implemented using Powder Bed Fusion Laser Beam, (PBF-L/M) technology on a metal alloy (AlSi10Mg). The horn antenna was manufactured via Vat Photopolymerization by exposure to Masked UV light (VPP-UVM) technology, using a polymeric resin (Gray from Applylabwork), subsequently metallized. Mechanical optimization for the additive manufacturing of the design allows the development of an ergonomic single-part device, enabling perfectly squared inner cavities. This reduces weight, volume, and manufacturing waste, while maintaining the electromagnetic response of the diplexer.

title: ISAC Channel Measurements and Closed-Loop Modeling with Bidirectional Parameter Coupling in Industrial IoT Scenarios
authors: Zengyao Bi, Xi Liao, Li Wang, Xiangquan Zheng, Yang Wang, Jie Zhang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: With the advent of 6G networks, integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key enabling technology for native perception in complex industrial Internet of Things (IoT) environments, which demands accurate ISAC channel models. However, most existing models mainly capture correlation at the cluster level, making it difficult to characterize finer-grained parameter relationships and intra-cluster variations, and lack an effective mechanism for bidirectional parameter coupling between communication and sensing channels in industrial IoT scenarios. This paper proposes a shared-cluster geometry-based stochastic ISAC (SC-GBSM-ISAC) channel model for industrial IoT. The model is parameterized using channel measurements at 38 GHz and 132 GHz in a high-clutter indoor factory (InF (A)) scenario. Compared with existing ISAC channel models, the proposed model has the following features. First, a closed-loop “sensing–communication–sensing” channel modeling framework is constructed based on 3GPP TR 38.901, which integrates bidirectional parameter interaction between communication and sensing channels. Second, by selectively reusing sensing-target clusters to construct shared clusters for communication-channel modeling, the model introduces a physically consistent shared-scattering structure while preserving the statistical characteristics specified in 3GPP TR 38.901. Third, by combining scatterer information inferred from the communication channel with a scenario-dependent sensing probability model, the sensing background component is reconstructed. Finally, measurement-simulation comparisons in an independent low-clutter InF (B) scenario demonstrate that the proposed model effectively reproduces bidirectional parameter coupling and primary spatio-temporal statistical metrics at 38/132 GHz, validating its accuracy and cross-scenario consistency in the measured scenarios.

title: AI-Driven Agricultural Portal for Optimized Crop Management
authors: Ms.A.P. Narmadha, Pindi Jaya Ramireddy, A V Pranay Kumar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Modern agriculture faces challenges in maximizing productivity and ensuring sustainable practices, which necessitate the integration of advanced technologies for real-time decision support. Climate uncertainties, pests, soil erosion and fluctuating markets are increasingly having impacts on agricultural production and require informed decisions by farmers. This paper proposes a solution to all these challenges by introducing a unified platform that integrates machine learning-based analytics, secure data management, and digital trading services. The suggested system is based on supervised machine learning models, which help in crop selection, yield forecast, rainfall forecast, and fertilizer prescription using historical yield data, soil parameters, and weather data. Moreover, pest and disease identification by the use of images helps farmers to detect and intervene on time. To guarantee the privacy and integrity of the data, the platform will store and transmit data via Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) and the integrity of the data will be ensured with the help of Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA-256), which prevents any manipulations with data by any third party. A trading module based on blockchains enables transparent and direct farmer-to-consumer processes, eliminating the need for intermediaries to ensure better profitability. The experimental analysis proves that the suggested system has about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 - 1 8 \%}$</tex> higher predictive accuracy and operational efficiency than the traditional methods. The findings illustrate that incorporating intelligent prediction models into secure data infrastructure is feasible in supporting sustainable and data-driven agricultural decision-making.

title: Redefining AI Architecture with NAND Flash
authors: Hao-Zhi Lee, Ming-Hui Wang, Yu-Hao Wang, Chia-Ming Hsu, Yi-Juan Du, An-Cin Li, Tso-Yuan Chen, Kai-Wei Tsou, Shiu-Ming Tsao, Jian-Ping Syu, Wei Lin
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: As Agentic AI accelerates data demands, Phison aiDAPTIV utilizes high-endurance NAND Flash as a memory expansion layer to optimize training, inference, and RAG applications. To overcome PCIe bandwidth bottlenecks, we introduce Computational Storage Devices (CSD), which offload tasks like weight updates and vector searches directly to the storage level. These cases demonstrate that CSD is a pivotal evolution for scaling efficient, AI-centric infrastructure.

title: SAATH:Smart Alert and Tracking Hardware
authors: Savitha MM, Supriya G, Varun Kumar G, Varun Kumar K A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This project presents a Smart ID Card system designed to enhance personal safety and asset tracking using RFID, GPS, and ESP32 microcontrollers. The Smart ID Card includes an RFID tag for entry and exit logging, and a GPS module for continuous location tracking. One ESP32 controller within the card collects and transmits data, while another serves as a gateway to send information securely to the cloud via Wi-Fi. The system uses the Telegram messaging platform to deliver real-time alerts when an individual moves outside predefined safe zones or interacts with an RFID reader. This enables instant communication with guardians or security personnel. The proposed solution demonstrates how low-cost hardware and widely used communication tools can be combined to create a reliable, efficient, and user-friendly safety monitoring system.

title: ShadowGPT: Linguistic Fingerprinting for Robust Detection of AI-Generated Phishing Emails
authors: Yousef Abu Dayeh
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: This paper presents ShadowGPT, a novel machine learning system for detecting ChatGPT-generated phishing emails through linguistic fingerprinting. As ChatGPT-3.5 become increasingly accessible, malicious actors exploit them to create sophisticated phishing campaigns that evade traditional detection methods. Our approach extracts 4 4 distinctive linguistic features encompassing stylometric patterns, readability metrics, vocabulary diversity, syntactic structures, and semantic indicators. We developed a robust gradient boosting classifier trained on 6,749 emails comprising 831 ChatGPT-generated phishing samples, 1,660 human-written phishing emails, and 4,258 legitimate communications. The training process incorporated noise augmentation to ensure real-world resilience. ShadowGPT achieves 99.6% accuracy with a 95% confidence interval of 99.2% to 99.8% in binary classification while maintaining an exceptionally low 0.14% false positive rate. The system demonstrates remarkable robustness, maintaining 99% accuracy under 20% feature perturbation. Our analysis reveals that ChatGPT-generated phishing exhibits consistent patterns including moderate urgency scores (0.98 ± 0.73), elevated trust language (0.70 ± 0.81), complex readability (Flesch: 23.9 ± 26.1), and low vocabulary diversity (0.684). These findings demonstrate that ChatGPT-3.5-generated phishing possesses detectable linguistic fingerprints, enabling effective defense against this emerging threat.

title: Superior Performance of Online Learning in Breast Cancer Classification: An AI-Driven Approach for Diagnosis and Prognosis
authors: Umme Haani A Haliyal, R. Kokila
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Breast cancer is a tumor that arises in the breast that is commonly overlooked and has resulted in the death of thousands of women. Artificial intelligence has simplified the process of diagnosis and prognosis, which is further categorized into machine learning. This advancement has made the detection process quicker than traditional methods, which can potentially increase the risk of fatalities. Approximately 15 algorithms were trained and evaluated using the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset (WBCD) to assess their accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score. The dataset includes 569 instances and 30 features, such as the radius, texture, and concavity. It also has a split of 62.7 % benign and 37.3% of malignant cases. The Stratified 10-Fold Cross-Validation were employed to ensure reliable performance estimation and quality assurance. Logistic Regression achieved the highest mean cross-validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.54 \% \pm 1.95$</tex>, while the Passive Aggressive Classifier achieved a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.67 \% \pm 2.41$</tex> and consistent high precision, recall and F1score. While batch learning models are performed well on static datasets, the Passive Aggressive Classifier has the advantage of online learning capability and low computational cost making it a better option for real time clinical decision support systems.

title: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Cloud Resource Forecasting: LSTM vs. SVR, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Prophet
authors: Lusani Mamushiane, Mduduzi C. Hlophe, Hlabishi Kobo, Albert A. Lysko, Joyce Mwangama
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Effective cloud resource forecasting is essential for optimizing virtual machine (VM) provisioning in increasingly dynamic and resource-constrained environments, especially now with the advent of 5G/6G networks. This paper evaluates a set of machine learning and deep learning models (LSTM, SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Prophet) for VM workload prediction using real-world data. The analysis emphasizes the trade-off between forecasting accuracy and computational complexity, two critical aspects for real-time and scalable deployment. Results show that while LSTM achieves superior accuracy, it incurs significant training overhead. Random Forest provides a more balanced trade-off, maintaining competitive accuracy with lower computational cost. These findings inform model selection in contexts like 5G/6G base station association, network slicing orchestration, multi-access edge computing, intelligent handover decisions, and dynamic service function chaining, where both efficiency and precision are vital for maintaining service-level guarantees under constrained resources.

title: Enabling Logic-in-Memory (LiM) Functionality with Junctionless FeFETs
authors: Neeraj Samant, Aarya Ameet Agavekar, Dhruv Ganesh, Rohit Kumar Nirala, Manish Gupta, Abhinav Kranti
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Implementing logic (NOR/NAND) by utilizing the polarization of HZO ferroelectric (Fe) layer in field effect transistors (FETs) opens new possibilities for realizing logic operations within memory. Despite the relatively simpler fabrication process, junctionless (JL) Fe FETs exhibit a degraded State 1 and are unsuitable for implementing logic-in-memory (LiM). The drawback is addressed through a co-optimization of channel doping (10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> instead of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup>) and gate workfunction (midgap 4.7 eV rather than 5 eV) which enables NOR/NAND operations in JL FeFETs and their adaptation in LiM modules.

title: Design of a 3D Braiding Online Tension Monitoring Spindle Based on Multi-Sensor Fusion
authors: Haowen Jiao, Keze Xu, Yujing Zhang, Zhuo Meng
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In view of the problems of insufficient measurement accuracy, large system size, and delayed data acquisition in traditional yarn tension monitoring methods during 3D braiding, a tension monitoring system based on multi-sensor fusion technology and combined with the original slider-type spindle is designed. An adaptive tension fusion algorithm based on vibration perception is also proposed. Based on the stm32 main processor and BLE wireless transmission, various sensors such as laser ranging sensors, micro pressure sensors, and inertial modules are deployed to achieve online acquisition of spindle yarn tension data during 3D braiding. The Sigmoid function is used to continuously reconstruct the dynamic model of spindle pulley displacement-yarn tension mapping, eliminating the singularity of the observation matrix. Considering the inertial impact on the micro pressure sensor during spindle trajectory changes, an adaptive weight function is utilized to dynamically adjust the noise covariance in the AKF algorithm. The system is applied to an inner-loop 3D braiding machine, and tension is selected as the observed quantity for offline and online experiments. The results show that this tension monitoring system can accurately and real-time output yarn tension changes, achieving higher measurement accuracy and better robustness compared to single-sensor measurement systems and other measurement methods.

title: A Real-Time Heuristic 3D Bin Packing Algorithm for Robotic Mixed Palletizing with Kinematic and Stability Constraints
authors: Yongyong Sun, Kezheng Sun, Wu Yan, Yisong Cai, Zhaoyang Liao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Mixed palletizing of multi-sized boxes is a critical operation in modern flexible manufacturing and smart logistics. However, traditional 3D Bin Packing Problem (3D-BPP) algorithms often fail in physical deployment because they prioritize theoretical volume utilization while neglecting the mechanical and kinematic realities of robotic execution. This paper proposes a real-time heuristic 3D-BPP algorithm specifically tailored for industrial robotic palletizing systems. By integrating a Guillotine Split space management strategy with strict physical guardrails-specifically, a minimum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 {\%}}$</tex> bottom support area constraint to prevent collapse, and a top-down kinematic clearance check to avoid end-effector collisions-the proposed algorithm effectively eliminates dangerous trajectories during the packing sequence. Furthermore, the algorithm is encapsulated within a highly extensible software framework, enabling millisecond-level inference on standard industrial PCs without requiring heavy GPU acceleration. Real-world implementation demonstrates that while the theoretical space utilization is marginally compromised, the algorithm achieves a near-perfect 99% industrial executability rate, successfully bridging the “Sim-to-Real” gap in robotic automation.

title: A Table of Contents-Based Novel Architecture for Compute-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Harshit Kondle, Ruhaan Singh, Sai Satheshkumar, Saketh Nandam, Yuvaan Chandra, Anvit Koppella, John McKee, Mihai Boicu, Kamaljeet Sanghera
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems often embed entire corpora upfront, which is computationally expensive in constrained environments such as military deployments. We introduce a novel architecture, TOC-Based Load-Time Section Filtering (TLSF), which parses structured documents like DAFMAN 36-2664 using a hierarchical TOC and routes queries to relevant sections before embedding. Our pipeline comprises three modules: TOC parsing, probabilistic keyword-based TOC indexing, and lazy embedding. We evaluate TLSF across latency, compute cost, retrieval precision, and memory usage. Our results demonstrate 3.15x latency reduction and significant compute savings over brute-force RAG, while increasing retrieval precision by 19%. This approach is promising for low-resource natural language processing applications in policy-intensive domains.

title: LUCID: An Explainable Multi-Stage Framework for Lung CT Image Analysis and Clinical Outcome Interpretation
authors: Rachana.S, Kokila Paramanandam
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be one of the most common causes of death globally, where timely detection and individualized treatment are key to better outcomes. The majority of the current medical image analysis platforms offer black-box predictions or correlations only. To address this, we introduce a multistage analytical pipeline that integrates lung CT segmentation, explainable AI (XAI), clinical data causal inference, and a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) chatbot to provide explainable and patient-specific explanations. We utilize two complementary datasets: the NSCLC Radiogenomics Archive (TCIA) for patient-specific clinical and survival data to facilitate causal modeling, and the Medical Segmentation Decathlon (Task 6) for expert-annotated lung CT scans to facilitate robust segmentation and interpretability. Our SE-Attention UNet segmentation pipeline achieved a Precision of 0.9144, a recall of 0.8647, an F1-score and Dice coefficient of 0.8888, and an IoU of 0.7999.Grad-CAM++ and attention maps improve interpretability by identifying clinically relevant tumor areas. Contrary to standard reports that only summarizes associations, causal inference gives actionable cause–effect knowledge e.g - smoking status highly increases the risk of mortality and recurrence, and radiation therapy and adjuvant therapy have protective roles. The relationships differ between subgroups, requiring the need for individualized care. Lastly, a domain-expert RAG chatbot combines segmented image outputs, causal results, and filtered medical knowledge to provide context-aware explanations for clinicians and patients. It integrates the outputs of imaging, causal reasoning, and interactive knowledge retrieval, this model extends existing models to provide a new, interpretable, and clinically meaningful basis for lung cancer diagnostic analysis

title: Structure-Aware Mamba Sequence Modeling for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Jian Xu, Jiaqi Liu, Xiaoli Meng, Jingang Shi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: This paper introduces a structure-aware Mamba sequence modeling approach for image super-resolution (SR). While Mamba has shown success in NLP with linear complexity and long-sequence modeling, its application to visual tasks faces challenges due to the two-dimensional structural constraints of images. To address this, we propose a lightweight structure-aware prior module that quantifies regional importance using gradient and local variance cues. Based on this prior, we design a dualbranch Mamba block (DCMB) that jointly models local structural details and global contextual dependencies, fused via an adaptive gating mechanism. Furthermore, an adaptive scanning path strategy is introduced to improve structural continuity and reduce computational redundancy. Experiments on Urban100 demonstrate that our method achieves a 0.34 dB PSNR gain with only 0.84 M parameters and improves inference speed by approximately 32%, validating the effectiveness of structureaware sequence modeling for efficient visual reconstruction.

title: Aero-Engine Remaining Useful Life Prediction Based on CNN-BiLSTM Bidirectional Temporal Modeling
authors: Jingzhi Luo, Lu Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Accurate prediction of the remaining useful life (RUL) of aero-engines is crucial for ensuring flight safety and enabling predictive maintenance. To address the coexistence of local degradation features and long-term temporal dependencies in sensor monitoring data, this paper proposes a hybrid prediction model that integrates a convolutional neural network with a bidirectional long short-term memory network (CNN-BiLSTM). The model extracts local spatial patterns from sensor sequences through the CNN layer and captures bidirectional long-term dependencies during the equipment performance degradation process via the BiLSTM layer, thereby achieving comprehensive modeling of degradation dynamics. Experimental results on the NASA C-MAPSS benchmark dataset demonstrate that the hybrid deep learning model achieves excellent performance on the FD001 subset, with a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$({R}^{{2}})$</tex> of 0.8641 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 10.27, significantly outperforming other models used for comparison. This work provides an effective technical approach for the intelligent health management of aeroengines.

title: A Global Vision-Based Mobile Obstacle Perception and Global Path Optimization Method for Unmanned Vehicles
authors: Shuang Yu, Liangxi Xie, Ming Xiao, Zihao Guo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In dynamic environments, unmanned vehicles are required to achieve safe and efficient autonomous navigation in the presence of dynamic obstacles such as workers and transport vehicles. This paper proposes a risk-aware global path planning algorithm based on the historical trajectories of dynamic obstacles under a global perspective. By collecting multiple historical motion trajectories of dynamic obstacles, the algorithm infers the probability of their possible motion routes according to the current states of dynamic obstacles, which is then applied to the global path planning of unmanned vehicles. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can generate effective dynamic obstacle avoidance trajectories and reduce potential collision risks. Compared with traditional methods, the method presented in this paper significantly improve safety of the planned paths.

title: Spatiotemporal Drought Analysis Using Hybrid Evolutionary Algorithm-Optimized Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Vandana Sardar, Surabhi B P, Varshitha D, Harshitha B M, Likitha J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The agricultural community in the semi-arid region of Bagalkot faces significant economic hazards due to the unpredictable occurrence of drought. The current system of monitoring is inefficient due to the use of global thresholds, which are inappropriate for classifying vegetation stress in the region. This paper proposes a region-specific decision support system based on a hybrid deep learning model. The core model combines a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) to identify short-term meteorological variations with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units to identify seasonal variations. Instead of using trial-and-error methods for hyperparameter selection, the paper proposes a hybrid optimization technique that combines Whale Optimization and Grey Wolf Optimization (WOA-GWO). The model is tested on new data and achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 90.74% and an RMSE of 0.0296. Moreover, the severity levels were revised to show that a Vegetation Health Index (VHI) value below 36.10 represents severe drought in Bagalkot, while the global value is 20. This revised and optimized tool provides a localized approach for accurate agricultural risk assessment.
